sources.summary: The CHANG-ES paper highlights NGC 660 as a polar-ring galaxy whose strong central source caused high dynamic range, elevated artifacts, possible flat spectral-index regions, and very weak polarization.

quotes.quote: The data of polar ring galaxy NGC 660 have the highest dynamic range in both bands, due to a strong central source. Consequently, the resulting images have higher than expected rms values, particularly in L-band. Nevertheless, the achieved signal to noise of the C-band map with robust weighting reached 70000, on the higher end of what best can be expected (see Section sec:stokesIimaging ). In spite of careful cleaning, including peeling Only the Stokes I and spectral index images were produced from the peeled data, while polarization images were not, since the off-centre confusing sources are not as problematic in Q and U as for total I. of the nearest of the two strongly interfering field sources in L-band (see Section sec:peeling ), artifacts are still present.

This could potentially be affecting the spectral index results, where regions of flat spectral index are crossing the disk. However, these regions could also be an effect of the possible AGN (see sec:AGNcontamination ). We note that the polarization intensity is low, with its fraction of polarization over Stokes I intensity just a 10th of the 0.5 which is considered a believable signal (see Section sec:polarizationimaging ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 660 as an inclined, gas-rich polar-ring galaxy whose two key properties cannot be reproduced by merger simulations and instead match tidal accretion.

quotes.quote: NGC 660 The main characteristic of NGC 660 is that the ring extends to about 45 degrees from perpendicular (Whitmore et al. 1990, Combes et al. 1992). This polar ring is thus likely to be unstable, even if self-gravity may stabilize it if massive enough (Arnaboldi Sparke 1994). The second noticeable feature is the presence of gas in the host galaxy: the host gas content in typical of a late-type spiral: Van Driel et al. (1995) have found M_ HI /L_ B ^0=0.3 M_ /L_ , B in the host disk, and detected a large mass of molecular gas in the disk.

In a tidal accretion event, the problem is solved: polar gas is directly placed around the ring radius R , so that it may never encounter the gas of the host galaxy, provided that the ring radius R is larger than the host gas component radius, which is the case in NGC 660. Constraints on the gas content of the host galaxy are weaker in the accretion scenario than in the merging scenario, and the gas-rich host galaxy of NGC 660 may result only from a tidal accretion event. Finally, both characteristics of NGC 660 (inclined ring, gas-rich host galaxy) cannot be reproduced by our simulations of merging events. On the contrary, they are in agreement with the results of the accretion scenario.

sources.summary: The paper argues that NGC 660, a polar-ring LINER galaxy, developed a compact radio outburst with jet-like structure, absorption signatures, and properties consistent with newly awakened AGN activity.

quotes.quote: The core of the nearby galaxy NGC 660 has recently undergone a spectacular radio outburst; using a combination of archival radio and Chandra X-ray data, together with new observations, the nature of this event is investigated. Radio observations made using e-MERLIN in mid-2013 show a new compact and extremely bright continuum source at the centre of the galaxy. High angular resolution observations carried out with the European VLBI Network show an obvious jet-like feature to the north east and evidence of a weak extension to the west, possibly a counter-jet.

The nearby galaxy NGC 660 ( 13 Mpc; 2009ApJS..182..474S ) exhibits a polar ring morphology and a LINER-type nucleus (low-ionisation nuclear emission-line region; 2002A&A...392...53N ). used observations of CO emission to infer a total H _ 2 mass in the main disk (radius 4 kpc) of 3.7 10^ 9 M _ , and in the polar ring (radius 12 kpc) of 10 ^ 9 M _ . They construct a model to account for both the CO and H i properties of the system, concluding that the ring is highly inclined and highly warped.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 660 star-forming regions, finding low infrared fluxes linked to ring surface brightness, while some disk regions have unusually high 8 micron surface brightness.

quotes.quote: The star-forming regions (SFRs) in the peculiar galaxies NGC 660, NGC 1512, NGC 4395 and NGC 4618 are studied. The relationships between such characteristics of star forming regions as UV, nearand far-IR fluxes, as well as in the H and HI lines, surface brightness in these ranges, and the scatter of the radial velocities of ionized and neutral hydrogen are considered. It is shown that in all the galaxies considered, the IR fluxes from SFRs are less than in the “normal” galaxies, but for different reasons: in the galaxies with signs of recent interaction NGC 660 and NGC 1512, this is due to the low surface brightness of SFRs; in the lopsided galaxies NGC 4395 and NGC 4618, the low brightness of SFRs in the infrared range is due to their compact size.

Figure F8_24_surfbri compares the surface brightness (in Jy/pc ^2 )) of the studied SFRs at wavelengths of 8 and 24 m separately for each galaxy studied in this work. The surface brightness of three SFRs of the NGC 660 galaxy disk at a wavelength of 8 m exceeds the surface brightness of all other considered SFRs, but this may be due to the uncertain isolation of SFRs in this subsystem. Two more SFRs of the NGC 660 disk do not differ in surface brightness from the SFRs in the comparison galgalaxy. In contrast, the SFRs of the NGC 660 ring have significantly lower surface brightness at wavelengths of 8 and 24 m, less than 10^ 3 Jy/pc ^2 , than the SFRs of the center and periphery of NGC628.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 660 as an example of polar gas kinematics, separating central-disk and external structures and noting warped H I and overlapping disk-ring regions.

quotes.quote: 001Moiseev_fig1 shows the velocity field of ionized gas in NGC 660 derived from the observation at the SAO RAS 6-m telescope with the scanning FPI in the SCORPIO focal reducer AfanasievMoiseev2005 . Using the tiled-rings approximation we were able to study separately the kinematics of the central disk and the external structure, which is inclined by the angle of about 50 to the main disk. The ring of ionized gas is flat ( PA,i const ), while a warp is observed in the H i for the larger radii 1995AJ....109..942V .
Now we are matching the ionized gas kinematics with the new WSRT H i data to estimate of the dark matter shape in NGC 660.
The analysis of NCG 660 velocity field demonstrates an overlapping of the central disk and the ring at the distances 60-- 75'' (4--5 kpc).

sources.summary: The paper reports GBT absorption detections in NGC 660, including the first 2_11-2_12 detection, and relates them to previous molecular-line measurements and source size.

quotes.quote: NGC 660: Our GBT detection of the H _2 CO 1_ 10 1_ 11 transition in absorption (Figure fig:NGC253NGC660Maffei2IC342FormSpec ) is similar in both intensity and velocity extent as that reported by Baan1986 , while our detection of the H _2 CO 2_ 11 2_ 12 transition is the first reported. Aalto1995 measured the ^ 12 CO J= 1-0 , 2-1 ; ^ 13 CO J= 1-0 , 2-1 ; C ^ 18 O J= 1-0 ; and HCN J= 1-0 emission, while Israel2009 measured the ^ 12 CO J= 1-0 , 2-1 , 3-2 , 4-3 and ^ 13 CO J= 1-0 , 2-1 , and 3-2 emission from this galaxy. From CO J= 1-0 Aalto1995 and 3-2 Israel2009 images these studies derived a source diameter of 15^ .

sources.summary: The paper reports H2CO 2-1 detection in NGC 660 with CO-like linewidth, contrasting earlier high-J non-detections and implying little warm dense gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 660 : The detected \ 2-1 in NGC 660 has a similar linewidth (FWHM 294.7 ) to CO 1-0 ( 280 ). While the \ survey by Lindberg2011 did not observe NGC 660 , its 10-9 and 12-11 transitions were not detected by Costagliola2011 . This difference in the detection of \ lines may imply that there is little warm and dense gas content in NGC 660 , thus the high- J \ lines can not be excited.

gmw_00001010.name: And VII
gmw_00001010.overview: Andromeda VII is a dwarf spheroidal satellite galaxy of Andromeda [arXiv:1912.02186, arXiv:2101.09900] that may possess a prolate stellar density distribution [arXiv:1703.02604]. The galaxy experienced its main epoch of star formation 6.2 billion years ago, followed by slow star formation until it was likely quenched after infalling into Andromeda's virial radius [arXiv:2101.09900, arXiv:2101.10874].

sources.summary: The paper includes And VII among five M31 dwarf spheroidals observed with Keck/DEIMOS, measuring member-star kinematics, dynamical masses, rotation limits, metallicities, and abundance trends.

quotes.quote: We obtained spectroscopy with Keck/DEIMOS fab03 of individual red giants in five dSphs: Andromeda VII, I, III, V, and X . They are numbered in the order in which they were discovered: And I and III van72 , And V arm98 , And VII kar99 , and And X zuc07 . However, in this paper, we list them in decreasing order of stellar mass whenever they appear in a list (e.g., in tables and in figures).

We present deep spectroscopy from Keck/DEIMOS of Andromeda I, III, V, VII, and X, all of which are dwarf spheroidal satellites of M31. The sample includes \ spectroscopic members across all five dSphs. We confirm previous measurements of the velocity dispersions and dynamical masses, and we provide upper limits on bulk rotation. Our measurements confirm that M31 satellites obey the same relation between stellar mass and stellar metallicity as Milky Way (MW) satellites and other dwarf galaxies in the Local Group. The metallicity distributions show similar trends with stellar mass as MW satellites, including evidence in massive satellites for external influence, like pre-enrichment or gas accretion.

sources.summary: The paper reconstructs Andromeda VII's star-formation history from long-period variable stars, finding a dominant episode 6.2 Gyr ago, later weak star formation, substantial stellar mass, and links to M 31.

quotes.quote: Here, we perform the first reconstruction of the SFH of And VII employing long-period variable (LPV) stars. As the most-evolved asymptotic giant branch (AGB) and red supergiant (RSG) stars, the birth mass of LPVs can be determined by connecting their near-infrared photometry to theoretical evolutionary tracks. We found 55 LPV candidates within two half-light radii, using multi-epoch imaging with the Isaac Newton Telescope in the i and V bands. Based on their birth mass function, the star-formation rate (SFR) of And VII was obtained as a function of cosmic time.

The main epoch of star formation occurred 6.2 Gyr ago with a SFR of 0.006 0.002 M _ yr ^ 1 . Over the past 6 Gyr, we find slow star formation, which continued until 500 Myr ago with a SFR 0.0005 0.0002 M _ yr ^ 1 . We determined And VII's stellar mass M=(13.3 5.3) 10^6 M _ within a half-light radius r_ 1 2 =3.8 0.3 arcmin and metallicity Z=0.0007 , and also derived its distance modulus of =24.38 mag.

To explore the link between host galaxies and their dSph companions, we here study Andromeda VII, the most massive and extended dSph associated with M 31. And VII (also known as the Cassiopeia dwarf) was discovered by Karachentsev and Karachentseva in 1999A&A...352..363K as a satellite of M 31, along with two companions, And V and And VI.

sources.summary: The paper reports a new proper motion for Andromeda VII, noting archival HST complications but deriving a physically feasible bound speed at its M31-satellite location.

quotes.quote: Our final target, the M31 satellite And VII, lies at a distance of 231 kpc from M31 mcconnachie2012,savino+2022 . While the stellar population e.g., Navabi2021 and radial velocity Kalirai2010 of this galaxy have been studied using \ data, it does not have a measured PM. And VII offers an attempt to utilize archival data of overlapping fields to recover a novel result that would increase the number of M31 dwarf PMs measured using \ by 50 .

Andromeda VII Figure fig:and7summary shows a summary of our PM result for And VII. Sundry properties of these data -- namely, the obstacles concerning the \ observations of this field touched on in sec:obs - have the potential to impugn our results. This includes that the first epoch of data is in a different filter than the second (F555W versus F606W).

Our final PM values for And VII are therefore: _ ^* _ ^ * _ And VII = 0.0598 0.0368 0.0037 0.0335 mas yr^ 1 , which, though large, still represents a gravitationally bound and feasible speed ( 235 km/s) at the location of And VII.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Andromeda VII as a dwarf spheroidal whose fitted stellar-density distribution may be prolate, making its inferred dark-halo parameters potentially systematic.

quotes.quote: Here, we note that the observational results from Sextans, Andromeda II and VII dSphs are significantly affected by the sizes of data samples and their peculiar stellar kinematics.

For Andromeda VII, although there is no observational evidence due to lack of data sample, HC2015b suggested from the fitting results that the three dimensional stellar density distribution of this galaxy would be prefer to the prolate system. Thus this galaxy could also have a peculiar system as well as Andromeda II. Therefore, there is the possibility that the obtained parameters for these dark halos may contain large systematics.

sources.summary: And VII is selected as an M31 dSph for dark-halo modeling; the paper maps its parameters and infers a steep inner density slope.

quotes.quote: For the M31 dSphs, we select five dSphs (And I, And II, And III, And V, and And VII) such that more than 50 stars which can be used as kinematic data, because for a dSph with less than 50 sample stars it is difficult to evaluate its two-dimensional velocity dispersion maps correctly as described below.

In this section, we describe the best-fit dark halo model for each of seven MW dSphs (Carina, Fornax, Sculptor, Sextans, Draco, Leo I, and Leo II) and five M31 dSphs (And I, And II, And III, And V, and And VII) and present confidence maps between each parameter.

In Figure  fig:fig2 and  fig:fig3 , we present 68  ( 1 ), 95  , and 99  confidence levels of contours in the two-dimensional plane of Q- _z , _0- _z , _z , b_ halo _z , and i- _z for all sample dSphs: Carina, Fornax, Sculptor, Sextans, Draco, and Leo I are shown in Figure  fig:fig2 , and Leo II, And I, And II, And III, And V, and And VII are shown in Figure  fig:fig3 .

sources.summary: The paper summarizes And VII as an Andromeda dwarf spheroidal where LPV candidates and a star-formation-rate peak were measured, with quenching likely after infall.

quotes.quote: Here we present results of published papers for three galaxies: And I and And VII, two dwarf spheroidal and satellite of Andromeda, and IC 10, an irregular and isolated galaxy. (Navabi et al. Submitted, 2020) detected 43 LPV candidates within the half-light radii of And VII, and estimated the SFR peak of this area about 0.002 M_ yr^ 1 at Z=0.0007. Probably, And VII was quenched by environmental impacts after infall into Andromeda's virial radius ( 2020svos.conf..383N , mahdie-ipm ).

gmw_00001011.name: NGC 1596
gmw_00001011.overview: NGC 1596 is an edge-on lenticular galaxy [arXiv:2009.14731, arXiv:astro-ph/0605600] featuring a boxy bulge [arXiv:astro-ph/0512143] and a disk-like structure [arXiv:2206.01578]. It is physically paired and tidally interacting with the nearby dwarf irregular galaxy NGC 1602 [arXiv:astro-ph/0605600, arXiv:astro-ph/0512143], which has transferred neutral hydrogen to NGC 1596 to form counter-rotating ionized gas [arXiv:2009.14731, arXiv:astro-ph/0605600].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1596 as an S0 in a physical pair with NGC 1602, lacking H-alpha regions but showing extended emission and counter-rotating acquired gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 1596/ NGC 1602 These two Dorado members, nearby in projection, are separated by V_ hel =230 \ (Figure NGC1596 ). NGC 1596 is a S0 galaxy (Type=-2.0 0.5). No \ regions are visible.

The case of NGC 1596/NGC 1602 physical pair (Figure NGC1596 ) can be another example of \ gas transfer by a donor. The area in which the pair is found is very rich of neutral hydrogen Chung2006 which extends from NGC 1602, the donor, to NGC 1596, the receiver. In NGC 1596, the acquired gas counter-rotate with respect to stars. However, we do not detect \ regions in NGC 1596 but only an extended \ emission. Chung2006 reported that the ionized gas in NGC 1596 produces mostly [ OIII ] 5007 emission in the central part see also Bureau2006 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1596 as an edge-on S0 whose counter-rotating gas likely came from NGC 1602 during a long-running tidal interaction.

quotes.quote: Draft Version firstpage - lastpage 2002 We present Australia Telescope Compact Array (ATCA) \ imaging of the edge-on galaxy NGC 1596, which was recently found to have counter-rotating ionized gas in its center ( <15'' ). We find a large \ envelope associated with a nearby companion, the dwarf irregular galaxy NGC 1602. The \ covers a region 11 9 13 4 (62 70 kpc ^2 ) and the total \ mass detected is 2.5 0.1 10^9 M_ (assuming an 18 Mpc distance). The \ is centered on NGC 1602 but appears to have two tidal tails, one of which crosses over NGC 1596. The \ located at the position of NGC 1596 has a velocity gradient in the same sense as the ionized gas, i.e. opposite to the stellar rotation. Both the existence of a large gas reservoir and the velocity gradient of the \ and the ionized gas strongly suggest that the ionized gas in NGC 1596 originated from NGC 1602.

We presented the \ distribution and kinematics of the galaxy pair NGC 1596/NGC 1602 and its surrounding. \ is found in a broad region 11 9 13 4 covering NGC 1596 and its rich companion NGC 1602. This region corresponds to 62 70 kpc ^ 2 assuming an 18 Mpc distance. The total \ mass found is 2.5 0.1 10^9 M_ and about half of this is concentrated in and around NGC 1602. Both the \ morphology and kinematics suggest that NGC 1602 is the main gas reservoir. Two tidal tails are found, respectively at lower and higher velocities with respect to NGC 1602, which were most likely caused by an interaction with NGC 1596. The counter-rotating ionized gas found in NGC 1596 thus probably originated from \ gas transferred from NGC 1602 to NGC 1596. Besides the fact that there is a huge gas reservoir available in this region, this is also supported by the consistent velocity gradient of the \ and the counter-rotating ionized gas along the major-axis of NGC 1596.

sources.summary: The paper reports newly identified counter-rotating gas in NGC 1596, describes its boxy bulge and younger disk colors, and links its distorted envelope to interaction with NGC 1602.

quotes.quote: We have identified two new galaxies with gas counter-rotation (NGC 1596 and NGC 3203) and have confirmed similar behaviour in another one (NGC 128), this using results from separate studies of the ionized-gas and stellar kinematics of a well-defined sample of 30 edge-on disc galaxies.

NGC 1596 Little detailed information on NGC 1596 is available in the literature. As for NGC 128, quantify the global parameters describing the boxy shape of the bulge, while jfk95 present more general global results, also from optical surface photometry. A more detailed analysis by , who attempted but failed to fit a thin plus thick disc model, reveals an extended, roughly speroidal but somewhat distorted outer envelope, which they suggest might be related to an interaction with NGC 1602 (see below). obtained both optical and NIR photometry and showed that, like most of their S0 sample, the NIR-NIR colors of the bulge and disc of NGC 1596 are similar, indicating a similar metalicity, while the optical-NIR colors are significantly different, suggesting a younger disc (in the mean). The only spatially resolved kinematic observations available for NGC 1596 are the current ionized-gas kinematics and the stellar kinematics of , who argued that the B/PS bulge of NGC 1596 is due to an edge-on bar viewed nearly end-on.
NGC 1596 forms a pair and is most likely interacting with the H I rich galaxy NGC 1602 (IB(s)m pec; V 60 km s ^ 1 ) only 3.0 arcmin away and easily visible in Figure fig:env .

sources.summary: The paper presents the first UV view of NGC 1596, finding mixed old and FUV light, extra-planar smoothed FUV features, disk-like Sersic index, and interaction with NGC 1602.

quotes.quote: NGC 1596 and NGC 1602 Our UVIT image is the first UV view of both these systems. Our composite image (Figure NGC1596 ) shows that the FUV.CaF2 emission dominates the light of the irregular galaxy NGC 1602 whereas it is mixed to older stellar populations in NGC 1596. We enhanced the signal of faint structures using ASMOOTH task to look for a FUV connection between the two galaxies following the behaviour the \ emission Bureau2006,Elagali2019 without success. The smoothed image (bottom right panel), however, reveals extra-planar light, so the FUV disk looses the regular shape shown in the optical bands (bottom left panel).

The value of the S ersic index in Figure NGC1596 (top: central and left panels) n=2.12 0.18 (Table UVIT-results ) is consistent with the presence of a disk in NGC 1596. The average position angle and ellipticity, 0.5 and 20 ^ , are consistent with HyperLeda values (Table UVIT-sources ) and the I-band measure of the PA 20.0 ^ 0.8 ^ from CGS.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1596 as an S0 with a huge spheroidal outer envelope, possible companion interaction, and unusually upward-bending radial profiles at large radii.

quotes.quote: NGC 1596: ( n1596 ) A `normal' 0 enclosed in a huge, spheroidal, outer envelope possibly triggered by interaction with the nearby physical companion (NGC 1602). The difference from other 0s is obvious from radial cuts. It is the only galaxy in which the profiles bend upwards (not caused by a background gradient) at larger galactocentric distances ( R 115 ). This looks similar to an R ^ 1/4 bulge component beginning to dominate the profile when extrapolating the curvature of the inner bulge towards the outer extension. However, the overall flatness of such an R ^ 1/4 bulge would be unusual.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1596 as having an upbending face-on-equivalent profile, a poorly fit broken exponential, and an unclear extended break region driven by deprojected cuts.

quotes.quote: NGC 1596: See for additional notes. The face-on-equivalent profile of NGC 1596 is classified as , a break with an upbending profile beyond. However, the profile is not well described by a simple broken exponential. The whole disk profile (beyond R 25 ) appears to be almost continuously upbending. One may argue for an extended, exponential break region between breaks at R 70 \ and R 130 . Therefore, the exact position of the break is unclear.

gmw_00001012.name: NGC 3203
gmw_00001012.overview: NGC 3203 is an early-type barred galaxy that hosts counter-rotating ionized gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0512143, arXiv:1202.4790]. This unsettled gas is likely the result of external accretion, supported by the presence of a nearby neutral hydrogen blob and a physically connected dwarf companion [arXiv:1202.4790].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3203 as a new gas counter-rotator, noting evidence for a thick disk, no vertical color gradient, a barred structure, and unsettled ionized gas.

quotes.quote: We have identified two new galaxies with gas counter-rotation (NGC 1596 and NGC 3203) and have confirmed similar behaviour in another one (NGC 128), this using results from separate studies of the ionized-gas and stellar kinematics of a well-defined sample of 30 edge-on disc galaxies.
Suffice it to say that while the ionized-gas appears fairly regular in NGC 128 and NGC 1596, it is rather unsettled in NGC 3203.

NGC 3203 No in-depth study of NGC 3203 exists in the literature, although present broadband optical imaging and argue for an increasing disc scaleheight with (projected) radius, which they attribute to the gradual dominance of a thick disc over the thin disc. however show that NGC 3203 has no discernable vertical colour gradient. gpk97 's ( gpk97 ) K^ band data also suggest a change in the steepness of the vertical light distribution near the centre, contrary to most galaxies in their sample. Because of the particular shape of the K^ light profiles along cuts parallel to but offset from the major-axis, which display a characteristic plateau and secondary maxima, argue that NGC 3203 is barred.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3203 as an early-type barred counter-rotator whose nearby H I blob and dwarf companion make external gas accretion likely.

quotes.quote: Draft Version firstpage - lastpage 2002 We probe the H i properties and the gas environments of three early-type barred galaxies harbouring counter-rotating ionized gas, NGC 128, NGC 3203 and NGC 7332.
In NGC 128 and NGC 3203, we find 9.6 10^7 and 2.3 10^8 M_ of H i , respectively, covering almost the entire stellar bodies of dwarf companions that appear physically connected.
NGC 3203 We find an H i blob toward the southwest edge of NGC 3203's optical disc.

gmw_00001013.name: 3C 281
gmw_00001013.overview: The radio-loud quasar 3C 281 resides within a very rich galaxy cluster [arXiv:0907.2405]. It exhibits optical microvariability, with detected brightness variations and a thermal component modeled at approximately 24,000 K [arXiv:0907.2405, arXiv:1004.1199].

sources.summary: The paper reports optical microvariability in 3C 281 on two March 2000 nights, including B- and V-band changes on March 1 and a V-band event on March 4.

quotes.quote: 3C 281.- This radio loud quasar is known to reside in a very rich galaxy cluster Yee87 . A variation of 0.15 magnitudes in one year has been reported in the infrared Enya02a,Enya02b . We observed this object for Paper I on May of 1997, but microvariability was not detected. We observed this quasar with EOCA on 2000 March 1 and 4, detecting OM events in both nights.

2000 March 4.- 3C 281 was observed during 3.75 hours in three occasions. A visual revision of the light-curves establishes the occurrence of a possible variation in the three bands (Figure fig1b ). However, ANOVA only detected a variation in V and a marginal one in R (with a significance level of 5 ).

sources.summary: The paper reports two optical microvariability events in 3C 281 and finds that the March 1 event requires adding a thermal component beyond the initial power-law fit.

quotes.quote: 3C 281. Two OM events were detected in 2000 on March 1 and 4 (see PII). 2000 March 1.- The object brightness was m_V = 17.40 0.03 (a little lower than the brightness reported by Sandage65 : m_V = 17.02 ) at the begin of the monitoring. A single power law with _ t_0 = -0.03 0.01 fits the initial data (Figure fig81a ). However, variations of this component cannot explain the OM event (see Figure fig81c ). Then, we added in the model a thermal component with temperature T_ t_0 24,000 K (Figure fig81b ).

gmw_00001014.name: PKS 0003+15
gmw_00001014.overview: PKS 0003+15 is a large double steep-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:2212.07061] that exhibits historical long-term and intermediate-term optical variability [arXiv:0907.2405]. Its emission features extensive polarization across its core, lobes, and hotspots [arXiv:2212.07061], alongside an at least partially unpolarized small blue bump [arXiv:astro-ph/0408105].

sources.summary: The paper reviews earlier variability detections for PKS 0003+15 and reports a new V-band decrement during a 3.13-hour October 2001 monitoring sequence.

quotes.quote: PKS 0003+15.- Historically, this quasar has shown intense activity on long-term and intermediate-term variability Barbieri79,Pica88,Schramm94,Guibin95,Guibin98,Garcia99 . OM has also been detected. On October of 1994, Jang95 observed a variation in R with an amplitude of 0.11 mags in 2 hours, while Eggers00 reported changes of 0.005 mag/h in R . Garcia99 also reported microvariability for this quasar.

PKS 0003+15 was reported in Paper I with no evidence of microvariability. We have monitored this quasar during 3.13 hours in four occasions on October 11 2001. Figure fig1d shows that while brightness remained constant in B and R , a decrement in V was detected. ANOVA confirms this visual revision.

sources.summary: The paper treats Q0003+158 as having an at least partially unpolarized small blue bump, possible ISP line effects, and tentative Balmer-edge structure.

quotes.quote: Q0003+158 ISP correction ^1 This object (Fig. res-Q0003+158 ) also has an at least partially unpolarized small blue bump, since P decreases shortward of 4000 , but the broad lines look partially polarized. There is no clear evidence of PA rotations. Based on the polarization measurements of two Galactic stars around this object (0.06/0.41 for 140/280pc at 115/118 ; He00 ), there could be a small ISP at a PA very similar to that observed for Q0003+158. Therefore, at least partially, the polarized lines could be due to ISP.

Corrected Spectra Fig. res-Q0003+158-cor103 shows the ISP-corrected spectra, but note that we do not have a firm basis for the ISP estimate for this object. In these corrected spectra, almost no emission lines are seen in the polarized flux, and at the Balmer edge region, there seems to be again a slope down-turn at rest 4000 . However, we need more data to confirm these, and also need more data on Galactic stars around this object.

sources.summary: The paper images PG0003+158 as a large double steep-spectrum radio quasar, detecting diffuse lobe emission, extensive polarization, jet curvature, and ordered lobe and hotspot magnetic fields.

quotes.quote: Notes on individual sources PG0003+158 This SSRQ ( aka 4C+15.01) was imaged with the VLA A-array at 5 GHz by Miller1993 who detected only the bright and compact regions (i.e., core and hotspots) of this large double radio source. Our image detects the diffuse lobe emission as well and shows extensive polarization in its core, lobes and hostpots (Figure fig1 ). The inferred B-field is transverse to the jet direction for the optically thick radio core.

The inferred B-fields in the lobes are largely aligned with the local jet direction assuming optically thin emission. A jet region entering the southern hotspot is highly polarized, exhibiting parallel inferred B-fields. The inferred B-fields are aligned with the edges of the hotspot regions.

sources.summary: The paper measures PKS 0003+15 brighter than a literature value and concludes that its color variation needs a second component, with both thermal and non-thermal changes possible.

quotes.quote: PKS 0003+15. At the beginning of the observations we measured m_V = 15.36 0.02 , brighter than m_V=15.96 reported by Barvainis05 . A single non-thermal component fits the first data set with _ t_0 = 0.08 0.02 (Figure fig84a ). However, this fit cannot describes satisfactory the color variation (Figure fig84c ), and a second component must be added. The new fit has a_ V t_0 36 and T_ t_0 19,200 K (Figures fig84b ). Both thermal and non-thermal changes may account for the showed variations.

gmw_00001015.name: MC3 1750+175
gmw_00001015.overview: MC3 1750+175 is a radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:1004.1199] that exhibits historical radio variability [arXiv:0907.2405]. Optical observations have revealed V-band microvariability [arXiv:0907.2405] and spectral variations that require a mix of thermal and non-thermal components to explain [arXiv:1004.1199].

sources.summary: The paper finds MC3 1750+175 brighter than a reference magnitude and says a single power law cannot reproduce its spectral variation, requiring a thermal/non-thermal component mix.

quotes.quote: MC3 1750+175. For this object, we measure m_V=16.18 0.02 at the beginning of the observations, i.e., brighter than the value reported by Veron01 ( m_V=16.60 ). A power law with _ t_0 = 0.64 0.02 fits this first data set (Figure fig85a ). However, a second component is necessary to reproduce the observed spectral variation (Figure fig85c ). A mix of thermal and non-thermal components fits the data with T_ t_0 33,700 K and a_ V t_0 27 (Figure fig85b ). Non-thermal changes cannot explain this variation.

sources.summary: The paper reports that MC3 1750+175 previously had radio variability and shows a V-band optical microvariability event in the authors’ August 1999 observations.

quotes.quote: MC3 1750+175.- The only variability event previously reported for this quasar corresponds to radio wavelengths Fanti81,Mantovani82 . On 1999 August 20, we observed this object three times during 3.13 hours. The light-curves show a microvariability event only visible in the V band (Figure fig1e ). ANOVA confirms this appreciation, the amplitude of this variation is V = 0.04 0.008 mags between the first dataset and the third.

gmw_00001016.name: CSO 21
gmw_00001016.overview: CSO 21 is a quasar that previously lacked a positive detection of microvariability [arXiv:0907.2405]. However, observations in December 2001 revealed a gradual brightness increase, with ANOVA confirming a variation in the V band [arXiv:0907.2405].

sources.summary: The paper says CSO 21 lacked a prior positive microvariability detection, but the new December 2001 data show a gradual brightness increase mainly confirmed in V.

quotes.quote: CSO 21.- Observations for CSO 21 were reported in Paper I, but without a positive detection of microvariability. For the present study, the object was observed in three occasions on 2001 December, during 2.5 hours. A gradual brightness increase is appreciated in the three bands (Figure fig1f ). However, ANOVA indicates a variation in V only, a barely marginal variation in R (with a significance level of 10 ), and no variation in B (if the observed tendency in the B light-curve is a variation, it is hidden by the internal data dispersion).

gmw_00001017.overview: The object 1628.5+3808 is a quasar that exhibits optical microvariability [arXiv:0907.2405]. Observations in June 2001 revealed a brightness decrease that was detectable in the B and R bands, but only marginal in the V band [arXiv:0907.2405].

sources.summary: The paper reports earlier microvariability for 1628.5+3808 and finds a June 2001 brightness decrease, significant in B and R and marginal in V.

quotes.quote: 1628.5+3808.- In Paper I we reported the cases of microvariability for this object for the first time. They are two events on 1997 February an May. In February, a variation with amplitude of 0.082 mags throughout 2.4 hours; while in May, throughout six monitoring hours, the OM consisted of pulsations with amplitudes from 0.04 to 0.057 mags. We observed this quasar in two occasions on 2001 June 13.

The light curves show an appreciable brightness decrease in the three bands (Figure fig1g ). ANOVA indicates that this variation is detectable in the B and R , but in V the variation is only marginal (the significance level is 3 ).

gmw_00001018.name: US 3472
gmw_00001018.overview: US 3472 is a quasar that exhibits long-term optical and infrared variability [arXiv:0907.2405]. It also displays optical microvariability, including a brightness decrease in the V and R bands observed in December 2001 [arXiv:0907.2405].

sources.summary: The paper reports prior optical microvariability and long-term variability for US 3472, then finds new V- and R-band brightness decreases in December 2001.

quotes.quote: US 3472.- In Paper I we reported an OM event for this quasar with an increase of 0.02 mags in 50 minutes. During 2 hours, a series of oscillations followed to this brightness increase, with amplitudes between 0.040 and 0.055 mags. Long-term optical and infrared variability has also been reported Enya02a . We observed this object during 2.5 hours in three occasions on 2001 December 20.

The light-curves show a brightness decrease in V and R (Figure fig1h ). ANOVA confirms the variation. In V , the microvariation seems to have kept a constant rate with a total amplitude of V=0.020 0.003 mags in 2.35 hours.

gmw_00001019.name: Mrk 830
gmw_00001019.overview: Mrk 830 is a quasar that exhibits both long- and short-term optical variability [arXiv:0907.2405]. Observations have confirmed microvariability and spectral variations that can be explained by a single non-thermal component [arXiv:0907.2405, arXiv:1004.1199].

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 830 as previously variable and reports optical microvariability detections or marginal events on three observed nights, including confirmed changes on June 14.

quotes.quote: Mrk 830.- Long- and short-term variability, and microvaribility have been reported for this quasar. We observed this object in four different occasions, OM events being detected on 1999 August 17 and 18, and on 2001 June 14. The object possessed a brightness very similar on the three occasions, V 17.6 . This brightness is similar to that reported by Stepa01 ( V=17 .29 0.03 ), and Chavu95 ( V=17 .62 ).

2001 June 14.- On this night the quasar was observed twice. A visual revision of the light-curves indicates a possible variation in the three bands (Figure fig1k ). The brightness increase is a few hundredth of magnitude between the two groups of observations. ANOVA confirms the reliability of this variation.

sources.summary: The paper reports three nights of variation for Mrk 830 and concludes that a unique non-thermal component is sufficient, so it avoids adding a second component.

quotes.quote: Mrk 830. This quasar showed variations in three different nights. Its brightness was similar during the three nights ( m_V 17.6 ), and near to the brightness reported by Stepanian01 ( m_V=17.29 0.03 ) and by Chavu95 ( m_V=17.62 ). A unique component marginally fits to the data of this object (Figures fig89a , fig810a , fig811a ). Although a thermal component can be added with T 10,000 K, contrary to the case of 1628.5+3808, the non-thermal component is sufficient to explain the spectral variation. Then, the addition of a second component is avoided.

gmw_00001020.name: IGR J19443+2117
gmw_00001020.overview: IGR J19443+2117 is an active galactic nucleus that is detected as a point-like very high energy source [arXiv:1110.2119]. Its overall spectral energy distribution suggests an extragalactic origin consistent with an extreme high-energy-peaked blazar [arXiv:1110.2119].

sources.summary: The paper treats IGR J19443+2117 as the positional counterpart of a point-like VHE source and argues its spectral energy distribution favors an extreme high-energy-peaked blazar origin.

quotes.quote: Its position is consistent with the unidentified hard X-ray source IGR J19443+2117 . The source is detected at 7.9 in 25 hours of observation live-time: its flux corresponds to 0.02 Crab units, with a photon index of 3.1 0.3_ stat between 470 GeV and 6 TeV. The study of the spectral energy distribution of this source, including radio, infrared and X-ray data, suggests an extra-galactic origin of the emission.

A gamma-ray binary hypothesis is disfavored mainly due to the lack of a plausible massive stellar counterpart in optical/infrared and the absence of orbital variability. A pulsar wind nebula origin is disfavored by the very soft TeV spectrum and the absence of extended X-ray counterparts. On the other hand, the overall spectral energy distribution is consistent with an extreme high-energy-peaked blazar, with a synchrotron peak energy >1 keV.

gmw_00001021.name: RGG 20
gmw_00001021.overview: RGG 20 is a dwarf galaxy that hosts a broad-line active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1609.07148, arXiv:2108.09307]. This active nucleus exhibits strong X-ray emission [arXiv:1609.07148, arXiv:2108.09307] and broad hydrogen-alpha emission likely produced by gas photoionized by a radiatively efficient accretion disk [arXiv:2108.09307].

sources.summary: The paper reports archival Chandra results for RGG 20, including a luminous X-ray detection, an extracted spectrum with photon index 1.54, and an X-ray-to-UV ratio.

quotes.quote: RGG 20 (SDSS J122342.82+581446.4) was found to have a 0.5-8 keV luminosity of L=6.7 10^ 41 erg s^ 1 . There were a sufficient number of counts to extract a spectrum; the best-fit photon index was = 1.54 0.10 . The ratio of X-ray to UV emission ( _ OX ; see Section 3.2) is _ OX = -1.30.

sources.summary: The paper recovers NSA 256802 as a broad-line AGN and attributes its Fe X emission to gas photoionized by a radiatively efficient accretion disk.

quotes.quote: NSA 256802: This galaxy was designated as a broad-line AGN by . We recover this designation, and note that this is the only AGN object from that overlaps with our sample. Given the strong X-ray emission shown in Figure fig:expxrbalt as well as the prominent broad H emission, we conclude the \ emission seen in this object is likely produced by gas photoionized by a radiatively efficient accretion disk.

gmw_00001022.name: RGG 123
gmw_00001022.overview: RGG 123 is a dwarf galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1609.07148]. Archival observations of the galaxy reveal a high X-ray luminosity in the 0.5 to 8 keV energy band [arXiv:1609.07148].

sources.summary: The paper summarizes archival Chandra results for RGG 123, finding a high X-ray luminosity, too few counts for spectral extraction, and a hardness-ratio photon index.

quotes.quote: RGG 123 (SDSS J153425.58+040806.6) has a 0.5-8 keV luminosity of L=8.5 10^ 41 erg s^ 1 . There were not sufficient counts to extract an X-ray spectrum. The photon index as estimated from the hardness ratio is _ HR = 2.57 0.27 found that the photon index estimated from the hardness ratio is highly correlated with the photon index of the fitted spectrum for >2 . , and _ OX was found to be -1.22.

gmw_00001023.name: RGG 127
gmw_00001023.overview: RGG 127 is a dwarf disk galaxy [arXiv:0907.1370] that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0907.1370, arXiv:1609.07148] and a central intermediate-mass black hole [arXiv:0907.1370]. Its structure includes a low-Sersic pseudobulge, a bar-like feature, and an exponential outer disk or envelope [arXiv:1908.00020].

sources.summary: RGG 127 has ambiguous edge-on or face-on structure; its low-Sersic inner component is classified as a pseudobulge with a bar-like feature and exponential outer disk/envelope.

quotes.quote: RGG 127 (Figure fig:RGG127_Model ) is more difficult to classify. Two obvious interpretations of its structure come to mind. First is that we are observing a disk galaxy edge-on, with the bright extended feature being the edge-on disk and the diffuse, rounder feature being an envelope/halo surrounding the disk. The second interpretation would be that the galaxy is being observed close to face-on and the bright, thin feature is a bar and the outer feature is a dimmer disk.

The inner most component has a Sersic index of n 1 with a half light radius of 0.09 kpc. From the low Sersic index of this component, the presence of a possible bar feature and the low central surface brightness indicates this component should be classified as a pseudobulge. The bar structure has a Sersic index of n 0.5 and a half-light radius of 0.88 kpc. The outer disk/envelope has a Sersic index of 0.7 with a half-light radius of 1.25 kpc, indicating a roughly exponential disk.

sources.summary: The paper finds that RGG 127 has enough Chandra counts for spectral fitting, possible intrinsic absorption, a fitted photon index near 2.18, and quasar-like hardness.

quotes.quote: RGG 127, however, has a best fit n_ H approximately twice the galactic value, indicating potential intrinsic absorption. For RGG 127, we find a corresponding best fit of 2.18^ +0.55 _ 0.35 . If we freeze n_ H to the galactic value, we obtain =2.03 .

The hardness ratios for RGG 119 and RGG 127 are consistent with the expected hardness ratios for 2.0-2.5 ; these are in turn consistent with the which best fit the extracted X-ray spectra (2.25 and 2.18 for RGG 119 and RGG 127, respectively).

sources.summary: The paper flags SDSSJ160531.84+174826.1 as a marginal AGN candidate, reports it as an X-ray source, and notes literature identifying it as a dwarf disk galaxy hosting an intermediate-mass black hole.

quotes.quote: A total of five galaxies lie clearly above the sequence of Kauffmann et al. -- KUG1602+175 ( 9), NGC6050 ( 21), IC1182 ( 39), PGC057064 ( 29) and KUG1603+179A ( 33) -- and two of them only marginally -- UGC10190 ( 27) and SDSSJ160531.84+174826.1 ( 34) --.
In addition IC1182 ( 39) and SDSSJ160531.84+174826.1 ( 34) are reported to be X-ray sources in NED, which could be indicative of the presence of an AGN.
SDSSJ160531.84+174826.1 ( 34) has been reported to host an intermediate-mass black hole, besides being a dwarf disk galaxy (Dong et al. 2007).

gmw_00001024.name: NGC 1512
gmw_00001024.overview: NGC 1512 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1907.10188, arXiv:1405.3495] in the Dorado group [arXiv:2404.19473] that is undergoing a tidal interaction with the dwarf companion NGC 1510 [arXiv:1405.3495, arXiv:1101.0815]. The galaxy features a double-ring structure [arXiv:2404.19473] with a star-forming nuclear ring [arXiv:1610.09125, arXiv:1004.5393] and a bar dominated by old stellar populations [arXiv:2405.00107].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1512 as a barred spiral with rings, pseudorings, nuclear ring, pseudobulge, bar, disk, and an outer ring traced by curvature.

quotes.quote: NGC 1512 is classified as a barred spiral galaxy SBa RC3_1991 , however there are many references bringing up other components in its morphology: rings, pseudorings, nuclear ring and pseudobulge see for example laurikainen2006,fisher2008 . According to , who conducted a detailed study on this galaxy, it has a bulge, a bar and a disk. fig:ngc1512ird2009kur shows the results for . The region inside R/R_p<0.5 shows two components delimited by the local peak at R/R_p 0.15 , and both shows small compared with the overall curvature profile.

From laurikainen2006,compere2014 , the innermost region (1) is a nuclear ring and region 2 is dominated by the bulge. By the shape of one must consider a pseudobulge component, which is in agreement with fisher2009,fisher2010 . Regarding the bar, it seems that pseudobulge and bar coexist (the change in the position angle and ellipticity between the bulge and the bar is very smooth ), with the bar connecting the pseudobulge and the outer ring -- according to , a pseudoring (region 3).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes young cluster luminosity functions in NGC 1512, describing its remarkably luminous circumnuclear starburst ring and HST imaging used for cluster selection.

quotes.quote: We focus specifically on NGC 1512 and NGC 6951, for which we have access to H data that allow us to unambiguously identify the youngest sample clusters. To place our results on a firm statistical footing, we first explore in detail a number of important technical issues affecting the process from converting the observational data into the spectral-energy distributions of the objects in our final catalogues. The CLFs of the young clusters in both galaxies exhibit approximate power-law behaviour down to the 90 per cent observational completeness limits, thus showing that star cluster formation in the violent environments of starburst rings appears to proceed similarly as that elsewhere in the local Universe. We discuss this result in the context of the density of the interstellar medium in our starburst-ring galaxies.

We will also examine in detail the CLF of the clusters in the remarkably luminous circumnuclear starburst ring in NGC 1512. NGC 1512 is an SB()a galaxy at a distance of 10 Mpc (e.g., Maoz et al. 2001).

sources.summary: The paper groups NGC 1512 among galaxies with declining recent star formation and describes its double-ring structure, ring age, Dorado membership, and interaction with NGC 1510.

quotes.quote: NGC 628, 4254, 4303, 4321, 4535, and 5068 belong to the high end of the distribution, while NGC 1365, 1512, 1566, 3351, and 3627 belong to its low end.This is also summarised in the last column of Table tab:yes_no . Looking at the distribution across the galactic disks in the 3 ^ rd column of Figure fig:all_maps , we observe large areas of increasing recent SF in NGC 628, 4254, 4303, and 4321, while NGC 1512, 1566, and 3627 exhibit recent SF activity that ranges from roughly unchanged to decreasing.

NGC 1512 has a double ring structure with one ring located around the nucleus and another one situated in the disk, which is not uncommon in barred galaxies. estimated the age of the nuclear ring in NGC 1512 to be 40 Myr on average.

NGC 1512 and NGC 1566 are members of the Dorado group Maia_89 . Finally, NGC 3627 is a galaxy in the Leo Triplet Ferrarese_00 . We caution, however, that a more thorough examination of the group and cluster membership would require a consideration about its size or density as well as a galaxy's position in it. Past gravitational interactions Among interacting galaxies, there is NGC 1512, which is interacting with the dwarf galaxy NGC 1510.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1512 as having a circumnuclear ring and spiral arms affected by companion NGC 1510; BLAST observed the system and detected the core and central spiral.

quotes.quote: NGC 1512 NGC 1512, presented in Fig. fig:ngc1512 , has a nucleus surrounded by a circumnuclear ring maoz2000 . Beyond the ring, its spiral arms are complicated by its satellite galaxy, NGC 1510 kinman1978 . BLAST made three observations of a 0.45 deg ^2 area centered on NGC 1512, with a total integration time of 28 minutes. BLAST detects the core and tightly wound central spiral of NGC 1512. BLAST also detects the companion galaxy, NGC 1510.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1512 as an early-type barred galaxy with inner and nuclear rings, whose blue and red nuclear-ring colors imply young supergiant populations.

quotes.quote: Figure nrcolors , left, shows optical and near-IR images and colour index maps of NGC 1512, an early-type barred galaxy that harbours both inner and nuclear rings. The nuclear ring in NGC 1512 is blue in a B-I colour index map because of large numbers of young blue supergiants. However, the ring is `red' in a J-K_ s colour index map, implying the likely presence of a large number of young red supergiants as well. In contrast, the nuclear ring of NGC 3081 is also blue in a B-I colour index map (Buta Purcell 1998) but is largely invisible in a J-K_ s map (Fig. nrcolors , right), implying far fewer red supergiants (and probably a more evolved ring than the one in NGC 1512).

sources.summary: NGC 1512 is described as a barred spiral interacting with NGC 1510; XMM-Newton finds sources in its D25 region and HMXBs consistent with its star formation rate.

quotes.quote: The galaxy NGC 1512 is interacting with the smaller galaxy NGC 1510 and shows a peculiar morphology, characterised by two extended arms immersed in an HI disc whose size is about four times larger than the optical diameter of NGC 1512. For the first time we performed a deep X-ray observation of the galaxies NGC 1512 and NGC 1510 with XMM-Newton to gain information on the population of X-ray sources and diffuse emission in a system of interacting galaxies. We identified and classified the sources detected in the \ field of view by means of spectral analysis, hardness-ratios calculated with a Bayesian method, X-ray variability, and cross-correlations with catalogues in optical, infrared, and radio wavelengths. We also made use of archival \ (X-ray) and Australia Telescope Compact Array (radio) data to better constrain the nature of the sources detected with . We detected 106 sources in the energy range of 0.2 12 keV, out of which 15 are located within the D_ 25 regions of NGC 1512 and NGC 1510 and at least six sources coincide with the extended arms.

Taking into account the contribution of low-mass X-ray binaries and active galactic nuclei, the number of high-mass X-ray binaries detected within the D_ 25 region of NGC 1512 is consistent with the star formation rate obtained in previous works based on radio, infrared optical, and UV wavelengths.

NGC 1512 is a barred spiral galaxy (SB()a; deVaucouleurs91 ) with two star-forming rings and two extended arms immersed in an HI disc about four times larger than the optical diameter, probably caused by an ongoing tidal interaction with the neighbouring galaxy NGC 1510 Kinman78 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1512 as one of the two youngest and least massive nuclear rings, with high specific star formation and the highest normalized young-cluster mass fraction.

quotes.quote: The two youngest nuclear rings in our sample, NGC 1512 and NGC 4314, which have the most extreme physical properties, represent the young extremity of the nuclear ring age distribution.

Interestingly, the nuclear ring of NGC 1512 is the second youngest ( 40 Myr-old) ring in the sample, with the lowest stellar mass, but it is characterized by the highest YMC mass fraction when normalized by parent ring stellar mass, as shown in Fig. 8.

Figures 6 to 8, show that the ring properties in NGC 1512 and NGC 4314 are outliers in almost all cases. These are the least massive and youngest rings with the highest specific SFRs and YMC mass fractions (for which the relatively lower ring stellar masses may play a crucial role in determining their physical properties). We have marked these two rings separately in Figs 6--8.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1512 as an interacting peculiar galaxy whose SFRs are faint in infrared because of low surface brightness and are dimmer than comparison regions.

quotes.quote: The star-forming regions (SFRs) in the peculiar galaxies NGC 660, NGC 1512, NGC 4395 and NGC 4618 are studied. The relationships between such characteristics of star forming regions as UV, nearand far-IR fluxes, as well as in the H and HI lines, surface brightness in these ranges, and the scatter of the radial velocities of ionized and neutral hydrogen are considered. It is shown that in all the galaxies considered, the IR fluxes from SFRs are less than in the “normal” galaxies, but for different reasons: in the galaxies with signs of recent interaction NGC 660 and NGC 1512, this is due to the low surface brightness of SFRs; in the lopsided galaxies NGC 4395 and NGC 4618, the low brightness of SFRs in the infrared range is due to their compact size.

Since the Herschel data are also available for the NGC 1512 galaxy, we can see how its near and far IR range fluxes compare with “normal” galaxies. In Fig. F8_24_FIR , the total flux in the far IR range (the sum of fluxes at wavelengths of 70, 100, and 160 m) is compared with fluxes at wavelengths of 8 and 24 m. It is obvious that the SFRs in the galaxy NGC 1512 in the far IR range are also significantly dimmer than the SFRs in the comparison galaxy (both central and peripheral), although in comparison with the fluxes at wavelengths of 8 and 24 m, they show the same trend.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1512's bar dust lane has enhanced velocity gradients and CO, while star formation is ring-concentrated and the bar is dominated by LINER/Seyfert-like old populations.

quotes.quote: We find that in NGC 1512, enhanced velocity gradients coincide with the CO emission detected along the bar dust lane which can be seen in the optical image.
In particular, SF in NGC 1512 is concentrated in the nuclear ring and the inner ring that encircles the bar, and bright H sources are rare inside of the ring that encompasses the bar.

The whole bar of NGC 1512 is dominated by the LINER or Seyfert type emissions judging by the BPT diagnostics. It should be also noted that enhanced emission-line ratios do not necessarily guarantee that the ionizing source of the regions are shock originated. The leaking of ionizing photons from HII regions are suggested to be able to explain the diffuse ionized gas with LINER-like emissions ( belfiore_22 ). Also, ionization by hot evolved stars (e.g., post AGB) are found to present LINER-like emission ( singh_13 ). As post AGB stars are common in galaxies, their ionizing effect can be observed unless a strong radiation from SF or AGN outshines them. As we observe superimposed different ionizing sources along the line of sight, not just on the disk plane but also in the galactic halo, if there is lack of SF in the disk, we may well be left with the LINER-like emission from the post AGBs located along the line of sight. The whole bar regions of NGC 1512 may consist of old stars. Indeed, pessa_23 find that the mean mass weighted age of the stellar populations in the bar region of NGC 1512 is 10 Gyr.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1512 as a barred spiral interacting with NGC 1510, using HI disturbances to infer the companion mass and location.

quotes.quote: Characterizing NGC 1512's Satellite From Its Tidal Imprints The NGC 1512/1510 interacting system provides another opportunity to test our method. Figure fig:1512data displays the Australia Telescope Compact Array (ATCA) image from the THINGS-SOUTH program of NGC 1512 by De Blok et al. NGC 1512 is a barred spiral galaxy, and its companion is a blue compact dwarf galaxy, NGC 1510, which is marked by a cross in Figure fig:1512data .

The separation between the perturber and NGC 1512 at the best-fit time in the simulation is a factor of 2 larger than the extent of the stellar spiral arms, which is reasonably close (to within a factor of 2 ) to the observational determination.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 1512 in its spiral metallicity-gradient sample, using literature morphology, confirming its reported O/H radial-break behavior, and excluding low-abundance inner-arm regions affected by interaction.

quotes.quote: For NGC 1365, NGC 1512 and NGC 2805 we have also partially used morphological parameters from the literature as compiled images were not suitable for the whole analysis.

For seven galaxies of our sample (NGC 1058, NGC 1512, NGC 3359, NGC 3621, NGC 4625, NGC 5236, NGC 5457) radial breaks in the 12+ (O/H) profile, at a galactocentric position close to the isophotal radius r_ 25 , have been reported by previous work (see Table bibliografia_gal for the references). We confirm with our re-analysis of the compiled data the presence of such breaks in 12+ (O/H) via an improvement in _ ^2 in the double linear fit with respect to the single linear fit to the radial profile (see Section fits and Figs. Afig1 Afig54 ).
NGC 1512: \ regions of the southern inner arm of NGC 1512 have not been taken into account in the fits (lower abundance possibly due to the interaction with NGC 1510; see lopez-sanchez15 ).

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1512 as a pseudobulge galaxy, citing The bulge has a prominent nuclear ring and spiral; n_b=1.8 , and the bulges is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =0.2 mag.

quotes.quote: NGC 1512 .SBR1../SBb(rs) pec Pseudobulge. The bulge has a prominent nuclear ring and spiral. The decomposition yields n_b=1.8 , and the bulges is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =0.2 mag.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 1512 among galaxies with radial cold-dust temperature dependence, heating maps similar to temperature maps, and stronger SFR-to-dust-mass proxy behavior.

quotes.quote: When both parameters are fitted simultaneously, the cold dust temperature maps for 10 galaxies (NGC 1097, NGC 1512, NGC 3351, NGC 4254, NGC 4321, NGC 4569, NGC 4579, NGC 4736, NGC 5055, and NGC 7331) exhibit a strong radial dependence.

As might be expected, we see a strong similarity between the heating maps and the T_c maps, particularly for NGC 1512, NGC 3351, NGC 4321, NGC 4579, NGC 4736, and NGC 5055, similar to the correlation between resolved 24 m emission and the cold dust temperature found for NGC 891 hughes2014 .
The remaining three galaxies, NGC 1512, NGC 4569, and NGC 5055, have 0.4 .

gmw_00001025.name: NGC 6951
gmw_00001025.overview: NGC 6951 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1102.5104, arXiv:1703.05836] hosting an active Seyfert 2 or transition-type nucleus [arXiv:0705.4162, arXiv:astro-ph/0306391]. Its central region features a starburst ring [arXiv:1102.5104, arXiv:1205.4242] and nuclear spiral arms [arXiv:astro-ph/0507070], with gas kinematics showing both streaming inflows and jet-driven outflows [arXiv:0712.3747, arXiv:2007.09123].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 6951 as a nearby barred spiral with a starburst ring, molecular gas inflow evidence, and gas morphology explained by a large-scale barred potential.

quotes.quote: In this paper we study the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 6951. Its distance of 24.1 Mpc allows for high spatial resolution ( 1 = 117 pc). There are several arguments that gas inflow is currently occurring in this galaxy. A large-scale stellar bar has been detected in the near-infrared Buta2003,Block2004 with a bar radius of 26 ( 3.0 kpc) and a position angle of 84 Mulchaey1997 . This galaxy also shows a pronounced starburst (SB) ring at 5 ( = 580 pc) radius in H Perez2000, Rozas2002 and radio Saikia2002 emission.

Motivation NGC 6951 has a prominent large-scale stellar bar with a radius of 3.0 kpc at a position angle of 84 Mulchaey1997 . We suggested in Sect. sec:properties that the gas morphology and kinematics in NGC 6951 can be well explained in terms of this barred potential. The spiral pattern and the high intensity twin peak morphology are both consistent with gas streamlines under the influence of a large-scale bar.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6951 as a comparison galaxy with a measured nuclear HCN/CO ratio, optically thinner LVG loci than NGC 3227, and similarly high central published ratios.

quotes.quote: Comparison to NGC 6951 and NGC 1068 NGC 1068 and NGC 6951 are two other galaxies for which the HCN(1-0)/CO(2-1) ratio has been measured on comparable 100 pc scales. We use flux densities reported by for the nuclear region (denoted `C' in their Table 1) of NGC 6951; and also the values for the circumnuclear disk of NGC 1068, as the sum of the red and blue channels reported in Table 3 of . These yield line ratios (for line fluxes in Jy km s ^ 1 ) of 0.37 0.05 and 0.214 0.002 respectively, and are denoted by the solid magenta lines on Fig.  fig:lvg . These lines appear almost exclusively in the panels corresponding to the highest HCN abundance we have considered, X_ HCN /X_ CO =10^ 2 . In contrast to NGC 3227, in which the line emission appears to be optically thick, the loci of the magenta lines for NGC 1068 and NGC 6951 are toward the optically thin (left) side of the panels.

NGC 6951 and NGC 1068 have similarly high published ratios in their central 100 pc. The nuclear HCN/CO intensity ratio in NGC 3227 may reflect optically thick line emission in dense gas with only a weak constraint on the HCN/CO abundance ratio. However, our LVG calculations also indicate that the high nuclear ratios in all three of these galaxies are more consistent with a single set of physical properties corresponding to warm 300 K, dense 10^ 5.5 cm ^ 3 gas, in which the emission lines are optically thinner, but in which the HCN/CO abundance ratio is very large 10^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper summarizes NGC 6951 nuclear dense-gas emission, compares its kinematics with ionized gas, and notes a starburst ring plus enhanced nuclear molecular emission near the AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 6951 The properties of this source have already been presented by K07. As stated in that work, reconstructing an image with uniformly weighted visibilities provides high enough resolution that the emission from the ring does not contaminate the nuclear signal. Here we briefly summarize the main characteristics of the nuclear emission that are required for our modelling, noting that the values listed in Table tb:lines agree very well with the K07 analysis and references therein. The \ central emission (Fig. fg:6951 ) originates from a region of 1.44 1.10 at PA = 73^ , at best only marginally resolved with respect to the beam (Table 1), with an integrated flux of 1.02 0.02 Jy km/s within 3 .

In addition to its AGN, NGC 6951 also exhibits a pronounced starburst ring located at 5 (480 pc) from the nucleus. Plausibly, most of the PAH emission may originate from this ring, which has been observed in several wavebands: near-IR mar03 , optical (e.g. sto07 and references therein), and radio sai02 . High resolution observations detect strong CO and \ emission both in the ring and the central AGN (K07; gar05 , koh99 ). While the /CO ratio in the ring is typical of a starburst, the \ is significantly enhanced in the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6951 as a Seyfert 2 with nuclear spiral arms and a pseudo-ring holding most gas, feeding a starburst while only a compact complex reaches the AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 6951 NGC 6951 is a prototypical Seyfert 2 galaxy for which sub-arcsecond resolution ^ 12 CO(2--1) maps have been completed within the NUGA project (Fig. fig:feeding-6951 a adapted from Schinnerer et al. 2005, in prep.). The molecular gas distribution in the central 1 kpc consists of two nuclear spiral arms that can be traced for over 180 ^ ; the winding spiral arms end up as a highly contrasted pseudo-ring at 350 pc. The spiral arms can be identified by their red color in the J--H HST image of the galaxy, shown in Fig. fig:feeding-6951 a . This m=2 gas instability contains a significant gas reservoir of 3 10 ^ 8 M _ which is presently feeding a nuclear starburst, also identified by its intense radio continuum and H emissions (Ho Ulvestad , Rozas et al. ).

The geometry of the molecular gas ridges likely reflects the crowding of molecular clouds along the x _2 family of orbits of the prominent stellar bar, detected in all NIR images of NGC 6951 (M arquez Moles ; Friedli et al. ; P erez et al. ). As shown in Fig. fig:feeding-6951 a , only a small amount of molecular gas has succeeded in making its way down to the AGN: most of the molecular gas mass is trapped in the nuclear spiral arms, quite similar to the case of NGC 4579 (though in this galaxy molecular gas likely populates both x _1 and x _2 orbits). A compact unresolved molecular complex (denoted as G in Fig. fig:feeding-6951 a, b ) of a few 10 ^ 6 M _ is detected at the position of the central engine. This component could correspond to a molecular torus (of 40-50 pc size).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6951 as a barred spiral with a Seyfert/LINER-like nucleus, a star-forming annulus, nested circumnuclear disks, regular rotation, and central emission-line dispersion.

quotes.quote: NGC 6951 is a spiral galaxy which has a large stellar bar (PA  90 ). Its nucleus is classified as Seyfert 2 Ho3 , but suggest that it could be considered as a transition object, between a very high excitation LINER and a Seyfert 2. The ionised gas ( ) is mainly concentrated in the nuclear region and form an annulus of radius 5 , in which massive star formation is taking place. interpreted the \ kinematics in the circumnuclear zone as a series of nested disks, decoupled from one another.

The stellar continuum presents isophotes elongated along a PA consistent with the galactic disk major-axis PA 138 6951_1 . The stellar velocity field shows regular rotation pattern whit the major-axis aligned with the line-of-nodes of the galaxy. The stellar velocity dispersion shows a drop in the inner 5 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 6951 as another LINER nucleus where H-alpha velocity residuals trace spiral streaming, ring motions, and radio-jet-driven outflows.

quotes.quote: We have recently found similar results to those described above for NGC 1097 around another LINER nucleus, NGC 6951 (Storchi-Bergmann et al. 2007), which are shown in Fig. kin . In the top left panel we show the velocity obtained from the peak wavelength of the H emission line, where arrows show deviations from circular rotation along two partial spiral arms. An exponential disk model, illustrated in the top right panel of this figure was again subtracted from the observed velocity field, leading to the residuals shown in the bottom right panel. The residuals include non-circular motions along the star-forming ring shown in the structure map in the bottom left panel of the figure, and, within the ring, residuals due to two motions: streaming motions along the partial spiral arms identified in the top left panel combined with outflows due to the effect of a nuclear radio jet in the interstellar gas.

We have discovered the effect of the radio jet in the velocity dispersion map, which showed enhancements in the gas velocity dispersion in two blobs to NW and SE, approximately within 1 arcsecond from the nucleus. Then we found out that there is indeed an elongated radio source observed by Saikia et al. (2002), extended in the direction where we observed the enhancements in the velocity dispersion, in agreement with the interpretation that the interaction of the radio jet with the circumnuclear ISM is producing the observed outflows. Thus, in this LINER, we could observe both the inflows and outflows.

sources.summary: NGC 6951 combines AGN activity, bars, and spiral arms; the authors infer bar- and spiral-related gas transport plus central inflow/outflow that accumulates gas near 0.45 kpc.

quotes.quote: NGC 6951 is a representative galaxy with a combination of AGN activity, stellar bars (inner and large-scale) and spiral arms (visible in NIR image and HI map). The gas flow pattern from 15-28 kpc reflects the stellar spiral arms and is increasingly dominated by the gravitational torques caused by the bar towards the center (see Fig. fig_torque ). The molecular gas distribution in the center shows two gaseous spiral arms whose gas flow underlie the gravitational torques caused by the inner bar.

For the outer disk we found that the gas is transported outwards (d L/L 0.15 ) from (19-27) kpc. On intermediate scales inflow occurs from (19-11) kpc, while outflow is dominant from (4-11) kpc. The radius where the gas flow is changing from inflow to outflow (11 kpc) does not overlap with any resonances of a single pattern (i.e. bar or spiral).
For the center we found gas inflow from (0.8-0.45) kpc and outflow from (0.3-0.45) kpc, suggesting a gas accumulation at 0.45 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper uses molecular line ratios in NGC 6951 to argue that its central molecular gas is not self-gravitating, is likely pressure-confined, and resembles a supernova-dominated environment.

quotes.quote: We focus on NGC 6951 which is one of the Seyfert galaxies in the combined sample summarised in Table tab:combined , and for which suitable data are available. Observations reported by provide the HCN (1-0)/CO (2-1) ratio, which is 2.5 (if units for line fluxes are K km s ^ 1 ; it is 0.37 if the line flux units are Jy km s ^ 1 ) as discussed by . These data have similar small beams and so are spatially resolved, which is important since the distributions of the two lines are very different. Here we use the line ratio corresponding to the central component only.

An analysis of the HCN (1-0)/CO (2-1) and HCN (3-2)/HCN (1-0) line ratios in NGC 6951 indicates that the molecular gas is not in self-gravitating clouds. Instead, the clouds are likely to be pressure confined. The implied pressure of P/k 10^7 cm ^ 3 K is comparable to that measured in the centers of superwinds, suggesting that the interstellar medium in the centre of NGC 6951 is consistent with an environment dominated by supernova.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 6951 as a spiral with a well-studied circumnuclear starburst ring, using multi-filter HST archive imaging to build young-cluster luminosity functions.

quotes.quote: We focus specifically on NGC 1512 and NGC 6951, for which we have access to H data that allow us to unambiguously identify the youngest sample clusters. To place our results on a firm statistical footing, we first explore in detail a number of important technical issues affecting the process from converting the observational data into the spectral-energy distributions of the objects in our final catalogues. The CLFs of the young clusters in both galaxies exhibit approximate power-law behaviour down to the 90 per cent observational completeness limits, thus showing that star cluster formation in the violent environments of starburst rings appears to proceed similarly as that elsewhere in the local Universe. We discuss this result in the context of the density of the interstellar medium in our starburst-ring galaxies.

We start by focussing on the starburst-ring cluster population in the spiral galaxy NGC 6951, an SAB(rs)bc galaxy with a well-known and well-studied ring: see Barth et al. (1995) for an early HST based cluster study, as well as the review by vdL13 (and references therein). We will also examine in detail the CLF of the clusters in the remarkably luminous circumnuclear starburst ring in NGC 1512. NGC 1512 is an SB()a galaxy at a distance of 10 Mpc (e.g., Maoz et al. 2001). We first explore in detail a number of technical issues: see Section 2. In Section 3, we address the shapes of the CLFs in our two main sample galaxies. Finally, we place the results obtained for the individual ring populations in the broader context of the field of star cluster evolution in Section 5. Data sets Our perusal of the HST Data Archive showed that NGC 6951 has been observed by a number of investigators using a range of cameras. Table data.tab (top) includes details of the full data set acquired from the Hubble Legacy Archive (HLA).

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 6951’s central 200 pc, finding a misaligned molecular disc, turbulent gas linked to jet/cone structures, and evidence for connected feeding-feedback activity.

quotes.quote: We present a study of the central 200 pc of the galaxy NGC 6951, SAB(rs)bc, an active twin of the Milky Way, at a distance of 24 Mpc. Its nucleus has been observed in the optical with the GMOS-IFU, showing an outflow, and with the HST/ACS, revealing two extended structures with similar orientation, suggesting the presence of a collimating and/or obscuring structure. In order to ascertain this hypothesis, adaptive optics assisted NIR integral field spectroscopic observations were obtained with the NIFS spectrograph in the Gemini North telescope.

We detected a compact structure of H _2 molecular gas, interpreted as a nearly edge-on disc with diameter of 47 pc, PA=124 and velocity range from -40 to +40 km s ^ 1 . This disc is misaligned by 32 with respect to the radio jet and the ionization cones seen in the optical. There are two regions of turbulent gas, with position angles similar to the jet/cones, seen both in molecular and ionized phases; these regions are connected to the edges of the molecular disc and coincide with a high ratio of [ N II ] /H =5, suggesting that these regions are shock excited, partially ionized or both. We explain these structures as a consequence of a ``digging process'' that the jet inflicts on the disc, ejecting the molecular gas towards the ionization cones.

sources.summary: The paper finds that NGC 6951 has non-circular ionized-gas motions, likely radial outflow from an active nucleus, with radio evidence for a small jet-like feature.

quotes.quote: Nevertheless, the [ NII ] rotation velocities are lower than those of the inside the circumnuclear starforming ring at =5''-10'' , while the IFP and [ NII ] and the MPFS variations are the same. In contrast, the MPFS [ OIII ] velocity field exhibits more significant non-circular ionized gas motions. The dynamical axis, found from this line measurements, turns by 30^ at <3'' , and the rotation velocity is lower than the velocities by a factor of 2. The intensity of the [ OIII ] emission decreases sharply outside this region.

It is possible that radial motions of highly excited ionized gas, with a speed of the same order of the rotation velocities, occur here. Radial motions are connected with the outflow from an active nucleus, which is of a transition type between a LINER and a Sy 2 (P erez et al., ). Moreover, the non-thermal radio continuum image shows a jet-like feature, extended from the nucleus up to 80 pc (Saikia et al., ).

sources.summary: The paper compares its circumnuclear-disk model to NGC 6951, emphasizing the galaxy's barred Seyfert morphology, starburst ring, stellar mass, age, and central black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: NGC 6951 is a Seyfert 2 galaxy ( z=0.004750 ) with a barred spiral morphology. It exhibits a characteristic starburst ring with radius of 580 pc vanderLaan:2013qy but other studies have claimed a radius of 480 pc Sani:2012sani . Van der Laan et al. (2013) has determined the stellar content on the circumnuclear rings that spans from young to old stars. The stellar clusters along the ring contain predominantly massive stars with intermediate ages. The total stellar mass estimated in the ring is 6 10^ 7 M_ with an estimated age between 1 Gyr and 1.5 Gyr.

We apply and adapt here our model to reproduce the properties of the ring in NGC 6951 related to the stellar mass, the radius of the ring, the evolution of the star formation rate as well as the central SMBH mass.

sources.summary: NGC 6951 is treated as a later-type barred galaxy where non-circular gas motions arise from a central flocculent gas-and-dust minispiral rather than a dynamically decoupled inner bar.

quotes.quote: In NGC 6951, the non-circular gas motions are associated with the central minispiral, which is discussed in the next section.

Wozniak et al. (1995) detected an isophotal twist at < 5-6'' in the ground-based optical images of NGC 6951 within its outer bar 60'' in size, which they interpreted as an inner bar. However, based on near-infrared photometry, Friedli et al. (1996) questioned this interpretation by assuming that the isophotal twist could result from a complex distribution of dust and star-forming regions within the central kiloparsec.

However, the minispiral in NGC 6951 differs from the large-scale spirals in disk galaxies in that the multi-arm spiral seen in the residual-intensity distribution (Fig. 6c) is probably associated only with the distribution of gas and dust rather than with the stellar component. As was shown by P erez et al. (2000), this inner spiral structure clearly seen in the V band completely disappears in the H band, where the effect of dust absorption is much weaker. Interestingly, the position angle of the dynamical axis constructed from the ionized-gas velocity field in the and [ N II ] lines at = 0-8'' deviates by 10-15^ from the line of nodes of the outer disk, suggesting a significant role of non-circular motions.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6951 as the barred spiral Seyfert host of SN 2000E, gives the supernova offset from its nucleus, and revises the host distance.

quotes.quote: We present optical ( UBVRI ) and near-infrared ( JHK ) photometry, along with optical spectra, of the Type Ia supernova SN 2000E in the spiral galaxy NGC 6951.

It was located 6.3 west and 26.7 south of the nucleus of NGC 6951 (see Fig. f:gal ), an inclined ( i=42 ) barred spiral galaxy hosting a type 2 Seyfert AGN whose coordinates are (2000.0)=20:37:13.97 and (2000.0)=+66:06:19.87 . For a list of galaxy properties, see Kohno, Kawabe Vila-Vilar o (1999) and references therein. The quoted distance is 24.1 Mpc.
