Assuming local virial scaling relations, we derive a black hole mass of (M_ BH /M_ ) =6.2 0.3 , accreting at about 5 times the Eddington rate. While super-Eddington accretion is probably episodic, if it has been occurring for the previous 100 Myr, then the black hole could have potentially originated even from a stellar mass seed at z 12-15. We finally discuss that our finding naturally explains the high luminosity of GN-z11 and can also provide an explanation for its exceptionally high nitrogen abundance.

sources.summary: The paper treats GNz11 as a z=10.6 high-redshift AGN candidate whose point source can be fit by a super-Eddington 1.5 million solar-mass black hole in a young host.

quotes.quote: For GNz11, Fig. fig:gnz11SED compares the predicted emission spectra powered by accreting BHs (upper panel) and their host galaxies (lower panel) with the photometric data reported by tacchella2023 for the central point source (which Maiolino et al. 2023 attribute to the AGN emission) and the extended component, respectively. In the upper panel, we show the flux density predicted for two accreting BHs with mass M_ BH 1.5 10^6 M_ , selected from the SE model ( _ Edd = 2.4 , azure lines) and from the EL model ( _ Edd = 0.4 , black dashed-dotted line). Using the same color-coding, in the bottom panel we show the flux density predicted for their host galaxies. The two accreting BHs are hosted by galaxies with different stellar mass ( log (M_ star /M_ ) = 8.6 in the EL model, and log (M_ star /M_ ) = 8 in the SE model), but with the same ISM metallicity ( Z = 0.06 Z_ ).

Our analysis supports the interpretation that the central point source of GNz11 could be powered by a 1.5 10^ 6 M_ super-Eddington accreting BH ( _ Edd 2 - 3 ), while CEERS-1019 harbours a more massive BH, with M_ BH 10^7 M_ , accreting sub-Eddington ( _ Edd 0.45 - 1 ), with a dominant emission from the host galaxy. We also analyse the evolutionary tracks of CEERS-1019 and GNz11 synthetic analogues in the EL and in the SE models to investigate the origin and fate of these systems. We find that there might be an evolutionary link between systems with a mass similar to GNz11 at z = 10.6 and systems with a mass similar to CEERS-1019 at z = 8.7 .

sources.summary: The paper reports spectroscopy around GN-z11 revealing candidate HeII emission from a PopIII clump, AGN-related HeII closer to GN-z11, and extended Ly emission.

quotes.quote: We present NIRSpec integral field spectroscopy and micro-shutter array spectroscopic observations of the region around GN-z11, an exceptionally luminous galaxy at z=10.6 , that reveal a greater than 5 detection of a feature consistent with being HeII 1640 emission at the redshift of GN-z11. The very high equivalent width of the putative HeII emission in this clump ( (EW_ rest (HeII)/ ) = 1.79^ +0.15 _ 0.25 ) and a lack of metal lines can be explained in terms of photoionisation by PopIII stars, while photoionisation by PopII stars is inconsistent with the data. The high equivalent width would also indicate that the putative PopIII stars likely have an initial mass function with an upper cutoff reaching at least 500 M _ .

We find that photoionisation by the active galactic nucleus (AGN) in GN-z11 cannot account for the HeII luminosity observed in the clump but can potentially be responsible for an additional HeII emission observed closer to GN-z11. We also consider the possibility of in situ photoionisation by an accreting direct collapse black hole hosted by the HeII clump. We find that this scenario is less favoured, but it remains a possible alternative interpretation. We also report the detection of a Ly halo stemming out of GN-z11 and extending out to 2 kpc as well as resolved funnel-shaped CIII emission likely tracing the ionisation cone of the AGN.

sources.summary: The paper includes GN-z11 as the previously identified z=10.6 JADES AGN, noting its outflow, targeted observations, NLSy1-like classification, and inclusion in the analysis.

quotes.quote: An intriguing example is the detection of a prominent AGN-driven outflow in the most distant AGN, GN-z11 maiolino_bh_2023 , which is observed to eject gas and metals in its Circum-Galactic Medium Maiolino23heii , while also heating and ionizing it Scholtz23 .

GN-z11 is part of the JADES sample and was observed both in the Medium/HST and Medium/JWST tiers in GOODS-N. It was specifically targeted because previously identified as a galaxy at z 11 based on HST and ground-based observations Oesch16 . The first spectrum was obtained by (within Medium/HST), and additional MSA (Medium/JWST) and IFS observations were obtained by , and , which identified it as a type 1 AGN (specifically an analogue of NLSy1). Although specifically observed because its previously known properties, it is part of the JADES sample and it is included in the analysis of this paper.

In Tab. tab:inferred_properties we also include GN-z11, whose properties were inferred in and . As mentioned, GN-z11 will be included in the rest of the analysis, as part of the JADES sample in GOODS-N (Medium-HST and Medium-JWST tiers).

sources.summary: The paper compares GN-z11 with Trinity predictions, finding its SMBH mass, luminosity, Eddington ratio, growth history, and rarity broadly consistent but observationally constraining.

quotes.quote: As of the writing of this paper, JWST has found at least two AGN candidates above z 8 , i.e., CEERS1019 at z=8.7 Larson2023 and GN-z11 at z=10.6 Maiolino2023 . CEERS1019 has an estimated SMBH mass M_ 10^7 M_ , and an Eddington ratio ( ) of 1.2 , whereas GN-z11 is estimated to be a M_ 10^6 M_ SMBH accreting at an Eddington ratio of 5 .

Given that both GN-z11 and CEERS1019 have bolometric luminosities of 10^ 45 erg/s, their masses ( M_ 6.2 and 6.94 ) and Eddington ratios ( _ rad 5.5 and 1.3 ) are consistent with Trinity 's predictions (shown in the solid and dashed vertical lines). We also note that in Trinity , z 10 SMBHs grow at slightly below the Eddington rate, when averaged over both their active and dormant phases. Consequently, although GN-z11 is estimated to be accreting at a super-Eddington rate, such fast accretion phases are likely intermittent.

However, it is still essential to account for AGN contributions when characterizing host galaxies of confirmed AGNs like GN-z11 and CEERS 1019. It also implies that GN-z11 and CEERS 1019 are rare cases where low-mass galaxies do host bright AGNs, which might be in tension with observations given the survey areas of JADES and CEERS (see also Maiolino2023b for an estimate of AGN fraction).

sources.summary: The paper interprets GN-z11 as a nitrogen-rich young proto-galaxy whose unusual N/O, compactness, and possible massive black hole can be explained by second-generation star formation.

quotes.quote: We explore the possibility that the N-rich young proto-galaxy GN-z11 recently observed at z=10.6 by the James Webb Space Telescope is the result of the formation of second generation stars from pristine gas and Asymptotic Giant Branch (AGB) ejecta in a massive globular cluster or nuclear star cluster. We show that a second generation forming out of gas polluted by the ejecta of massive AGB stars and mixed with gas having a standard composition accounts for the unusually large N/O in the GN-z11 spectrum. The timing of the evolution of massive (4--7.5 ) AGBs also provides a favourable environment for the growth of a central stellar mass black hole to the Active Galactic Nucleus stage observed in GN-z11. According to our model the progenitor system was born at an age of the Universe of 260 - 380 Myr, well within the pre-reionization epoch.

The recent observations of GN-z11 are opening an unexpected window on our knowledge of the first phases of evolution of the Universe. The precise redshift measurement of z=10.603 from JWST/NIRSpec bunker2023 dates it at 430 Myr in the CDM model, based on the determination of cosmological parameters. In the JADES NIRCam imaging tacchella2023 , this object shows an intrinsic half-light radius of only 0.0016 0.005 " (64 20 pc), suggesting that it may host a possible Globular Cluster (GC) in formation senchyna2023, belokurov2023 . Such a low age poses a puzzle, as the spectrum reveals a very high nitrogen abundance, not seen at similar metallicity in the nearby Universe e.g. izotov2012, izotov2023 , nor predicted by standard chemical evolution models e.g. vincenzo2016 , while a high [ N/O ] is consistent with the abundances in the ``anomalous'' Globular Cluster stars, suggesting that we may be witnessing the formation of their ``second generation" charbonnel2023, renzini2023, marques2023 .

sources.summary: The paper tests whether simulations can reproduce GN-z11 as a z=10.6 star-forming AGN with a super-Eddington massive black hole, finding the observed accretion difficult to explain.

quotes.quote: The recent claim of a vigorously accreting MBH in GN-z11, a well-known star forming galaxy at z=10.6 maiolino2023 , goes in such a direction. GN-z11 was first identified by bouwens2010 and oesch2016 , and recently followed up in the JWST Advanced Deep Extragalactic Survey JADES, eisenstein2023 by means of NIRSpec spectroscopy bunker2023 and NIRCam imaging tacchella2023 . These observations suggest that GN-z11 is an Active Galactic Nuclei( AGN), with several evidences in favour of this interpretation maiolino2023 : (i) high ionization transitions (e.g. [ NeIV ] 2422,2244 ), which are commonly observed in AGNs terao2022,lefevre2019 and are considered as tracers of AGN activity since they require high photon energies ( E_ > 63.5 \ eV ) that are not easily produced by stars; (ii) NIII ] multiplet, indicating gas densities ( > 10^ 10 \ cm^ 3 ) typically found in the Broad Line Regions (BLRs) of AGN; (iii) a very deep equivalent width ( EW_ rest 5 ) and blueshifted absorption trough of CIV doublet, suggesting the presence of outflows with velocities ( 800-1000 \ km \ s^ 1 ) commonly observed in mini-Broad Absorption Line (BAL) quasars rodriguez2009phd ; (iv) clear detection of MgII doublet hinting at the presence of an accreting MBH with mass 1.2 10^6 \ M _ and a surprisingly large Eddington ratio ( _ Edd =L_ bol /L_ Edd 5.5 2 ).

We find one BH-galaxy system in our simulated volume that can be representative of GN-z11. The BH, seeded at z 16.1 , has a mass of =1.4 10^6 M _ at z=10.6 , fairly consistent with the BH mass of GN-z11; however, it is accreting at _ Edd =0.6 , that is smaller than what is suggested for GN-z11. We further report in Table table:properties_host_galaxy_&_Gnz11 the properties of its host galaxy at z=10 , namely at the closest snapshot in our simulation to the GN-z11 redshift. The galaxy properties are consistent (within 1.6- ) with those of GN-z11, both in terms of stellar mass ( M_* ) and star formation rate (SFR), as computed by tacchella2023 .

sources.summary: The paper treats GN-z11 as a nitrogen-enhanced z=10.6 galaxy whose N/O and C/O abundances can be reproduced by enrichment from extremely massive Pop III stars.

quotes.quote: The observation of nitrogen-enhanced high- z galaxies, particularly in GN-z11 Oesch2016 and CEERS-1019 Finkelstein2017 , has sparked a revolution in the study of the Universe's earliest star and galaxy formation. GN-z11, located at a redshift of z = 10.6 Bunker2023 , exhibits a N/O abundance ratio in its interstellar medium (ISM) that is more than four times the solar value ( ( N/O ) > 0.25 ) Cameron2023, Senchyna2023 . This ratio is not only significantly higher than what is typically found in low-redshift galaxies and HII regions with a comparable metallicity ( 12+ ( O/H ) < 8.0 ) but it also slightly exceeds the values observed in galaxies with super-solar metallicities Vincenzo2016, Berg2019 .

Our results demonstrate that the first generation of Pop III stars within a mass range of 2000 to 9000 M _ result in N/O, C/O and O/H ratios compatible with the values observed in very high-z galaxies GN-z11 and CEERS 1019. The ejecta of these Pop III stars are predominantly composed of ^4 He, ^1 H, and ^ 14 N.

A sub-population of very massive PopIII stars produces an abundance pattern of nitrogen which is consistent with current observations by JWST of GN-z11 and CEERS 1019. The high nitrogen abundances are driven solely by the most massive stars among that population.

sources.summary: GN-z11 is analyzed as a luminous z=10.60 galaxy whose JWST/NIRSpec gas emission is spatially extended, velocity-structured, and best modeled as a compact, rotation-dominated disk.

quotes.quote: We investigate the dynamics of GN-z11, a luminous galaxy at z=10.60 , carefully analyzing the public deep integral field spectroscopy (IFS) data taken with JWST NIRSpec IFU. While the observations of the IFS data originally targeted a He ii clump near GN-z11, we find that 1907,1909 emission from ionized gas at GN-z11 is bright and spatially extended significantly beyond the point-spread function (PSF) in contrast with the GN-z11's compact UV-continuum morphology. The spatially extended \ emission of GN-z11 shows a velocity gradient, red- and blue-shifted components in the NE and SW directions, respectively, which cannot be explained by the variation of [ 1907/ 1909 line ratios. We perform forward modeling with GalPak ^ 3D , including the effects of PSF smearing and line blending, and find that the best-fit model is a nearly edge-on disk with a rotation velocity of v_ rot = 376_ 151 ^ +110 km s ^ 1 , a velocity dispersion of _v=113_ 48 ^ +24 km s ^ 1 , and a ratio of v_ rot / _v=3.3_ 1.5 ^ +1.7 , indicative of a rotation-dominated disk at z=10.6 .

Interestingly, the disk rotation velocity is faster than the circular velocity at the virial radius, v (r_ 200 )=205_ 34 ^ +28 , estimated from the stellar mass, suggesting a compact disk produced under weak feedback such predicted in numerical simulations. In fact, the half-light radius of the \ emitting gas disk is only 294 45 pc, while the one of the stellar UV component is even smaller, 196 12 pc. While higher S/N data are necessary for a conclusion, these observational results would suggest that GN-z11 has a fast-rotating gaseous disk whose center possesses luminous stellar components or AGN providing weak feedback.

sources.summary: The paper models GN-z11 as an exceptionally bright z=11.09 galaxy whose simulated analogues match observations and show rapid, sustained mass growth rather than transient merger brightening.

quotes.quote: Using Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) Wide Field Camera 3 (WFC3) grism spectroscopy, Oesch2016 recently identified the most distant galaxy known to date (GN-z11). The spectrum, combined with photometric data from the Cosmic Assembly Near-infrared Deep Extragalactic Legacy Survey (CANDELS) survey, placed the system at z =11.09 ^ +0.08 _ 0.12 , with lower redshift spectral energy distribution (SED) solutions excluded with high confidence. At M_ UV = 22.1 0.2 , GN-z11 is approximately three times more luminous than L^* at z =7--8, and is therefore extremely bright for such a high- z object.

Existing in the two most massive subhaloes at z =11.1 ( M_ vir =1.4 10^ 11 M _ and 6.7 10^ 10 M _ ), our model analogues show excellent agreement with all available observationally derived properties of GN-z11. Although they are relatively rare outliers from the full galaxy population at high- z , they are no longer the most massive or brightest systems by z =5. Furthermore, we find that both objects possess relatively smooth, but extremely rapid mass growth histories with consistently high star formation rates and UV luminosities at z > 11 , indicating that their brightness is not a transient, merger-driven feature.

sources.summary: The paper interprets GN-z11-flash as a possible Pop III supernova shock breakout in the star-forming z 11 galaxy GN-z11, consistent with its observed star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: GN-z11-flash --- a rest-frame ultraviolet flash with an observed duration of a few hundred seconds --- was recently reported jiang2020 from GN-z11, a luminous, star forming galaxy situated at z 11 . We examine the scenario in which this transient originated from the shock breakout taking place in a supernova explosion during the end stages of a 300 M_ Population III (Pop III) star in the galaxy. The alternative explanation of a gamma-ray burst afterglow for GN-z11-flash is much less likely given the much lower rate of occurrence for such events jiang2020, kann2020 . The observed star formation rate jiang2020a, oesch2016 of the GN-z11 galaxy ( 26 \ M_ /yr) with a top heavy IMF (characteristic of the first stars) is consistent with 1 Pop III supernova observed every few years.

GN-z11 is a luminous galaxy at z 11 with an intrinsic star formation rate of 26 \ M_ /yr. The breakout of a shock e.g., chevalier1976, colgate1974 from a supernova associated with a Population III star in GN-z11 would be detectable in the UV to X-ray bands.

sources.summary: The paper discusses GN-z11's extreme nitrogen enrichment at z=10.6 and argues rotating Wolf-Rayet direct-collapse models can reproduce its high N/O ratio.

quotes.quote: The high N/O ratio recently reported in GN-z11 cannot be explained even by rotating PISNe, but could be reproduced by the winds of rotating Wolf Rayet stars that end up as a direct collapse.

The Origin of Rich Nitrogen Recently, 2023arXiv230207256B report bright nitrogen emission N iv ] 1486 and N iii ] 1748 in a star-forming galaxy, dubbed GN-z11, at z=10.6 with a high quality JWST/NIRSpec spectrum. and derive nitrogen-oxygen abundance ratio of [ N/O ] 0.5 , greater than four times solar.

GN-z11 shows a high [ N/O ] ratio despite being at z = 10.6 . The main candidates for nitrogen origin are WR stars and SMS stars 2023arXiv230210142C,2023arXiv230307955C . We compare the [ N/O ] values of GN-z11 with the ejecta from the rotating WR stars, the rotating PISNe, and the AGB stars.

sources.summary: GN-z11 is the brightest GOODS-N object in the sample, spectroscopically confirmed at z=10.603, fit by EAZY at z_a=11.0, and by far the brightest in M_UV.

quotes.quote: In the GOODS-N region, we find the brightest object overall in our sample (a Kron F277W aperture magnitude of 25.73 AB), GN-z11, discussed at length in tacchella2023 and spectroscopically confirmed to lie at z = 10.603 0.001 in bunker2023 . In our EAZY fit, we estimate z_a = 11.0 , which is within 2 of the spectroscopic redshift, but again, higher than the spectroscopic redshift. We further explore this difference in Section sec:speczs .

As can be expected, GN-z11 is by far the brightest source in the sample at M_ UV = -22.0 In bunker2023 , the authors calculate M_ UV = -21.5 for GN-z11 from JWST/NIRSpec spectroscopy for the source, and in tacchella2023 the authors present a value of M_ UV = -21.6 from the ForcePho fits to the NIRCam photometry to the source. Our value is higher due to the fact that we estimated M_ UV from the Kron magnitudes to the source, which includes excess flux from a ``haze'' observed near the source tacchella2023 . The Kron fluxes we use are 1.5 times brigher than what are presented in tacchella2023 , and if we scale our fluxes by this amount our value changes to M_ UV = -21.58 for GN-z11. .

sources.summary: The paper treats GN-z11 as a z=10.6 N-rich comparison galaxy whose AGN-like ionization, high N/O, low C/N, and CNO-cycle enrichment resemble CEERS 01019 and GLASS 150008.

quotes.quote: Moreover, GN-z11 at z=10.6 , photometricly identified by Oesch2016 and spectroscopically confirmed by the JWST Advanced Deep Extragalactic Survey (JADES; Bunker2023arXiv ), has a super-solar value of ( N/O) 0.5 Cameron2023btmp,Senchyna2023arXiv , which is significantly higher than those of local galaxies at a given metallicity. Given the ultra-high redshift of z=10.6 corresponding to 440 Myr after the Big Bang, nitrogen production by AGB stars is difficult to explain the N/O enhancement of GN-z11 e.g., Cameron2023btmp . Since the report, several studies have been trying to explain the high N/O ratio of GN-z11 (see Section subsec:nco_org for more details).

We derive C/N, C/O, and Ne/O ratios of GN-z11 from ] / ] Maiolino2023arXiv , ] / ] Maiolino2023arXiv,Bunker2023arXiv , and [ ] / ] Maiolino2023arXiv,Bunker2023arXiv ratios in the same way as for the N/O ratio, using the low- _ OX AGN models with 12+ ( O/H)=8.23 -8.50 and [ N/O ] =1 . The bottom right panel of Figure fig:nco describes the result of the C/N measurement as an example. We obtain a low value of [ C/N ] =(-1.43) -( 1.17 ), [ C/O ] >-0.53 , and [ Ne/O ] >-1.22 (i.e., ( C/N)=(-0.80) - (-0.54) , ( C/O)>-0.78 , and ( Ne/O)>-1.85 ).

sources.summary: The paper models GN-z11's super-solar nitrogen abundance, arguing that a dual-burst, intermittent star-formation history with Wolf-Rayet enrichment explains its observed CNO ratios.

quotes.quote: We interpret the peculiar super-solar nitrogen abundance recently reported by the James Webb Space Telescope observations for GN-z11 ( z=10.6 ) using our state-of-the-art chemical evolution models. The observed CNO ratios can be successfully reproduced -- independently of the adopted initial mass function, nucleosynthesis yields and presence of supermassive ( > 1000 M_ ) stars -- if the galaxy has undergone an intermittent star formation history with a quiescent phase lasting 100 Myr, separating two strong star bursts. Immediately after the second burst, Wolf-Rayet stars (up to 120M_ ) become the dominant enrichment source, also temporarily ( < 1 Myr) enhancing particular elements (N, F, Na, Al) and isotopes ( ^ 13 C, ^ 18 O).
Even more puzzlingly, GN-z11 shows an unusually high ( > 4 solar) N/O ratio.

We have presented, for the first time, a chemical evolution model that naturally explains the `anomalous' elemental abundance ratios of GN-z11. Without changing the IMF or the nucleosynthesis yields, the observed data can be reproduced if this galaxy has experienced star formation history with a quiescent phase lasting 100 Myr, separating two strong star bursts.

sources.summary: The paper reports deep NOEMA non-detections of GN-z11 in dust continuum and 158 micron emission, setting dust and molecular-gas limits for this z=10.603 galaxy.

quotes.quote: We present results of dust continuum and 158 m emission line observations of a remarkably UV-luminous ( M_ UV =-21.6 ) galaxy at z=10.603 : GN-z11. Using the Northern Extended Millimeter Array (NOEMA), observations have been carried out over multiple observing cycles. We achieved a high sensitivity resulting in a _ rest =160 m continuum 1 sensitivity of 13.0 Jy/beam and a \ emission line 1 sensitivity of 31 mJy/beam km/s using 50 km/s binning with a 2 arcsec synthesized beam.

Neither dust continuum nor 158 m line emission are detected at the expected frequency of _ CII = 163.791 GHz and the sky location of GN-z11. The upper limits show that GN-z11 is neither luminous in \ nor L_ CII , with a dust mass 3 limit of log (M_ dust / M_ ) < 6.5-6.9 and with a \ based molecular gas mass 3 limit of log (M_ mol, [ CII ] / M_ ) < 9.3 . Together with radiative transfer calculations, we also investigated the possible cause of the dust poor nature of the GN-z11 showed by the blue color in the UV continuum of GN-z11 ( _ UV =-2.4 ), and found that 3 deeper observations are crucial to study dust production at very high-redshift.

sources.summary: The paper discusses GN-z11's strong nitrogen enrichment and argues that ejecta from accreting 3000-solar-mass Pop III stars could help explain its high N/O abundance.

quotes.quote: The recent detection of bright NIII and NIV emission lines in GN-z11 points towards a high nitrogen enrichment in this galaxy Bunker2023 . The value of N/O (log(N/O) > -0.25 ) for GN-z11 is found to be four times higher than solar values Cameron2023,Senchyna2023 . Works by Charbonnel2023 and Nagele2023 have attempted to explain SMSs as the main polluter responsible for the high nitrogen enrichment.

The value of N/O is above the lower limit for GN-z11 reported by Cameron2023 , while the value of C/O is slightly below the lower limit. This suggests that accreting Pop III stars of 3000 could be the cause of the high N/O abundance observed in GN-z11. However, future work is required to perform a more detailed comparison with GN-z11 and any future objects that are detected by JWST.

sources.summary: The paper treats GN-z11 as a nitrogen-enhanced high-redshift galaxy whose N/O and O/H ratios can be matched by fast-rotating Population III or extremely metal-poor stellar populations.

quotes.quote: We present an analysis of the chemical compositions in high-redshift galaxies, with a focus on the nitrogen-enhanced galaxies GN-z11 and CEERS-1019. We use stellar models of massive stars with initial masses ranging from 9 to 120 M_ across various metallicities to deduce the chemical abundances of stellar ejecta for a few light elements (H, He, C, N, O). Our study reveals insights into the chemical processes and elemental synthesis in the early universe. We find that Population III stars, particularly at initial fast equatorial rotation and sampled from a top-heavy initial mass function, as well as stars at Z=10^ 5 with moderate rotation, align closely with observed abundance ratios in GN-z11 and CEERS-1019. These models demonstrate (N/O) = -0.38, (C/O) =-0.22 and (O/H) + 12 = 7.82 at dilution factors of f 20 - 100 , indicating a good match with observational data.

Stars: evolution -- Stars: Population III -- Stars: massive -- Stars: abundances The observation of nitrogen-enhanced high-redshift (high- z ) galaxies, particularly in GN-z11 Oesch2016 and CEERS-1019 Finkelstein2017 , stands as a testament to the intricacies involved in deciphering the cosmos. GN-z11, placed at an unprecedented redshift of z = 10.6 Bunker2023 , showcases a N/O abundance ratio that is not only more than four times solar Cameron2023 , but also challenges previously held assumptions of such high- z objects being nitrogen-poor. This ratio, which is elevated in comparison to galaxies at lower redshifts, and even higher than in some galaxies with super-solar metallicities, points towards peculiar chemical processes in the early universe Vincenzo2016,Berg2019 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies GN-z10-1 as the brightest high-redshift candidate, favors a z about 10 interpretation, and uses its IRAC detections to constrain its SED.

quotes.quote: Taken together, these results suggest that the most likely interpretation is that GN-z10-1 is at high redshift.
For example, GN-z10-1 (the brightest source), shows evidence for a flat continuum from the HST H_ 160 to the IRAC 3.6 m and 4.5 m bands.

The most striking feature of the SED of GN-z10-1 is that it does not show any Balmer break, having an IRAC color of [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] = -0.14 0.29 . Overall, the SED of this source is extremely flat with few features. Using a constant star-formation history and a Bruzual03 model with 0.5 Z_ , the SED is best fit with the minimum allowed age of only 10 Myr and a stellar mass of 10 ^ 8.7 0.3 .

sources.summary: The paper treats GN-z10-1 as the brightest z~10 galaxy candidate, robustly detected in Spitzer/IRAC, and derives its UV-continuum slope and inferred dust attenuation.

quotes.quote: Three of the four z 10 galaxy candidates from Oesch et al. (2014) are significantly detected ( >4.5 ) in these data in at least one filter. In particular, the brightest source GN-z10-1 with a photometric redshift of z_ phot = 10.2 0.4 is robustly detected in the 3.6 m channel at 7 , allowing for an individual estimate a UV-continuum slope for this source.

The observed values of the UV-continuum slope for the z 10 candidates (and the stack) are listed in Table tab:photometry and shown in Figure fig:M_beta . For the brightest candidate (GN-z10-1) we find _ obs =-2.1 0.2 while for the bright stack (see sec:bright_stack ) we find _ obs =-2.1 0.3 . If we stack only those sources which have JH_ 140 band observations (providing a second WFC3/IR filter where the z 10 candidates are detected), i.e., GN-z10-1 and GN-z10-2, we find _ obs =-2.2 0.3 .

For the brightest candidate, GN-z10-1 (Oesch et al. 2014), we infer a rest-frame UV attenuation of 0.9 assuming the Calzetti et al. (2000) attenuation curve ( 0.5 assuming an SMC extinction law), while for the stack of bright candidates we find 0.9 0.4 assuming the Calzetti et al. (2000) law ( 0.6 0.2 assuming an SMC extinction law).

sources.summary: The paper compares GN-z11 with simulated first galaxies, noting its observed SFR and stellar-mass implications require sustained high star formation beyond the simulated Halo-12 history.

quotes.quote: It is interesting that none of our simulated galaxies reproduce the observed SFR of GN-z11 ( = 24 ) which is the most distant galaxy observed to date Oesch15 . Halo-12 shows only SFR 1 at z 10 even in its starburst phase. In order to reproduce the high SFR, other conditions may be required, e.g., more massive halos or different halo growth histories.

Again, of Halo-12 is lower than GN-z11 by an order of magnitude at z 11 . The observed high indicates that GN-z11 kept high SFRs of 1-10 over t 10^ 8 yr , and it is unlikely to be an instantaneously bright galaxy due to major merger if the observed stellar mass is correct.

sources.summary: The paper fits GN-z11 with a spheroidal model but notes weaker redshift constraints because the data lack Balmer-break coverage; it finds a younger, less massive, dust-poorer galaxy.

quotes.quote: The case of the galaxy GN11 At the present time, the most distant galaxy is GN-z11 at z 11, Oesch2016 . The photometric redshift will only be tentative because the observational wavelength coverage does not cover the Balmer break. Fig  GN-z11 proposes the best-fit of observations where the signature of Lyman discontinuity and the attenuated continuum are well identified. Once more while the spheroidal model best-fits data on the global wavelength coverage, the redshift determination is less robust since observations are not covering the Balmer discontinuity. However the present results confirm a younger age of 160Myrs, a significantly less massive galaxy with reduced stellar mass and dust mass.

sources.summary: The paper evaluates whether GN-z11-flash was a GRB prompt ultraviolet flash associated with the ultra-high-redshift galaxy GN-z11, finding its luminosity consistent with early GRB optical emission.

quotes.quote: The recently discovered rapid transient GN-z11-flash has been suggested to be the prompt-emission ultraviolet flash associated with a gamma-ray burst serendipitously exploding in the ultra-high- z galaxy GN-z11. We here place the flash into the context of the early ultraviolet emission of gamma-ray bursts, and find it is in agreement with the luminosity distribution of these events. Recently, reported a spectroscopic redshift for the ultra-high- z candidate galaxy GN-z11 Oesch2016ApJ , finding it to lie at z=10.957 , confirming it to be the most distant object known at the time.

They exclude atmospheric, terrestrial, and Solar System sources and present evidence that the transient is very likely associated with GN-z11 and therefore at the same redshift. This implies extreme luminosity, and they therefore argue that GN-z11-flash is most likely the prompt flash of a GRB exploding while they were observing the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper includes GN-z11 in its high-redshift metallicity analysis, noting debated ionizing-source nature, direct-method oxygen abundance, and indirect estimates with large systematics.

quotes.quote: In addition, we include in the analysis four targets whose metallicity have been derived with the `direct' method, namely three galaxies observed in the framework of the Early Release Observations programme as described in , and the galaxy GN-z11 observed in the framework of JADES and presented in , though the nature of its ionising source is debated and the presence of an AGN has been proposed maiolino_gnz11_2023 .

For GN-z11, fiducial values for and SFR are taken from and are based on beagle fitting to PRISM spectrum assuming a IMF with an upper mass cut-off of 100 , whereas the oxygen abundance is measured exploiting the detection of 4363 in the PRISM spectrum and its ratio over 3869 and , as detailed in , which quote a fiducial value of 12+log(O/H) =7.82 . For comparison, we also obtain an indirect estimate of the 5007 flux (which is not covered in the spectra of GN-z11) based on the observed / \ (from medium resolution gratings) and the / \ versus / \ calibration, which we then use to derive the R diagnostic and use it conjunction with Ne3O2 and the methodology described in Section sec:metallicity : this approach provides 12+log(O/H) =7.67 +0.16 0.10 . At the same time, using the same indirect estimate of the 5007 flux to apply the method (and adopting pilyugin_electron_2009 to derive the temperature of the low-ionisation zone) delivers a much higher 12+log(O/H) =8.42 . Given the large systematic uncertainties affecting both methods, we here assume the value reported by (7.82) as our fiducial value, with a conservative uncertainty of 0.35 dex.

sources.summary: The paper presents GN-z11 as the farthest spectroscopically confirmed black-hole host, at z=10.6 with a 1.6 million solar-mass SMBH, possibly tracing heavy-seed evolution.

quotes.quote: Currently, the farthest SMBH is observed spectroscopically in GN-z11, a galaxy at z=10.6 , with a mass of 1.6 10^6 Maiolino_2023 . This discovery came shortly after the 2 detection of another quasar at z=8.679 , with a slightly heavier mass of 10^7 Larson_2023 .

The blue points represent the sources we used in this study, together with GN-z11 (in red), the farthest black hole with a spectroscopic confirmation Maiolino_2023 . While the general population of quasars stacks up on the ``southeast'' of this chart (i.e., large black holes at lower redshift), JWST allows us to reach farther in redshift and lower in mass than ever.
GN-z11 can be interpreted as the evolution at a constant Eddington accretion rate of a typical heavy seed Maiolino_2023 .

sources.summary: The paper cites GN-z11 as a z=10.6 star-forming galaxy with low metallicity and unexplained super-solar nitrogen-to-oxygen ratio, discussing globular-cluster-like chemistry and WR-dominated young-cluster yields as possible explanations.

quotes.quote: A specific case has attracted attention since its discovery by oesch16 : the star-forming galaxy GN-z11 at a redshift of 10.6 and metallicity of 12+log(O/H) 7.9 bunker23 displays a peculiar nitrogen-to-oxygen ratio cameron23 that no standard chemical evolution can explain. Several solutions have been put forward to explain the super-solar value senchyna23,charbonnel23,marques23 . The leading scenario is that GN-z11 shows the same chemistry as that of globular clusters and that the mechanism at work is similar in both types of objects. marques23 studied alternative possibilities and argued that, when WR stars dominate, a young cluster at a well-defined age can also reproduce the observed abundances. However, these authors do not favour this solution because the predicted yields from such a stellar population only match the observed constraints for a narrow age range.

sources.summary: The paper uses GN-z11 as evidence for early chemical enrichment, discussing its high spectroscopic redshift, nitrogen excess, and possible very massive or supermassive stellar origins.

quotes.quote: A remarkable [ N/O ] +0.6 enrichment cameron23 is seen in GN-z11 bunker23 , with one of the highest confirmed spectroscopic redshifts for any galaxy. One possible source could be very massive stars with initial masses of hundreds of solar masses vink23 , associated with a top-heavy IMF bekki23 . An alternative could be supermassive stars marques23 , with initial masses of thousands of solar masses, or direct-collapse black-hole precursors with masses possibly reaching 10^5-10^6 M_ . Either option requires rapid early star formation in massive central star clusters belokurov23 , possibly in intermittent episodes kobayashi23 . Similar episodes are predicted in our boosted nuclear star formation model.

sources.summary: The paper describes GN-z11 as the most distant black-hole candidate considered, with marginal ngVLA detectability and host or supernova radio emission affecting bands.

quotes.quote: GN-z11, the most distant BH candidate observed to date at z =10.6 , has a 1.5 10^6 \ BH that can be marginally detected by ngVLA with 100 hr integration times but only with longer times by SKA. Radio emission from its H II regions becomes equal to the flux from some FPs due to the 25 \ yr ^ 1 SFR in the host galaxy. Below 3 GHz, flux from SNe dominates emission from the BH.

We find that almost all the sources can be detected with ngVLA with 10 - 100 hr integration times except GN-z11, which may require longer times. The AGN flux densities for = 0.7 are about a factor of 2 - 3 higher at 0.1 GHz and 2 - 3 times lower at 10 GHz in comparison to = 0.3. BH fluxes for = 0.7 in general are lower in the sensitivity range of ngVLA (1-10 GHz) so longer integration times of 10 -100 hr would be required to observe them depending on the BH mass and redshift except for GN-z11 and J-10013704, which still require more than 100 hr integration times.

sources.summary: The paper reports a JWST/NIRSpec UV continuum excess in the z=10.603 AGN-host galaxy GN-z11, finding it resembles but cannot be fit by a Balmer continuum alone.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of continuum excess in the rest-frame UV between 3000 \ and 3550 \ in the JWST/NIRSpec spectrum of GN-z11, a galaxy hosting an active galactic nucleus (AGN) at z = 10.603 . The shape of the continuum excess resembles a Balmer continuum but has a break around 3546 \ in the rest frame, which is 100 \ bluewards to the Balmer limit at 3646 . A Balmer continuum model alone cannot fit the spectrum, implying a different origin for the continuum excess. The absence of the Balmer jump indicates an electron temperature of 3 10^4 K, which is significantly higher than the temperature of T_ e ( O^ 2+ ) 1.3 10^ 4 K inferred from 4363 .

However, in this manuscript we report the discovery of a significantly detected UV continuum excess in the JWST/NIRSpec spectrum of GN-z11, a luminous galaxy hosting an AGN z = 10.603 oesch2016,bunker2023,tacchella2023,maiolino2023 , which partly resembles the shape of the small blue bumps previously observed in lower-redshift AGNs.

sources.summary: The paper treats GN-z11 as the only spectroscopically confirmed z>10 galaxy, using its z=11.1 confirmation, stellar mass, and rarity to discuss high-redshift selection purity.

quotes.quote: The only spectroscopically confirmed galaxy at z>10 is GN-z11, which was discovered photometrically with the Hubble Space Telescope Oesch2014 and confirmed to be at z 11.1 with a grism measurement of the continuum break at the location of the Lyman- line Oesch2016 . This was the brightest of six objects identified in the photometric selection as laying at z 9 -12; the other candidates have not been confirmed to be at such high redshifts. The measurements also suggested M_ 10^9 \ M_ . According to Figure fig:interloper-fraction , N_t > N_i only if f_i 1 -10 (assuming _i=10 ), depending on the galaxy's f_ .

sources.summary: The paper compares GN-z11 to Sunburst as a z~11 galaxy with strong nitrogen lines, dense photoionized gas, high nebular nitrogen abundance, and a possible high-mass enrichment analogue.

quotes.quote: Shortly after the completion of this work, four pre-prints have appeared and have examined remarkably strong nitrogen emission lines seen in GN-z11, a z 11 galaxy confirmed through JADES NIRSpec IFU spectroscopy Bunker2023gnz11,Cameron2023gnz11,Senchyna2023gnz11,Charbonnel2023gnz11 . Using Cloudy photoionization calculations, showcase a similarly high density n_e 10^5 cm ^ 3 for photoionized gas, and a high nebular N abundance ( N/O ) 0.25 at a gas metallicity of Z 0.1 Z_ . This object may be a high-redshift, high-mass (stellar mass 10^8 - 10^9 M_ ) version of the enrichment processes seen in Sunburst. nitrogen shows the inferred LyC cluster nitrogen abundance compared to GN-z11, as well as to individual chemically anomalous stars in a local globular cluster, local star forming galaxies, and local HII regions.

sources.summary: The paper treats GN-z11 as a z=10.6 luminous galaxy hosting an AGN, using its inferred black-hole mass and abundance as constraints on early black-hole growth.

quotes.quote: Maiolino_2023 recently reported the NIRSpec observation of GN-z11, an exceptionally luminous galaxy at z=10.6 , revealing the high ionization [ Ne iv ] 2423 transition and semi-forbidden nebular lines tracing clouds of broad-line regions of an AGN. The Mg ii based virial BH mass is estimated as 1.6 10^6 .

In the early evolution of low-mass BHs, our model presents a mass density at z 11 that is consistent with the observations of GN-z11, as inferred from its cosmic volume density and BH mass, 4.0^ +21.3 _ 3.6 ^ 3 (star symbol), considering the uncertainty in its mass and abundance measurement Maiolino_2023,Oesch_2016 . Intriguingly, even sophisticated cosmological simulations fail to attain this value, which likely points to their insufficient treatments of BH seeding dashed-dotted curves; Ni2022 .

sources.summary: The paper uses GN-z11 as the pre-JWST benchmark luminous z~11 galaxy, noting its NIRSpec redshift z=10.60, SFR, stellar mass, possible IGM damping wing, candidate accreting black hole, halo, and companions.

quotes.quote: Nevertheless, Hubble 's deepest surveys led to the discovery of GN-z11 skelton14a,oesch14a,oesch16a , then a z 11 candidate that was not only the most distant galaxy candidate identified before JWST's launch, but also one of the most luminous, with an observed rest-frame UV magnitude of 21.5 . Selected from 0.2 deg ^2 of aggregate deep Hubble imaging, its implied volume density was rather rare but difficult to constrain with a single source. We now know GN-z11 to be at z=10.60 thanks to JWST NIRSpec observations bunker23a with a star formation rate 20 yr ^ 1 and stellar mass 10 ^ 9 \ tacchella23a -- all well in place within the first 400 Myr after the Big Bang. Beyond its extraordinary brightness, further JWST observations of GN-z11 reveal even more surprises: it exhibits \ in emission, has a possible damping wing of the intergalactic medium observed as a Lorentzian absorption profile in \ and previously only seen as a signature of neutral IGM absorption in quasars, miralda-escude98a , as well as signatures of a candidate accreting supermassive black hole, \ halo, and possible nearby companions scholtz23a .

sources.summary: The review describes GN-z11 as a former highest-redshift galaxy, with Lyman-break and JWST emission-line redshift measurements placing it about 400 Myr after the Big Bang.

quotes.quote: A proclaimed goal of JWST is to look for some of the very first galaxies, and it was therefore expected that we would eventually find galaxies with redshifts higher than the previous ``record-holder'', GN-z11, which has a redshift of z 11 Oesch2016 Oesch2016 measured GN-z11's redshift from the Lyman break (see info box info:lyabreak ) to 11.09_ 0.12 ^ +0.08 . A very recent study by Bunker2023 report the detection of several emission lines with JWST, deducing a redshift of 10.6034 0.0013 . and is hence seen some 400 Myr after the Big Bang. Finding the highest-redshift galaxy is not just a matter of breaking a record.

sources.summary: The paper uses GN-z11 as a bursty star-forming high-redshift test case, giving its mass, halo mass, metallicity, and burst duration to show supernovae should not quench it.

quotes.quote: Let us consider the case of GN-z11, the most UV-luminous galaxy at z=10.6 known to date Oesch16gn11, Bunker23gnz11 . It has a stellar mass of M_ = 5.4 10^8 , a halo mass of M_h 3 10^ 10 Scholtz23gnz11 and gas metallicity of (Z/ )=-0.92 . Its SFH can be approximated with a SFR 20 burst lasting t_b 30 Myr. These values are below the SFR_ min threshold, confirming that SNe are not expected to quench the galaxy as expected.

sources.summary: The paper uses GN-z11 as a high-redshift nitrogen-emitting galaxy whose elevated nitrogen abundance could be explained by winds from very massive stars.

quotes.quote: Recent studies of high-redshift galaxies with James Webb Space Telescope (JWST), such as GN-z11 at z=10.6, show unexpectedly significant amounts of nitrogen (N) in their spectra. One of the surprises gleaned from galaxies such as GN-z11 (Bunker et al. 2023) involves the discovery of large amounts of nitrogen (N), with elevated [ N/O ] ratios (Cameron et al 2023; Senchyna et al. 2023). In other words, VMSs may indeed produce the large amount of nitrogen in the Sunburst Arc at Cosmic noon and they may also be the main source of N emission in galaxies such as GN-z11 at high redshift.

For the high-redshift galaxy GN-z11, numbers of order of 0.1 Z_ have been discussed (Cameron et al. 2023) and this is still within the high Z regime of Sabhahit et al. (2023), where the dependence dominates over the Z dependence.

sources.summary: The paper remeasures GN-z11's compact size with forcepho, finding it smaller than prior galfit estimates and consistent with JWST results suggesting its luminosity may be AGN-driven.

quotes.quote: In summary, we performed a new size-measurement for GN-z11, the highest redshift galaxy spectroscopically confirmed by HST, using the Bayesian fitting software forcepho . Our physical size of 0.15 0.1 kpc is much smaller than previous measurements with galfit , but consistent with measurements based on newer JWST/NIRCam data https://doi.org/10.48550/arxiv.2302.07234 . The size of GN-z11, along with its redshift and luminosity, present interesting possibilities for the physical processes at play in such an early galaxy bunker2023jades,https://doi.org/10.48550/arxiv.2302.07234 . Our measurement of a very compact size in GN-z11 is consistent with WST/NIRSpec observations maiolino2023small suggesting that at least some of the remarkable luminosity of this object may be driven by an AGN.

sources.summary: The paper compares GN-z11 with BRAHMA black-hole seed predictions, noting its z=10.6 AGN, roughly local stellar-to-black-hole mass ratio, and possible mismatch with the explored seed parameters.

quotes.quote: The pre-JWST highest redshift GN-z11 galaxy has now acquired a new title for hosting the highest redshift AGN 2023arXiv230512492M at z 10.6 . With an estimated BH mass of 10^6 M_ in a 10^9 M_ galaxy, its stellar to BH mass ratio is comparable to the local scaling relations (black square in Figure SM_BHM_fig , top row). Therefore, its BH mass is significantly below simple extrapolations of our simulated z=11 M_ bh M_* relations for all the seed models. This suggests that the seeding mechanism for the GN-z11 BH may not be encompassed by our explored set of seed parameters.

sources.summary: The paper discusses GN-z11 as a z=10.6 nitrogen-enriched galaxy whose high N/O and nitrogen emission may be reproduced by stellar-wind enrichment scenarios.

quotes.quote: For instance, GN-z11, that is at z=10.6 2023A&A...677A..88B , has a high N/O abundance ratio 2023MNRAS.523.3516C, 2023arXiv230304179S .

In particular, WRs can be nitrogen sources for producing the observed abundance of GN-z11 2023MNRAS.523.3516C . 2024ApJ...962L...6K showed that metal enrichment of WR stars can reproduce the observed abundance ratio of GN-z11, with two starbursts in a time interval of 100 M_ .
GN-z11 shows the strong nitrogen emission 2023A&A...677A..88B and the very high nitrogen-oxygen abundance ratio N/O 0.5 2023MNRAS.523.3516C, 2023arXiv230304179S .

sources.summary: The paper cites GN-z11 as a high-redshift galaxy with strong nitrogen and carbon lines implying rapid supersolar nitrogen enrichment, with WR stars suggested as a possible nitrogen source.

quotes.quote: For instance, deep rest-frame FUV observations of the galaxy GN-z11 find strong N 4 ] 1486, N 3 ] 1750, and C 3 ] 1907,1909 emission lines that suggest that the galaxy has been enriched to super solar nitrogen abundances just a few 100 Myr after the Big Bang e.g., maiolino23,charbonnel23,senchyna24 . However, the nitrogen and carbon lines observed within GN-z11 are rarely detected in local star-forming galaxies, such that a complete understanding of the production and origin of these features has not fully been revealed.

Second, there has been heightened interest in N 4 ] due to its strong detection in the JWST /NIRSpec spectrum of the exceptionally bright z=10.6 galaxy GN-z11 bunker23 . As a result, WR stars have been suggested as a source of increased N emission for GN-z11 and other galaxies e.g., senchyna24 .

sources.summary: The paper compares GN-z11 with model size-luminosity predictions, noting its z 11 luminosity, measured size, and agreement with modeled stellar mass, star formation, and UV properties.

quotes.quote: Recently, 2016ApJ...819..129O found an unexpectedly luminous galaxy (GN-z11) at z 11 , which has M_ UV = 22.1 0.2 and R_ e =0.6 0.3 kpc. In 2016MNRAS.463.3556M we demonstrated that the properties of GN-z11 are in good agreement with the results of our model in terms of stellar mass, star formation rate and UV luminosities. We show the measured size of GN-z11 in Fig. fig:re-muv and we find that it is in agreement with our fitted size--luminosity relation at z 10 .

sources.summary: The paper uses GN-z11 to illustrate cosmology-dependent ages and distances for high-redshift galaxies, contrasting LCDM and early-dark-energy lookback times.

quotes.quote: To explore this point further, consider the reported detection of emission lines corresponding to redshift z=10.957 0.001 in GN-z11 jiang2021 , a source originally detected photometrically with the Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ). This would make GN-z11 the highest redshift galaxy detected to date, and it is a pre-reionization-era object according to the definition adopted here. The redshift of GN-z11 (plotted as a gray line in the left panel of Fig. fig:t_vs_z ) corresponds to =13.39 (cosmic time of 416 Myr, and a radial comoving distance of d=9.83 Gpc ) in the cosmology as opposed to =12.81 (cosmic time of 402 Myr, radial comoving distance of d=9.44 Gpc ) in the EDE cosmology.

sources.summary: The review identifies GN-z11 as the most distant known spectroscopically confirmed galaxy, noting its WFC3/IR discovery and redshift measurements from grism and MOSFIRE spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: The most distant known, spectroscopically-confirmed object is the GOODS-N galaxy GN-z11 originally identified using WFC3/IR imaging. oesch2016a used grism spectroscopy to measure a continuum spectral break for GN-z11 corresponding to redshift z=11.09^ +0.08 _ 0.12 . Recently, using /MOSFIRE confirmed the redshift using three restframe-UV emission lines, finding a redshift of z=10.957 if the lines correspond to the CIII ] doublet and OIII ] .

gmw_00001071.name: SDSS J231038.88+185519.7
gmw_00001071.overview: SDSS J231038.88+185519.7 is a highly luminous broad-absorption-line quasar at a redshift of approximately 6.0 [arXiv:1302.4154, arXiv:2207.03314]. The host galaxy features a rotationally supported disk with a central gaseous outflow [arXiv:2207.03314], a compact stellar bulge [arXiv:2301.07023], and highly excited molecular gas [arXiv:2007.12339].

sources.summary: The paper presents J2310+1855 as a z=6 broad-absorption-line quasar host with strong dust, CO, and [C II] detections, resolved source size, and a velocity gradient.

quotes.quote: The quasar SDSS J231038.88+185519.7 (hereafter J2310+1855) is a broad absorption line quasar discovered in the SDSS (Fan et al. 2013, in prep.). The redshift measured with the quasar rest-frame UV line emission is z=6.00 0.03. It is one of the optically brightest sources among the known z 6 quasars with rest frame 1450 magnitude of m_ 1450 =19.3 .

This object is the brightest detection among all the z 6 quasars from our MAMBO observations. The 250 GHz continuum flux density is 8.29 0.63 mJy . Our PdBI observation of the CO(6-5) line emission shows a line flux of 1.52 0.13 Jy km s^ 1 , which is also by far the strongest CO line flux. The redshift and FWHM measured with the CO (6-5) line are z_ CO =6.0025 0.0007 and FWHM_ CO =456 64 km s^ 1 . The dust continuum at 99 GHz has also been detected in the line-free channels with a flux density of 0.40 0.05 mJy (Figure 1).

sources.summary: The paper resolves molecular gas in the z~6 QSO host, measuring compact CO and dust sizes, gas and dynamical masses, turbulent/merger-like kinematics, and comparable black-hole and host growth.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA observations of the CO(6-5) and [ CII ] emission lines and the sub-millimeter continuum of the z 6 quasi-stellar object (QSO) SDSS J231038.88+185519.7. Compared to previous studies, we have analyzed a synthetic beam that is ten times smaller in angular size, we have achieved ten times better sensitivity in the CO(6-5) line, and two and half times better sensitivity in the [ CII ] line, enabling us to resolve the molecular gas emission. We obtain a size of the dense molecular gas of 2.9 0.5 kpc, and of 1.4 0.2 kpc for the 91.5 GHz dust continuum. By assuming that CO(6-5) is thermalized, and by adopting a CO--to-- H_2 conversion factor _ CO = 0.8  M_ K^ 1   (km/s)^ 1  pc^ 2 , we infer a molecular gas mass of M(H_2)=(3.2 0.2) 10^ 10 M_ .

We derive a ratio v_ rot / 1-2 suggesting high gas turbulence, outflows/inflows and/or complex kinematics due to a merger event. We estimate a global Toomre parameter Q 0.2-0.5 , indicating likely cloud fragmentation. We compare, at the same angular resolution, the CO(6-5) and [ CII ] distributions, finding that dense molecular gas is more centrally concentrated with respect to [ CII ] . We find that the current BH growth rate is similar to that of its host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper studies SDSS J231038.88+185519.7/J2310+1855 with ALMA fine-structure lines, describing a FIR-bright z=6 quasar host whose multiphase ISM shows lower ionized-gas fraction than other systems and metallicity around 1.5-2.1 solar.

quotes.quote: In this work, we present an ISM study of the z=6.003 quasar SDSS J231038.88+185519.7 (hereafter J2310+1855). Being one of the FIR brightest quasars at z 6 , J2310+1855 has been widely studied in lines probing different phases of the ISM, e.g., emission from the neutral gas; the CO, H_ 2 O , and OH^ + lines from the molecular material; and the line from the ionized gas ( wang13 ; hashimoto18 ; shao19 ; li20 ). In a previous paper li20 , we report a study of the CO spectral line energy distribution (SLED) of this object. We detected highly excited molecular gas with complex excitation mechanisms in which heating from the powerful quasar could be involved. In this work, we report new ALMA observations of and FS lines in J2310+1855, to further probe the impact of the AGN and as well as the star formation from its host galaxy, on the multiphase ISM of this quasar.

The / ratio is lower than that of other local and high- z systems, indicating a smaller ionized gas fraction in this quasar. The / ratio is comparable to that of local systems, and suggests a metallicity of Z/Z_ =1.5 2.1. Based on the detection, we estimate that 17 of the emission is associated with ionized gas.

sources.summary: The paper studies the z 6 QSO J2310+1855 with ALMA, finding a luminous isolated host with extended dust and [CII], a rotating unstable disk, and a central gaseous outflow.

quotes.quote: We present new high-resolution ALMA observation of the [ CII ] , the H _2 O emission lines, and the sub-millimeter (sub-mm) continuum of QSO SDSS J231038.88+185519.7 (hereafter J2310+1855 or J2310). J2310, first discovered in SDSS jiang2006,wang2013 , is one of the most FIR-luminous QSOs and one of the brightest optical QSOs known at z 6 , with L_ bol =9.3 10^ 13 \ L_ . The redshift measured with the QSO rest-frame UV line emission is z = 6.00 0.03 wang2013 .

The dust continuum and [ CII ] surface brightness profiles are spatially extended out to 6.7 kpc and 5 kpc, respectively, with half-light radii of 0.9 and 1.1 kpc for the dust and gas, respectively. The derived gas surface density, _ gas , and star formation rate density, _ SFR , place the J2310+1855 host galaxy above the Kennicutt-Schmidt relation. We derived a best estimate of the dynamical mass M_ dyn = 5.2 10^ 10 \ M_ within = 1.7 kpc based on a dynamical model of the system with a rotating disk inclined at i = 25 deg.

The picture that finally arises is that of an isolated QSO, without evidence of ongoing mergers, that is characterised by a rotationally supported disk with a Toomre parameter Q_ gas 3 out to a radius of 1.5 kpc. The gas kinematics shows evidence of a gaseous outflow within the central kpc, as also supported by the flat rotation curve and the rather high velocity dispersion at the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper dynamically analyzes this z 6 quasar host, finding that its rotation curve requires a compact stellar bulge in addition to the SMBH and implies asynchronous BH-galaxy growth.

quotes.quote: Fermi 5, 50125, Firenze, Italy Institute of Astronomy, University of Cambridge, Madingley Road, Cambridge CB3 0HA, UK Kavli Institute for Cosmology, University of Cambridge, Madingley Road, Cambridge CB3 0HA, UK Department of Physics and Astronomy, University College London, Gower Street, London WC1E 6BT, UK We present a dynamical analysis of a quasar-host galaxy at z 6 (SDSS J2310+1855) using a high-resolution ALMA observation of the [ CII ] emission line. The observed rotation curve was fitted with mass models that considered the gravitational contribution of a thick gas disc, a thick star-forming stellar disc, and a central mass concentration, which is likely due to a combination of a spheroidal component (i.e. a stellar bulge) and a supermassive black hole (SMBH). The SMBH mass of 5 10^9 \ M_ , previously measured using the CIV and MgII emission lines, is not sufficient to explain the high velocities in the central regions.

Our dynamical model suggests the presence of a stellar bulge with a mass of M_ bulge 10^ 10 in this object, when the Universe was younger than 1 Gyr. To finally be located on the local M_ SMBH M_ bulge relation, the bulge mass should increase by a factor of 40 from z=6 to 0, while the SMBH mass should grow by a factor of 4 at most. This points towards asynchronous galaxy-BH co-evolution.

sources.summary: The paper describes J2310+1855 as a FIR-luminous z~6 QSO with resolved CO, [CII], dust continuum, large gas and dust masses, and a massive black hole.

quotes.quote: QSO SDSS J231038.88+185519.7 (hereafter J2310+1855), first discovered in SDSS jiang2006,wang2013 , is one of the most FIR-luminous QSOs and one of the brightest optical QSOs known at z 6 , with L_ bol =9.3 10^ 13 \ L_ . The redshift measured with the QSO rest-frame UV line emission is z = 6.00 0.03 wang2013 . feruglio2018 detected and analysed the CO(6-5) and [ CII ] emission lines and the sub-millimetre continuum of J2310+1855, deriving a size of the dense molecular gas of 2.9 0.5 kpc and of 1.4 0.2 kpc for the 91.5 GHz dust continuum and a molecular gas mass of M( H _2)=(3.2 0.2) 10^ 10 M_ . They estimated a dynamical mass of M_ dyn = (4.1^ +9.5 _ 0.5 ) 10^ 10 M_ , measuring a disk inclination of i 50 deg. They also inferred the BH mass from the CIV emission line, measured in the X-shooter/VLT spectrum of the QSO, obtaining M_ BH =(1.8 0.5) 10^9 M_ .

sources.summary: The appendix says this quasar has unusually broad lines, possible absorption or throughput effects at a line edge, and needed simplified line and continuum modeling.

quotes.quote: SDSS J2310+1855 The line and the line are unusually broad in this spectrum. In addition, the blue edge of the line is either affected by the declining throughput at the blue edge of the spectrum or by absorption. Therefore, we decided to model the line with only a single Gaussian component and exclude the complex from the fit. To properly fit the continuum over the entire wavelength range, it was necessary to disable the fit weights for the continuum model.

gmw_00001072.name: J2114+820
gmw_00001072.overview: J2114+820 is a low-power radio galaxy with a bright elliptical optical counterpart that exhibits broad Balmer emission lines [arXiv:astro-ph/9812254]. Its radio structure consists of a core, a jet directed to the north-west, and two extended S-shaped lobes [arXiv:astro-ph/9812254].

sources.summary: The paper describes J2114+820 as a low-power broad-line radio galaxy with a core, jet, S-shaped lobes, bright elliptical optical counterpart, and broad Balmer emission.

quotes.quote: In the frame of the study of a new sample of large angular size radio galaxies selected from the NRAO VLA Sky Survey, we have made radio observations of J2114+820, a low power radio galaxy with an angular size of 6'. Its radio structure basically consists of a prominent core, a jet directed in north-west direction and two extended S-shaped lobes. We have also observed the optical counterpart of J2114+820, a bright elliptical galaxy with a strong unresolved central component. The optical spectrum shows broad emission lines. This fact, together with its low radio power and FR-I type morphology, renders J2114+820 a non-trivial object from the point of view of the current unification schemes of radio loud active galactic nuclei.

J2114+820 exhibits strong broad Balmer lines, but not only in the core region, but also at distances up to 4 Kpc from the center, indicating the existence of high velocity gas over large extensions of the galaxy. J2114+820 can be classified as a low power FR-I radio galaxy. According to current unification schemes of radio loud AGN's urry , FR-I radio sources constitute the parent population of Bl-Lac type objects, which are characterized by the absence of broad emission lines (at most, only weak broad lines have occasionally been observed in Bl-Lac type objects vermeulen ).

sources.summary: The paper includes S5 2116+81 as a radio-loud AGN with uncertain nature, Swift/XRT data, required absorption, no detected Fe K line, and low-quality constraints.

quotes.quote: These double-lobed objects are classified in the literature as FR II galaxies, with the exception of 4C 74.26 and S5 2116+81, which still have an uncertain/unknown nature. The sources in our sample have all been observed by XMM-Newton with the exceptions of QSO B0241+62, for which Chandra data are available, and IGR J13109-5552 and S5 2116+81, for which Swift/XRT data have instead been used. In all but 2 AGN (B3 0309+411 and 3C 390.3), an intrinsic absorption component is strongly required by the data, at more than 90 confidence level for IGR J13109-5552 and S5 2116+81 and at more than 99 confidence level for the remaining objects.

The Fe K line is detected in all the sources (99 confidence level), except in IGR J13109-5552 and S5 2116+81, but this may be due to XRT not being sensitive enough to detect the cold iron line around 6.4 keV. Not much can be said about S5 2116+81 because of the poor statistical quality of our data; however, comparison with BeppoSAX data shows that our values do not contradict those found by b7 .

sources.summary: The paper describes S5 2116+81 as a twice-observed FSRQ detected by PDS up to 90 keV, with modest variability and flat power-law spectra.

quotes.quote: S5 2116+81 This object was observed by twice, six months apart (see Tab. tab2 ). In both observations the source was detected in the PDS up to 90 keV. The source varied between the two observations ( 20 ), with the highest state in the first observation. Both datasets are well fitted by a flat single power--law model (see Fig. fig6 and Tab. singpowbl ), with very similar spectral indices (within the errors).

gmw_00001073.name: ESO294-G010
gmw_00001073.overview: ESO294-G010 is a dwarf dS0/Im galaxy located 130 kpc from NGC55 and 490 kpc from NGC300 [arXiv:0811.0329]. It contains neutral hydrogen with a low gas mass [arXiv:astro-ph/0508062], and its gas peak is significantly offset from its stellar component [arXiv:astro-ph/0508062].

sources.summary: The paper compares previous spectroscopic line detections for ESO294-G010 with new emission north of the center and gives its distances from NGC55 and NGC300.

quotes.quote: ESO294-G010 Existing spectroscopic data for this object showed clear detections of the (656.3 nm) and [ O iii ] (500.7 nm) lines but the signal to noise ratio did not allow the detection of the second [ O iii ] (495.9 nm) line see Figure 5 jerjen1998 . These lines reportedly originate from a feature 18 or 150 kpc projected distance (at D = 1.7 Mpc) south of the center of the galaxy. This is in disagreement with the present results which show emission 7 or 60 kpc north of the center, more or less coincident with the detected emission bouchard2005 . Careful inspection of our residual image shows no sign of emission on the southern side. This dS0/Im galaxy is situated 130 50 kpc (3D) from NGC55 and 490 140 kpc from NGC300.

sources.summary: The paper detects neutral hydrogen in ESO294-G010, estimates a low H I mass consistent with stellar outflows, and finds its gas peak significantly offset from the stellar component.

quotes.quote: Four of these objects, ESO294-G010, ESO410-G005, ESO540-G030, and ESO540-G032, were detected in with neutral hydrogen masses in the range of 2-9 10^5 ( M_ HI /L_ B = 0.08, 0.13, 0.16, and 0.18 M_ /L_ , respectively). These masses are consistent with the gas mass expected from stellar outflows over a large period of time.

These offsets are 35 ^ (290 pc projected distance or 0.9 times its Holmberg radius, R_ Ho , jerjen2000 ) for ESO294-G010 --- greater than the 20^ accuracy --- and 30 ^ (460 pc or 0.85 R_ Ho ) for ESO540-G030 --- greater than the 10^ accuracy.
This might suggest that in ESO294-G010, the supernova explosion/stellar wind scenario might play a bigger role than in the case of Phoenix.

gmw_00001074.name: ESO410-G005
gmw_00001074.overview: ESO410-G005 is a dwarf galaxy proposed for reclassification from a dwarf spheroidal to a transition dwarf spheroidal/dwarf irregular type [arXiv:astro-ph/0508062]. It contains neutral hydrogen gas and a centrally concentrated population of young blue stars [arXiv:astro-ph/0508062].

sources.summary: The paper detects neutral hydrogen in ESO410-G005, finds its gas mass consistent with passive stellar-outflow buildup, and proposes reclassifying it from dSph to dSph/dIrr.

quotes.quote: Four of these objects, ESO294-G010, ESO410-G005, ESO540-G030, and ESO540-G032, were detected in with neutral hydrogen masses in the range of 2-9 10^5 ( M_ HI /L_ B = 0.08, 0.13, 0.16, and 0.18 M_ /L_ , respectively). These masses are consistent with the gas mass expected from stellar outflows over a large period of time.

In line with the morphology-density relation and because of the presence of both gas and a centrally concentrated population of young blue stars karachentsev2000 in ESO410-G005, we propose a reclassification of ESO410-G005 (initially labelled dSph, karachentsev2000 ) to dSph/dIrr.
The masses for ESO294-G010, ESO410-G005, ESO540-G030, and ESO540-G032 are all consistent with passive, undisturbed evolution where all the gas from stellar winds has condensed in the form of H i .

gmw_00001075.name: ESO540-G030
gmw_00001075.overview: ESO540-G030 is a low surface-brightness dwarf galaxy located approximately 3.40 Mpc away [arXiv:astro-ph/0302045] that is classified as a transition dwarf spheroidal/dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0302045]. The galaxy contains neutral hydrogen [arXiv:astro-ph/0508062] and exhibits a spatial offset between its optical and neutral hydrogen components [arXiv:astro-ph/0508062].

sources.summary: The paper treats KDG 2 as a low surface-brightness dwarf whose HST photometry gives a 3.40 Mpc TRGB distance and whose blue central stars suggest a transition dSph/dIrr type.

quotes.quote: This dSph galaxy of low surface brightness was found by Karachentseva (1968) and then selected as the Local Volume member candidate by Karachentseva Karachentsev (1998). According to the surface photometry carried out by Jerjen et al. (1998, 2000), KDG 2 has an integrated magnitude of B_T = 16.37 mag, an integrated color of (B-R)_T = 0.83 mag, and a central surface brightness of 24.1 ^m/ in the B band. Jerjen et al. (1998) determined its distance via SBF to be 3.19 0.13 Mpc. The HST photometry measured about 1900 predominantly red stars. We derived the TRGB to be located at 23 65 0 20 , which corresponds to D = 3.40 0.34 Mpc, in good agreement with the previous estimate.

Apart from red stars, we also found a number of blue stars, which occupy the central part of the galaxy. Probably, KDG 2 is not a dSph galaxy, but belongs to a transition dSph/dIrr type, like LGS-3 and Antlia.

sources.summary: ESO540-030 is highlighted as discrepant: its uncertain SFH, possibly overestimated young metallicity, and modeled extremely red RGB objects complicate TP-AGB model comparisons.

quotes.quote: Two galaxies, ESO540-030 and HS117, in particular appear discrepant in the left-hand panel of Figure fig:agbfrac , showing less TP-AGB light than their SFH might imply. However, the SFH is highly uncertain for ESO540-030. For instance the best fit SFH predicts a moderately high metallicity ( [ m/H ] > -0.8 ) for the youngest populations of this low-mass galaxy. This is likely an over-estimate, as the metallicity is significantly lower for the bulk of cosmic time.

Two galaxies present very high model/data discrepancies for both number and flux contribution of AGB stars, ESO540-030 with a model/data flux ratio = 6.70 and M81-DEEP with model/data flux ratio =7.43 .

The metallicity of ESO540-030 is not unusually high, but its modeled CMD is characterized by a large fraction of extremely red objects all along the RGB sequence, so the accuracy of its SFH may be called in question.

sources.summary: The paper detects neutral hydrogen in ESO540-G030, measures one of the larger H I masses in the sample, and reports a significant gas-optical offset likely linked to star-forming winds.

quotes.quote: Four of these objects, ESO294-G010, ESO410-G005, ESO540-G030, and ESO540-G032, were detected in with neutral hydrogen masses in the range of 2-9 10^5 ( M_ HI /L_ B = 0.08, 0.13, 0.16, and 0.18 M_ /L_ , respectively). These masses are consistent with the gas mass expected from stellar outflows over a large period of time.

These offsets are 35 ^ (290 pc projected distance or 0.9 times its Holmberg radius, R_ Ho , jerjen2000 ) for ESO294-G010 --- greater than the 20^ accuracy --- and 30 ^ (460 pc or 0.85 R_ Ho ) for ESO540-G030 --- greater than the 10^ accuracy.
At least two of the mixed-morphology galaxies, ESO294-G010 and ESO540-G030, have a significant offset between their optical and components.

gmw_00001076.name: Scl-dE1
gmw_00001076.overview: Scl-dE1 is an isolated, gas-deficient early-type dwarf galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0508062] with a very low surface brightness [arXiv:astro-ph/0302045]. It is populated predominantly by red stars [arXiv:astro-ph/0302045] and lacks any detected neutral hydrogen or interstellar medium [arXiv:astro-ph/0302045, arXiv:astro-ph/0508062].

sources.summary: The paper describes Sc 22 as a very low surface-brightness dwarf spheroidal, not detected in H I, with predominantly red stars and a TRGB distance of 4.21 Mpc.

quotes.quote: Surface photometry of Sc 22 was carried out by Jerjen et al. (1998, 2000), who determined its integrated apparent magnitude, B_T = 17.73 mag, integrated color, (B-R)_T = 0.79 mag, and central surface brightness, 25.8^m/ in the B band. Using the method of surface brightness fluctuations (SBF), they estimated the distance to Sc 22 to be 2.67 0.16 Mpc. The galaxy was not detected in the H i line by C o t e et al. (1997), neither was it detected in the ``blind'' HIPASS survey by Staveley-Smith et al. (1998) and Barnes et al. (2001). The color-magnitude diagram of Sc 22 (see Fig. 2) is populated predominantly by red stars. The application of the Sobel filter yields I(TRGB) = 24 10 0 21 , which corresponds to a linear distance of 4.21 0.43 Mpc.

sources.summary: The paper reports no H I detection in Scl-dE1, identifies it as the only Sculptor group galaxy then known without interstellar medium, and emphasizes its isolated gas-deficient early-type nature.

quotes.quote: The fifth object, the spatially isolated dwarf elliptical Scl-dE1, remains undetected at our 3 limit of 22.5 mJy and thus must contain less than 10^5 of neutral hydrogen. This leaves Scl-dE1 as the only Sculptor group galaxy known where no interstellar medium has been found to date.
Consequently, the Sculptor group has only one single early-type dwarf system: Scl-dE1, without H i or any young stars karachentsev2003 .
Scl-dE1 joins Tucana and Cetus as an isolated low mass gas-deficient early-type dwarf.

gmw_00001077.name: BX418
gmw_00001077.overview: BX418 is a young, low-mass star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.3 [arXiv:1504.06948, arXiv:1909.07386]. It features a hard ionizing spectrum [arXiv:1109.0288], strong rest-UV nebular emission lines [arXiv:1405.5473], and a low-metallicity stellar population that may be leaking Lyman continuum photons [arXiv:1109.0288, arXiv:1909.07386].

sources.summary: The paper models BX418 as a young, low-mass z=2.3 galaxy whose varied line widths require shock plus photoionization components, with high-density gas and possible hidden AGN conditions.

quotes.quote: In column 9 of Table 4 we present the observed line ratios for BX418 galaxy at z=2.3, defined by Erb et al., as "one of the youngest and lowest stellar mass continuum-selected z 2 galaxies". The spectral lines show different FWHM for different wavelengths decreasing from 840 for to 640 for HeII 1640, down to 260 for lines in the optical-near-UV range (Table 4, col 10). The observations show that different components contribute to different lines. Although permitted line fluxes (e.g. and HeII) depend strongly on the photoionizing radiation reaching the nebula, they also increase with the temperature of the emitting gas (see Sect. 3.8). Therefore, models accounting for shock+ photoionization are used.

We first investigate whether an AGN (hidden?) is present. The high and HeII 1640 FWHM suggest shock velocities of 850 and 600 , respectively. They are accompanied by a AGN flux characteristic of Seyfert 2 galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper models BX418 as a z=2.3 Lyman-break galaxy whose weak C IV, strong He II, hard ionizing spectrum, low metallicity, and carbon depletion require QHE in the stellar population model.

quotes.quote: The UV spectrum of the z=2.3 galaxy BX418 from erb (shown in Figure bx418 ) has a very weak C IV stellar wind line with a P Cygni profile and strong and broad He II emission. There is clearly a narrow nebular component superposed on the broader He II stellar emission line. This indicates a very hard ionising spectrum present within the galaxy. The He II line implies that the metallicity must be low, especially in carbon, suggesting a metallicity range of 0.001 to 0.004 (i.e. significantly sub-Solar). However the more prominent C IV profile implies a slightly higher (but still sub-Solar) carbon abundance in this source.
As is the case for the Shapley et al composite, matching both lines simultaneously requires the presence of both a reduced relative carbon abundance and QHE in the population synthesis model.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Q2343-BX418 as an extreme KBSS-MOSFIRE galaxy with strong rest-UV nebular lines, compact emission, and new MOSFIRE J, H, and K spectra.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies in the current KBSS-MOSFIRE sample (Q2343-BX418, Q2343-BX660, and Q0207-BX74) are found near the EGPs in the BPT diagram, and have particularly good MOSFIRE (J, H, and K bands) as well as LRIS-B (rest-frame UV) spectra; they are indicated in Figure fig:bpt_mod_peas . Q2343-BX418 ( z=2.3053 ) was studied in detail by as a prototypical example of a UV-bright galaxy with little or no reddening, strong Lyman emission, and unusually strong rest-UV nebular lines of OIII ] 1661 ,1666 and CIII ] 1906 ,1909. We have re-observed Q2343-BX418 with MOSFIRE in J, H, and K bands, covering, in addition to the BPT line ratios (Table tab:n2ha_and_o3n2 and Figure fig:bpt ), the [ OII ] 3726,3729 doublet, detected with S/N 30 .

sources.summary: The paper treats BX418 as a low-mass star-forming galaxy whose low H-beta equivalent width, high line ratio, and young age make LyC leakage plausible.

quotes.quote: It is also interesting to note that, for the low-mass star-forming galaxy BX418 with low EW( ) 44 \ and high / >26 (using the 1 limit on the \ flux), erb10 constrain an age less than 100 Myr from ultraviolet and \ observations as well as dynamical arguments. This young age, despite the location of BX418 at low EW( ) and high / \ in Figs  fig:baton3z_opt and fig:baton3u_opt , is suggestive of the fact that the galaxy might be leaking LyC photons, which would be compatible with the other properties of the galaxy in Figs  fig:baton3z_uv - fig:baton3u_opt . An ionization-bounded model with =-1 can reach EW( ) 44 \ after about 100 Myr of constant star formation, although the corresponding / \ is too small relative to the observed one in Fig.  fig:baton3u_uv h (which pertains to the 25-per-cent nebular contribution to the total \ emission of this object; see erb10 ).)

gmw_00001078.name: IC 4320
gmw_00001078.overview: IC 4320 is a lenticular galaxy that hosts a dust lane [arXiv:1503.01711]. The hyper-luminous X-ray source candidate 2XMM J134404.1 271410 was previously studied for a potential association with the galaxy, but it was later identified as a background quasar [arXiv:1503.01711].

sources.summary: The paper tests whether an HLX candidate is associated with IC 4320, then rejects that association after spectroscopy shows the source is instead a background QSO.

quotes.quote: Here we focus on 2XMM J134404.1 271410, the HLX candidate in IC 4320. At an X-ray luminosity of L_ X = 3.5^ +0.2 _ 0.3 10^ 41 erg s^ 1 (if it is at the distance of IC 4320), it is potentially second only in peak luminosity amongst all HLXs to ESO 243-49 HLX-1. Even at this extreme luminosity, it has been observed in an apparent low/hard X-ray state, making it an excellent IMBH candidate sutton_etal_2012 . As such, it is critical to confirm whether this source is indeed associated with IC 4320.
We can therefore confidently exclude 2XMM J134404.1 271410 from being a HLX associated with the galaxy IC 4320; instead we identify it as a background QSO.
In fact, both its apparent host (IC 4320) and that of ESO 243-49 HLX-1 are lenticular galaxies that host a dust lane (e.g. farrell_etal_2012 ; cf. Fig. 2).

gmw_00001079.name: 3C 123
gmw_00001079.overview: 3C 123 is a luminous Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy at a redshift of approximately 0.22 [arXiv:1801.01751, arXiv:0804.0849]. It is characterized by a peculiar radio structure featuring diffuse, twisted plume-like lobes [arXiv:astro-ph/0312530, arXiv:astro-ph/0101240] and a weak, unresolved core [arXiv:0804.0849].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C123 as a low-redshift FR II galaxy with bright, unusual radio structure and possible recurrent activity, but with no statistical preference for KDA EXT modeling.

quotes.quote: 3C123 This next radio source from the 3C catalogue is classified as a FR II-type galaxy with extended tails of both lobe components (Fanaroff Riley 1974).
