Of most interest are the three dSphs which dominate the excess observed by Fermi: Segue 1, Usra Major II, and Willman 1 Ackermann:2013yva . Although, these three dSphs each have one BZCAT or CRATES source within this radius, none of these sources are particularly nearby (all are >0.7^ away). In order to test the impact of these three sources, we utilize the Fermi tools and calculate their TS values to be 0.00, 4.23 and 9.71 for J100955+160223 (0.70 ^ from Segue 1), J0854+6218 (0.91 ^ from Ursa Major II) and J1048+5009 (0.87 ^ from Willman 1), respectively. In order to estimate the TS value of these sources as evaluated at the location of the dSphs under investigation, we produce 50 simulations for a location 0.7 ^ from a simulated TS=10 source.

This indicates that these three BZCAT and CRATES sources are unlikely to be responsible for a significant fraction of the dSph excess. However, we note that a more thorough re-analysis of the dSph population should investigate the potential for low-TS emission from this population.

sources.summary: The paper treats Willman 1 as an ambiguous ultra-faint Milky Way companion at the dwarf galaxy/globular-cluster boundary, important for detection limits and extrapolated satellite census estimates.

quotes.quote: Willman 1, Segue 1 and Bo otes II willman1,quintet,boo2 occupy a region of size-luminosity space not previously known to be inhabited by old stellar populations, at the intersection of MW dSphs and globular clusters. Spectroscopic studies spectro showed that Willman 1 may be resident inside a dark matter subhalo with a mass-to-light ratio of 470 . If these ambiguous objects are gravitationally bound, then tidal arguments also favor them being dark matter dominated boot .
Objects with Segue 1, Bo otes II, or Willman 1-like luminosities would not have been detectable with scale sizes of 100 pc or larger, even at very nearby distances.

If we include the ambiguous objects Segue 1, Willman 1, and Bo otes II 1 in the calculation, then the R_ 90 ( R_ 99 ) DR6 estimate would be 56 ( 85 ) satellites only 15 of which are known, or 224 ( 340 ) across the sky. The ambiguity of Segue 1, Willman 1, and Bo otes II has considerable effect on the extrapolated MW census, underscoring the need for an understanding of these extremely faint systems.

sources.summary: Willman 1 is described as a peculiar ultra-faint object, likely the smallest observed dSph, with high mass-to-light ratio and uncertain dark-matter profile.

quotes.quote: Willman 1 Willman 1 (SDSS J1049+5103) is a very peculiar object, located at a distance of 38 7 kpc from the Earth in the constellation of Ursa Major. Discovered in 2004 by B. Willman , using data from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey , it was then further observed with Keck/DEIMOS and more recently by Siegel et al. . With an absolute magnitude of M _V 2.5 and a half--light radius (i.e. the radius of a cylinder, pointing to the Earth, that encloses half of the luminosity of the object) of 21 7 pc, it looks very similar to a globular cluster, even if its narrow distribution of stellar velocities and the large spread in stellar metallicities suggests that it is indeed the smallest dSph ever observed. The object may show evidence for tidal disruption from its tri-axial stellar distribution .

With a luminosity of 855 L _ , and a mass of the order of 5 10^5 M _ , Willman 1 could feature a mass--to--light ratio in the range 500-700 M _ / L _ , or even more, making it one of the most DM dominated objects in the Universe . The small number of stars that belong to this dSph hinders, however, an accurate determination of the DM density profile.

sources.summary: The paper treats Willman I as a faint possible dwarf galaxy with a large mass-to-light ratio, observes it with VERITAS, and derives WIMP annihilation constraints from its gamma-ray upper limits.

quotes.quote: Willman I belongs to the group of faint stellar systems that were recently discovered in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey willman05 . Although Willman I shares some similarities with tidally disrupted globular clusters, the extreme mass-to-light ratio inferred from its stellar velocity dispersion martin07 suggests that it is a dwarf galaxy. strigari08 have projected that this system may have significantly larger DM annihilation signal than that calculated for previously known dwarf galaxies.

VERITAS took 20 hours of observation on both Draco and Ursa Minor from March 2007 to May 2007 and 15 hours of observation on Willman I from December 2007 to Feburary 2008. No significant ray emission were detected, and the 95 C.L. upper limits on the integral ray flux from each source were set at the level of approximately 1 percent of the Crab Nebula flux. Using the DM distribution model presented in strigari08 , constraints on the thermally averaged product of velocity and cross section of WIMP ( v ) as a function of its mass were derived from the ray flux upper limits for Willman I (see Figure susy ).

sources.summary: Willman 1 is one of the dwarf galaxies used for gamma-ray bounds; the paper gives it a separate scale-radius range and VERITAS flux upper limits.

quotes.quote: These bounds are implied by gamma-ray observations of the dwarf galaxies Sagittarius, Willman 1, Draco, and Ursa Minor. We use updated stellar kinematic data to calculate the dark matter density profiles of these dwarf galaxies, marginalizing over a wide range of profile shapes.

For Sagittarius, Ursa Minor, and Draco, the scale radius is fixed to be within the range r_0 = [ 0.1-10 ] kpc, while for Willman 1 the scale radius is fixed to be within the range r_0 = [ 0.01-1 ] kpc. For each galaxy, _0 is set by the condition that the mass within 300 pc is fixed to the respective observed values .

The bounds for Willman 1, Ursa Minor, and Draco are from recent VERITAS observations . VERITAS observed Willman 1 for 15 hours and Draco and Ursa Minor for 20 hours each, and found gamma-ray flux upper limits for all three dwarfs at the level of 1 of the Crab Nebula Hui2008 .

sources.summary: The review describes Willman 1 as a very low-luminosity ultra-faint dwarf whose surface brightness, dynamical mass, and mass-to-light ratio do not match simulated fossils.

quotes.quote: According to results by RG05 and , Willman 1, Bootes II, Segue 1 and Segue 2 do not lie on the luminosity-surface brightness relationship of simulated pre-reionization fossils. This result is based on the assumption that fossil properties are not modified by tides. Their surface brightness is larger than the model predictions for objects with such low-luminosity.

Willman 1, Bootes II, Segue1 and Segue2 are among the lowest luminosity ultra-faint dwarfs discovered so far; however, they do not fit the typical properties of pre-reionization fossils (see sec:comp ). For instance Willman 1 has a dynamical mass within the largest stellar orbit ( 100 pc) of 5 10^ 5 M_ and a mass-to-light ratio 470 , similar to other ultra-faint dwarfs Willmanetal05AJ . However, given its low luminosity, Willman 1 has central surface brightness that is too large when compared to simulated ``fossils''.

sources.summary: The paper uses XMM-Newton observations of Willman 1 to constrain sterile-neutrino dark matter, adopting its dwarf-spheroidal nature, distance, optical extent, and uncertain mass profile.

quotes.quote: We report the results of a search for an emission line from radiatively decaying dark matter in the ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy Willman 1 based on analysis of spectra extracted from XMM-Newton X-ray Observatory data. The observation follows up our analysis of Chandra data of Willman 1 lk10 that resulted in line flux upper limits over the Chandra bandpass and evidence of a 2.5 keV feature at a significance below the 99 confidence threshold used to define the limits.

The first corresponds to 1.7 the half-light radius ( 1.7 25 pc 232'' at the 38 kpc Willman 1 distance) within which the mass is particularly well-determined ae11 and for which we apply mask ``a'' above, the second to 150 pc ( 818'' ; mask ``b''), and the third to 100 pc ( 545'' ; mask ``'') -- the mass distribution is estimated within 100 pc in . The latter two are of the same order as the optical extent of Willman 1, and may be compared to the tidal radius of 930 pc (Section 3) and a virial radius on the 10^9 M_ mass-scale of 25 kpc.

While this estimate is based on equilibrium dynamical models fit to the stellar velocity dispersion profile, the dynamical status -- and even the very nature of Willman 1 -- were re-examined subsequent to our original X-ray observation of Willman 1 with Chandra . Recent optical spectroscopy supports the case that Willman 1 is (or was) a dwarf spheroidal and not a star cluster; however -- in addition to the limited statistics of the velocity dispersion profile -- doubts as to whether Willman 1 is in dynamical equilibrium introduce a level of uncertainty in the Willman 1 dark matter mass that must now be estimated from non-equilibrium dynamical modeling wil11 .

sources.summary: The paper includes Willman 1 in the joint dwarf-spheroidal likelihood analysis, finds no significant gamma-ray signal, and identifies it as a primary contributor to the deviation from the null hypothesis.

quotes.quote: Thus, we are left with a subset of 15 dwarf spheroidal galaxies as input to the joint likelihood analysis: Bootes I, Canes Venatici II, Carina, Coma Berenices, Draco, Fornax, Hercules, Leo II, Leo IV, Sculptor, Segue 1, Sextans, Ursa Major II, Ursa Minor, and Willman 1. No significant signal is found for any of the spectra tested in the combined analysis, prompting us to calculate 95 CL constraints on using the delta-log-likelihood procedure described above ( fig4 ).

The sensitivity of the combined analysis depends most heavily on the observations of the dwarf galaxies with the largest . Specifically, the results of the combined analysis are dominated by Coma Berenices, Draco, Segue 1, Ursa Major II, Ursa Minor, and Willman 1. Because the combined analysis is dominated by a few dwarf galaxies with high , any statistical fluctuation in the combined results will necessarily be associated to an excess coincident with these dwarf galaxies.
We find that the primary contributors to the deviation of the combined analysis from the null hypothesis are the ultra-faint dwarf galaxies Segue 1, Ursa Major II, and Willman 1.

sources.summary: VERITAS observed Willman 1, but the paper excludes it from joint J-profile analyses because irregular stellar kinematics make its inferred dark-matter profile unreliable.

quotes.quote: From the beginning of four-telescope operations in 2007 to the summer of 2013, five dwarf galaxies in the northern hemisphere have been observed by VERITAS: Segue 1, Ursa Minor, Draco, Bo o tes and Willman 1. While the statistical uncertainty due to finite kinematic sample sizes dominates the errors in J for ultrafaint dwarfs (e.g. Segue 1, Bo o tes 1, Willman 1), the assumption of spherical symmetry may cause a moderate bias (comparable to the statistical error bar) for the classical dwarfs (e.g. Draco, Ursa Minor). The stellar population of Willman 1 shows irregular kinematics, which may be due to ongoing tidal disruption of the satellite .

Regardless of the cause, the observations strongly suggest that Willman 1 is not in dynamical equilibrium, violating a core assumption of the Jeans equation. This object was excluded from the analysis of 2015ApJ...801...74G , who considered the inferred J profile to be unreliable with no handle on the magnitude of the error. In the present work, we therefore exclude Willman 1 from results which require an estimate of its J profile.

sources.summary: The paper classifies Willman 1 as a dwarf galaxy, citing a significant member-star metallicity spread and an unsegregated stellar distribution inconsistent with the expected star-cluster ratio.

quotes.quote: Willman 1 was discovered by willmanetal2005 in SDSS data. willmanetal2011 obtained Keck/DEIMOS spectroscopy for 45 likely member stars, and, while they were not able to detect a velocity dispersion for the system, they found a significant metallicity spread between two member stars. They therefore concluded that Willman 1 is a dwarf galaxy.

We find R_ H,Bright /R_ H,Faint = 1.05 0.08 , indicating that Willman 1 is unsegregated. The expected value for a star cluster would only be around 0.90, which is significantly below the observed ratio. We therefore conclude that Willman 1 is a dwarf galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper selects Willman 1 as an old, metal-poor ultrafaint dSph with a large J-factor, gives its location and structural parameters, and fits its stellar density profile.

quotes.quote: In this work, We select three dSphs, namely Willman 1 (SDSS J1049+5103), Reticulum II (DES J0335.6-5403) and Triangulum II, in our analysis. These old and metal-poor stellar sources have almost the largest J-factors among known dSphs. Willman 1 is a low-luminosity Milky Way satellite, which is located at (l, b)=( 158.58^ , 56.78^ ). This source is an old, metal-poor stellar system at a distance of 38 kpc, with a half-light radius r_h 21 pc and an absolute magnitude M_V 2.5 . The stellar density of Willman 1 with 1 uncertainties and background subtraction is shown as Fig. fig:zy1 , which is obtained from the Wide Field Camera (WFC) mounted on the Isaac Newton Telescope (INT).

sources.summary: The paper uses Willman I as an ultra-faint dwarf constraint, arguing low-mass axion models imply too-large halo masses and Gaia-informed dynamical-friction limits rule out part of the parameter space.

quotes.quote: However, at such low masses, the predicted halo masses of the UFDs (such as Segue I) is too high given their dynamical state as we will return to in the next section. On the other hand, similar to the results of Calabrese:2016fx , high mass axions ( m>10^ 22 ) can explain the halo masses of the UFDs such as Draco II, Triangulum II, and Segue I, however, such high masses would predict halo profiles that do not agree with the observed mass profile slope of Fornax and Sculptor.

Segue I and Willman I ( M_h 10^ 10.5 ) which is not only more than 3- away from the plausible halo mass for these satellites based on their dynamical friction timescale, but even more in tension with their halo masses inferred based on their stellar masses.

The arrived upper limits are more stringent as the orbits of these satellites show highly eccentric orbits with an effectively shorter semi-major axis. Such upper limits for Segue I and Willman I rule out axion masses with m_ 22 <10 with high confidence.

sources.summary: The paper excludes Willman 1 because its uncertain dynamical state, radial velocity structure, poorly constrained metallicity, and very small stellar sample make its BSS fraction uninterpretable.

quotes.quote: We excluded Willman 1 from Table tab:santana due to its uncertain dynamical state - the derived systemic velocity for the innermost candidate member stars is significantly offset from the outer members willman2011 . Indeed, as discussed in their section 5.2 willman2011 , the systemic velocity falls off by 8 km/s from the centre to the outskirts, with a steep drop within one half-light radius, while the velocity dispersion about the systemic velocity is formally 0 2.5 km/s when calculated in three radial bins. Further, the estimated mean metallicity ( [ Fe/H ] 2 dex) is based on only three stars with a large dispersion in metallicity. The unknown dynamical history, and unknown present state, of Willman 1 makes any attempt at quantification of the BSS population very difficult to interpret. Furthermore, the very small number of (candidate) member stars in this extremely faint system ( M_V 2 willman2011 ) leads to such large uncertainties in the fraction of BSS that it would not provide a meaningful addition to the present sample (for reference, the value derived by santana2013 is F^ BSS _ RGB = 0.85 0.75 ).

sources.summary: The paper finds Willman 1 favors a sharply cusped dark matter density profile, close to NFW-like slopes and useful for constraining nonstandard dark matter models.

quotes.quote: Firstly, Segue 1 and Willman 1 favor a sharply cusped inner slope of their dark matter density profiles ( 1.6 for Segue 1 and 1.2 for Willman 1), even considering 95 confidence intervals of the profile. Eridanus II also prefers a cusped dark matter density profile ( 1.2 ), even though the uncertainty for its is larger than for Segue 1 and Willman 1. Intriguingly, the slope of Segue 1 is much steeper than an NFW cusp ( =1 ) but is roughly consistent with ones predicted by the other simulation works e.g., 1997ApJ...477L...9F,1999MNRAS.310.1147M,2008MNRAS.391.1685S,2020MNRAS.492.3662I , while those of Eridanus II and Willman 1 could advocate the NFW ones.

Moreover, several pieces of literature have reported that these UFDs, especially for Segue 1 and Willman 1 can provide the strongest constraint on fuzzy dark matter (FDM) and SIDM models which can create a naturally cored dark matter density profile without relying on any baryonic physics e.g., 2021ApJ...912L...3H,2021PhRvD.103b3017H . This implies that dark matter densities of Segue 1 and Willman 1 could be cusped inner slopes.

sources.summary: The paper models Willman 1 as an extremely faint Milky Way dwarf with a massive dark matter halo, predicting dark-matter-annihilation gamma-ray fluxes comparable to brighter satellites.

quotes.quote: We use kinematic data from three new, nearby, extremely low-luminosity Milky Way dwarf galaxies (Ursa Major II, Willman 1, and Coma Berenices) to constrain the properties of their dark matter halos, and from these make predictions for the ray flux from annihilation of dark matter particles in these halos. We show that these 10^3 L _ dwarfs are the most dark matter dominated galaxies in the Universe, with total masses within 100 pc in excess of 10^6 M _ . Coupled with their relative proximity, their large masses imply that they should have mean ray fluxes comparable to or greater than any other known satellite galaxy of the Milky Way. Our results are robust to both variations of the inner slope of the density profile and the effect of tidal interactions.

sources.summary: The note treats Willman 1 as an extremely faint dwarf generally regarded as a galaxy, using its luminosity and half-light radius to illustrate galaxy-cluster overlap.

quotes.quote: Interesting examples of faint dwarfs are Bo o tes II [ M _ v = -3.1 ] wjw07 , Coma [ M _ v = -3.7 ] bel07 and Willman 1 [ M _ v = -2.5 ] mar07 . All of these galaxies are fainter than the overwhelming majority of globular clusters.

On the other hand Willman 1 mar07 , which has a half-light radius of 20 pc, has generally been regarded as a galaxy. This overlap in size and luminosity raises deep questions about the nature of the distinction between these two classes of objects.

sources.summary: The review says Willman 1 is probably a dwarf but dynamically difficult: its central stars differ in velocity and may violate equilibrium assumptions.

quotes.quote: Some specific objects that should be treated with caution include: Willman 1 -- Although willman11 identified a sizable sample of 40 likely Willman 1 member stars with high-quality Keck/DEIMOS velocity measurements, the internal kinematics of Willman 1 defy straightforward interpretation. The stars closest to the center of Willman 1 differ in velocity by 8 \ from the remainder of the system, and their velocity dispersion is consistent with zero. While it is possible that this configuration is simply the result of small number statistics, it could also indicate that the assumption of dynamical equilibrium is not valid for Willman 1.

Some disagreement persisted for several years regarding the nature of the two least-luminous systems, Willman 1 and Segue 1 e.g., belokurov07,siegel08,no09 , but ultimately the combination of stellar kinematics, metallicities, and chemical abundance measurements led to the conclusion that both are dwarfs willman11,simon11,fsk14 .

sources.summary: The paper uses Willman 1 to constrain self-interacting dark matter, finding it consistent with zero self-interaction and part of the strongest UFD limits on the cross-section.

quotes.quote: In particular, the fitting results for Segue 1 and Willman 1 are consistent with zero cross-section at 1 C.L. and provide the 2 upper-limit 8.6 10^ 2 cm^2/g and 0.39 cm^2/g , respectively. In Fig. fig:sigmav , we show the 1 estimation of v v /m with the MLE for Segue 1 and Willman 1, which correspond to two dimensional contours of 2 ( L _ tot )=2.3 . Compared with the favored SIDM cross-section in the previous study using the dwarf irregular galaxies , the low surface brightness galaxies (blue), and galaxy clusters (green) (see Ref. for details), the Segue 1 and Willman 1 place stringent upper limits.
In particular, Segue 1 and Willman 1 provide stringent constraints on the self-interacting cross-section: /m < 0.1 cm^2/g .

sources.summary: The paper uses Willman I as the lightest known compact dwarf spheroidal in its WDM model; its 3.9e5 solar-mass mass limit sets m > 0.96 keV.

quotes.quote: The lightest known dwarf galaxy (Willman I) implies a lower bound for the WDM particle mass m > 0.96 keV.
The lightest known galaxy of this kind is Willman I (see Table pgal ). Imposing M_ min < M_ Willman I = 3.9 10^5 M_ provides a lower bound for the WDM particle mass: m > 0.96 keV .

sources.summary: The paper says Willman I remains four sigma from MOND expectations and, contrary to earlier explanations, Gaia DR2 gives it the least eccentric UFD orbit.

quotes.quote: Willman I stands 4 away from the MOND's prediction. This satellite has a pericenter and apocenter approach of 44^ +15 _ 19 and 53^ +57 _ 13 kpc respectively, which yields this UFD the lowest orbital eccentricity of e 0.2 . While the deviation of Willman I was identified in MW2010ApJ , this was attributed to speculating a highly eccentric orbit for Willman I while the new data from Gaia DR2 actually suggests Willman I to be actually on the least eccentric orbit of all the UFDs Simon2018ApJGaia .

gmw_00001095.name: SECCO-dI-2
gmw_00001095.overview: SECCO-dI-2 is an isolated, star-forming dwarf irregular galaxy located at a distance of approximately 40 Mpc [arXiv:1701.07632, arXiv:1703.05610]. It features a central fluffy nucleus, compact star-forming knots, and a gas-rich ultra-diffuse structure surrounded by an asymmetric low-surface-brightness halo [arXiv:1701.07632, arXiv:1703.05610].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SdI-2 as a distant, isolated dwarf irregular with an asymmetric low-surface-brightness halo, central nucleus, and compact star-forming knots.

quotes.quote: In particular, following pap2 we call them SECCO-dI-1 and SECCO-dI-2 (where dI = dwarf Irregular), abbreviated as SdI-1 and SdI-2.

SdI-2 lacks any redshift, size and optical magnitude estimate. There are two GALEX sources projected onto the main body of the galaxy, GALEXMSC J114433.79-005200.0 classified as a UV source, and GALEXASC J114433.60-005203.0 classified as a galaxy. Our deep images (see Fig. imaC1C2 ) reveal two remarkably elongated irregular blue galaxies, with some compact knots superimposed. SdI-1 may be interacting with some smaller companions, located 15 westward of its center. The main body of SdI-2 is surrounded by a very low SB asymmetric halo, more extended in the North-East direction. A fluffy nucleus is visible at the very center of SdI-2, which can be perceived also in the light profile (as a slight change of slope in the innermost 2 , see below).
We obtain a distance of 112 8 Mpc and 40 1 Mpc for SdI-1 and SdI-2, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper reports Effelsberg HI observations of SECCO-dI-2, finding it is a star-forming gas-rich isolated UDG compatible with feedback-driven UDG formation scenarios.

quotes.quote: We report on the results of radio observations in the 21 cm emission line of atomic hydrogen (HI) of four relatively isolated ultra-diffuse galaxies (UDGs): , , , and SECCO-dI-2. Our Effelsberg observations resulted in non-detections for the first three UDGs, and a clear detection for the last. On the other hand, SECCO-dI-2 is a star forming gas-rich dwarf, similar to two other field UDGs that have literature HI data: SECCO-dI-1 and .

In this letter, we present new radio observations in the 21 cm emission line of HI of four isolated UDGs, , , \ Dalcanton1997 , and SECCO-dI-2 (hereafter ; Bellazzini2017 ), taken with the Effelsberg radio telescope. The first three objects represent all known quiescent UDGs that are relatively isolated, while the last object is an optically identified star-forming UDG with no prior information on its HI content.

gmw_00001096.name: SDSS J142055.01+400715.7
gmw_00001096.overview: SDSS J142055.01+400715.7 is a low-mass early-type galaxy surrounded by tidal features [arXiv:1808.01730]. Its inner rotation-dominated disk harbors ongoing star formation, while its outer dispersion-dominated region exhibits LIER-like emission [arXiv:1808.01730].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J142055.01+400715.7 as a low-mass early-type galaxy with tidal features, decoupled kinematics, and ongoing nuclear star formation indicating recent gas-rich merging.

quotes.quote: We report a discovery of an ETG (SDSS J142055.01+400715.7) with M_ * =10 ^ 10 M _ that offers direct evidence for gas-rich merging as the origin of low-mass ETGs. The integrated properties of the galaxy are consistent with a typical low-mass ETG, but the outer and inner regions show distinct dispersion- and rotation-dominated kinematics, respectively. There are some tidal features surrounding the galaxy.

In contrast to the kinematically decoupled cores in other elliptical galaxies, the one in SDSS J142055.01+400715.7 harbors ongoing star formation, as indicated by the spatially resolved BPT diagram Bal:81, Kew:01, Kau:03 . As shown in Fig. 5, the rotation-dominated disk is filled with star forming regions, while the outer region has LIER-like Bel:16 emission likely excited by old stellar populations. The star formation rate is calculated from the H- emission, accounting for dust extinction which we calculated using the Balmer decrement along with the Calzetti's attenuation curve Cal:00 . As shown in Fig. 5 (), the inner disk is located within the main sequence of star-forming galaxies, indicating that its level of star formation is more like that of a spiral galaxy than a starburst. This presence of ongoing star formation offers direct evidence that the merging responsible for this complex galaxy structure must be gas rich.

gmw_00001097.name: GRG-J2233+1315
gmw_00001097.overview: GRG-J2233+1315 is a giant radio galaxy with a redshift of 0.09956 [arXiv:2209.13059] and a barbell-like morphology that spans 1.83 Mpc [arXiv:2209.13059]. The galaxy features collimated jets with a kink structure [arXiv:2209.13059] and diffuse outer lobes that lack hotspots [arXiv:1704.00516, arXiv:2209.13059].

sources.summary: The paper presents GRG-J2233+1315 as a 1.83 Mpc barbell-like giant radio galaxy with kinked jets, diffuse steep-spectrum lobes, and a low-mass cluster environment.

quotes.quote: We present for the first time a study of peculiar giant radio galaxy (GRG) J223301+131502 using deep multi-frequency radio observations from GMRT (323, 612, and 1300 MHz) and LOFAR (144 MHz) along with optical spectroscopic observations with the WHT 4.2m optical telescope. Our observations have firmly established its redshift of 0.09956 and unveiled its exceptional jet structure extending more than 200 kpc leading to a peculiar kink structure of 100 kpc. We measure the overall size of this GRG to be 1.83 Mpc; it exhibits lobes without any prominent hotspots and closely resembles a barbell. Our deep low-frequency radio maps clearly reveal the steep-spectrum diffuse emission from the lobes of the GRG. The magnetic field strength of 5 G and spectral ages between about 110 to 200 mega years for the radio lobes were estimated using radio data from LOFAR 144 MHz observations and GMRT 323 and 612 MHz observations.

Overall, the radio morphology of GRG-J2233+1315 (hearafter Barbell GRG) resembles a barbell, the weightlifting equipment. Even though the Barbell GRG shows two lobes, as do typical FRII RGs, it lacks the presence of hotspots, and hence it is difficult to categorise the source strictly in the classical definition of FRI or FRII morphology. However, the presence of collimated jets connecting to the lobes is the same as seen in typical RGs or GRGs of FRII type.

sources.summary: The paper describes GRG15 as a peculiar giant radio galaxy hosted by a brightest cluster galaxy, with diffuse outer lobes and a dense cluster environment.

quotes.quote: GRG15 (J223301+131502): A GRG hosted by a BCG The radio morphology of this GRG is non-linear and peculiar. We observe the host galaxy SDSS J223301.30+131502.5 in optical band (Figure optical ) to coincide with the radio core whose position is marked '+' on the NVSS colour contour map (Figure subfig-15 ). On the western side of the core, we observe a bright unresolved peak which we conjecture to be a bright knot structure on the jet. A similar but fainter knot is also observed on the eastern jet closer to the radio core. Beyond these two close hotspot/knots on either side of the radio core are the outer lobes of GRG15 which appear quite diffuse.

gmw_00001098.name: Mkn 78
gmw_00001098.overview: Mkn 78 is a barred [arXiv:astro-ph/0012035], heavily obscured Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:2204.13140] and potential post-merger system [arXiv:2212.02513]. It features an active galactic nucleus with a jet-gas interaction driving bipolar flows [arXiv:astro-ph/0603420], alongside extended soft X-ray emission [arXiv:2204.13140] and distant southwest ionized gas clouds [arXiv:1908.01376].

sources.summary: The paper studies Mrk 78 as a Seyfert 2 with extended ionized gas; it finds distant southwest clouds moving in its potential and ionized mainly by AGN radiation.

quotes.quote: Mrk 78 was included in the sample of the confirmed EELR selected from the Galaxy Zoo survey, but the follow-up spectra were obtained along PA=90 Keel2012 , therefore, the properties of the southwestern external emission knots were not measured. In this paper, we analysed the large-scale ionized gas distribution and spectral properties of the most distant parts of the EELR in Mrk 78 derived from new long-slit and 3D spectroscopic observations at the 6-m telescope of Special Astrophysical Observatory of Russian Academy of Sciences (SAO RAS).

However, the FPI map also revealed separate ionized-gas clouds (labeled as C1 and C2 in Fig. fields ) at a distance of 15-20 ( 12-16 kpc) southwest of the galaxy. The line-of-sight velocity field shows that the cloud velocities lie in the velocities range of the disc gas (10900--11500 km s ^ 1 ). Whereas the velocities of C1 and C2 by 50--200 km s ^ 1 exceed the nearest S-W side of the galactic disc. This indicates that both clouds are moving in the Mrk 78 gravitational potential, but rotate out of the galaxy plane on retrograde orbits possibly.

The BPT diagrams have demonstrated that the AGN radiation makes the main contribution to the ionization state of these clouds. Moreover, the location of both clouds together with other external [ OIII ] emitting structures in the west side of the Mrk 78 disc is in a good agreement with the ionization cone borders proposed by according only to the circumnuclear region structure and kinematics.

sources.summary: The paper uses Chandra imaging of Mrk 78 to study AGN feedback, finding an obscured Seyfert galaxy with extended soft X-ray emission, complex gas conditions, and likely shocks.

quotes.quote: Mrk 78 is classified as an SB Seyfert 2 galaxy in the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED). It has a redshift of z=0.03715 michel88 , residing at a distance of 160 Mpc (for H_0=70 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ). There is evidence that Mrk 78 is heavily obscured based on its X-ray and mid-infrared (IR) properties.

X-ray morphology As can seen in Figure fig:xraylabels , the 0.3-2 keV emission at the center of Mrk 78 is extended, especially in the E-W direction. There is a bright knot of 0.3-2 keV emission labeled XC-1 in Figure fig:xraylabels that is roughly 1 ^ across in the vicinity of the nucleus. As expected given that Mrk 78 hosts an obscured Seyfert 2 AGN, this emission does not originate directly from the innermost part of the AGN (i.e. accretion disk or corona) as evidenced by the fact that it is not centered on the peak of the 2-8 keV emission and it has a more asymmetric profile than expected for the Chandra PSF.

Thus, it seems most likely that the soft X-ray emission results from a mixture of photoionization and collisional ionization, the latter of which may arise in shocks. Overall, our spectral analysis reveals that the extended soft X-ray emission in Mrk 78 arises from a complex medium, exhibiting a range of densities and temperatures, as seen in a number of AGN extended X-ray regions (e.g. travascio21 ; jones20 ; fischer19 ; maksym19 ; paggi12 ).

sources.summary: The paper studies Mrk 78 as a Seyfert 2 with jet-gas interaction, extended narrow-line emission, bipolar flows, measured redshift and distance, and ionized gas aligned with the radio axis.

quotes.quote: We report near-infrared spectroscopic data for the Seyfert 2 galaxy Mrk 78, taken with the LIRIS near-infrared camera/spectrometer at the William Herschel Telescope (WHT). The long-slit spectra clearly show extended emission. The resolution and depth of the near-infrared spectra allows the examination of its morphology and ionization regions, and a direct comparison with similarly deep visible spectra.

Mrk 78 is a classical example of an AGN with a jet--gas interaction, showing large bipolar flows Whittle88, pedlar , with about half of the NLR flux originating in two components, having velocities of 680 km s ^ 1 and 480 km s ^ 1 , respectively. In this paper we present NIR long-slit data of Mrk 78 with the aim of understanding and characterizing the mechanisms responsible for its emission spectrum. Mrk 78 is a Seyfert 2 with galactic extinction amounting to E(B-V)= 0.035 mag Schlegel98 . Its measured redshift ( z=0.0371 ) Michel88 gives a distance of 150 Mpc and a physical scale of 715 pc/arcsec ( H_ 0 = 75 km s ^ 1 is used throughout this paper).

sources.summary: The paper studies the Seyfert 2 galaxy Mkn 78 with HST spectroscopy, finding its narrow-line region favors nuclear photoionization by optically thick and thin gas over shock ionization.

quotes.quote: To investigate this claim, we have studied the Seyfert 2 galaxy Mkn 78, using HST UV/optical images and spectroscopy. Since Mkn 78 provides the archetypal jet-driven bipolar velocity field, if shocks are important anywhere they should be important in this object. Having mapped the emission line fluxes and velocity field, we first compare the ionization conditions to standard photoionization and shock models. We find coherent variations of ionization consistent with photoionization model sequences which combine optically thick and thin gas, but are inconsistent with either autoionizing shock models or photoionization models of just optically thick gas. Furthermore, we find absolutely no link between the ionization of the gas and its kinematic state, while we do find a simple decline of ionization degree with radius.

sources.summary: The paper finds Mrk 78 extended over nearly 4 arcsec, with a resolved bright central component, a western secondary component, east-west extension, and faint southwest VLBI emission.

quotes.quote: Mrk 78 (0737+652) Y : Mrk 78 is an extended source nearly 4 arcsec in size in our 5 GHz image. The bright central component is resolved and is coincident with the optical nucleus Clements81 . The secondary component is nearly 2 arcsec to the west of the main peak (P.A. 90 ^ ). The brighter component is also extended roughly in the east-west direction (P.A. 80 ^ ). G : Our map shows a faint extension towards the south-west (P.A. 130 ^ ).

sources.summary: The paper fits Mrk 78 with soft thermal/power-law components and an absorbed hard power law plus iron line, deriving X-ray fluxes and rest-frame luminosity.

quotes.quote: The \ spectrum of Mrk 78 (top-left panel in Fig. A.1; effective exposure time in EPIC-pn of 7.2 ks) is well fitted with a model including, in the soft \ energy band, a thermal ( kT 0.6 keV) and a power-law component (with a photon index 2.3). The emission at energies above 2 keV is well reproduced by an absorbed ( N_H 5.7 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ) power-law (with photon index fixed to 1.8 because of the limited photon statistics) plus an iron emission line. The line energy (E=6.31--6.45 keV) indicates emission from neutral or mildly ionized iron, while its EW ( 340 eV) is consistent, within the errors, with being produced by transmission through the same matter responsible for the absorption of the nuclear component. The 0.5--2 keV and 2--10 keV fluxes are 8.1 10^ 14 \ and 5.9 10^ 13 , respectively; the de-absorbed, rest-frame 2--10 keV luminosity for this source is 8.5 10^ 42 .

sources.summary: Mkn 78 is a known EELR case recovered by the search; its compact ionized region can be lost against galaxy starlight and shows inner multiple components.

quotes.quote: Both targeted and serendipitous lists overlap for many objects with bright emission-line structures, and recover such well-studied cases from the literature as Mkn 266, NGC 5252 and Mkn 78; we observed these so as to have a consistent set of spectra for comparison.

Of the remainder, Mkn 78 and Mkn 463 were selected in the targeted AGN sample, while Mkn 1498 and UGC 11185 were recognized only in the serendipitous survey. It may be relevant that both Mkn 78 and Mkn 463 have ionized regions with relatively small projected extent, easily lost against the galaxy starlight (which in Mkn 463 is morphologically complex).
In Mkn 78, multiple components are seen in the inner few arcseconds, even in [ Ne V ] Fischer .

sources.summary: The paper models Mrk 78 as a post-merger type 2 AGN with bulge-dominated enclosed mass and radiation-pressure outflows that may ionize and evacuate galaxy gas.

quotes.quote: Mrk 78 is a potential post-merger system Whittle2004 with a type 2 AGN.
The total enclosed mass in Mrk 78 is dominated by the large bulge (2nd component) over the full extent of the galaxy.

Therefore, the large launch radii in Mrk 78 suggest a case of strong negative feedback from AGN radiation pressure, where the gas in a significant fraction of the galaxy is being ionized and evacuated, thereby potentially impeding star-formation as well as (eventually) black hole accretion.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 78 as a barred Seyfert/starburst composite whose PSPC emission is extended or marginally resolved and whose soft spectrum is best fit by thermal plasma.

quotes.quote: Mrk 78 is significantly resolved only according to the constrained (second) method.
Mrk 78 Mrk 78 is a barred galaxy.
The PSPC spectrum of Mrk 78 is best fit with a thermal plasma, kT = 0.76 keV, and Galactic absorption.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 78 as an inclined composite whose small radio/[O III] structure is much smaller than its wedge-shaped X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: At radio wavelengths, Mrk 78 extends around a central core, linearly toward the east and in a diffuse lobe toward the west, with associated [ O 3 ] emission Ped89 .
The radio size is around 2 \ (1.4 kpc), which is much smaller than the X-ray radius of 20--30 kpc.
Another distinction is that while the radio and optical line emission extend east-west, the X-ray emission is wedge-shaped, opening toward the south (Paper I).

gmw_00001099.name: Mkn 463
gmw_00001099.overview: Mkn 463 is a luminous infrared galaxy and late-stage merger containing a dual active galactic nucleus system with tidal tails [arXiv:1803.11223, arXiv:2201.04149]. The eastern nucleus, a Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1807.04298], exhibits a biconical ionization morphology [arXiv:2201.04149] and drives both ionized gas outflows and molecular gas inflows [arXiv:1807.04298].

sources.summary: Mkn 463 was selected from the targeted AGN sample; its compact ionized region, complex morphology, double-AGN evidence, and extended [O III] emission are discussed.

quotes.quote: Of the remainder, Mkn 78 and Mkn 463 were selected in the targeted AGN sample, while Mkn 1498 and UGC 11185 were recognized only in the serendipitous survey. It may be relevant that both Mkn 78 and Mkn 463 have ionized regions with relatively small projected extent, easily lost against the galaxy starlight (which in Mkn 463 is morphologically complex).

In both Mkn 463 and Mkn 739, Chandra imaging has furnished evidence for double AGN ( Koss , Bianchi ). In both cases, the emission regions are much larger than the separation between AGN components, so we do not know whether the ionization is associated mostly with one or the other. More detailed [ O III ] images could resolve this. However the flux sources are apportioned between components, one AGN in each system must have an ionizing/FIR ratio at least as high as our tabulated limit. Several earlier studies have noted the extended [ O III ] emission around Mkn 463 ( Mazzarella , Uomoto , Chatzichristou ).

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 463E as a challenging Seyfert 2 object with extended UV emission, a blue continuum, weak polarization, and uncertain featureless-continuum origin.

quotes.quote: In contrast, in Mrk 463E, the UV emission is extended 1 Kpc and it looks like scattered light of the hidden S1 nucleus (Figure 2). Two exceptions to this dual clasification are Mrk 477 and Mrk 463E. Both objects show a very blue continuum, very diluted stellar absorption features and very broad HeII 4686 emission (Figure 6). In the case of Mrk 463E, the interpretation is more controversial.

Like in Mrk 477, in Mrk 463E, the polarized spectrum does not show HeII emission, and the fractional degree of polarization is only a few percent. However, the morphology of the UV emission of Mrk 463E is very different to that of those S2 that show starburst characteristics (Figure 1). Thus, we have to conclude that the origin of its FC is an enigma and Mrk 463E is a very challanging object.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 463 as a high-radio-loudness dual AGN whose eastern hidden Seyfert nucleus dominates X-rays and has non-standard radio morphology.

quotes.quote: The two objects with the highest radio-loudness parameters are Mrk 463 and 3C 303.0. The object 3C 303.0 is expected to be radio loud based on its inclusion in the 3C catalog. Further investigation of Mrk 463 reveals that it contains two nuclei at very close separation (3.8 kpc, 2008MNRAS.386..105B ), with the eastern source Mrk 463E dominating the X-ray emission by a factor of 4 . The brighter Mrk 463E nucleus was previously found to have a Seyfert 2-type optical spectrum, but a fuller consideration of the radio morphology reveals that it is a `hidden' Seyfert 1 nucleus 1999ApJ...518..117K . Both nuclei show moderate-to-weak radio emission ( 2003AJ....126.2237D ).

In the case of Mrk 463, this is a known dual AGN system ( radioloudness ) the radio images available on NED for this object http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ reveal a non-standard morphology for the radio source which cannot be straightforwardly classified as one of the traditional Fanaroff-Riley classes (FR-I/FR-II). There is little evidence for a significant component of X-ray jet emission in radio-loud non-blazar AGN 1999ApJ...526...60S , and indeed the X-ray analysis of Mrk 463 in does not consider any X-ray component in the Chandra or data.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 463 as a LIRG with AGN-dominated mid-infrared emission, strong high-ionization line ratios, heavy internal extinction, and luminous X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: Mrk 463 : Mrk 463 is a LIRG. The 3--24 \ SED is well fit by an AGN model, and the derived AGN contribution is 68 of the IR luminosity. The spectral line ratios for [ Ne v ] / [ Ne ii ] and [ O iv ] / [ Ne ii ] are a factor of 5 larger than others in this sample due to relatively low [ Ne ii ] flux, indicating very little star formation is occurring.

That the line ratio is so particularly strong is surprising, because an AGN strong enough to ionize neon and oxygen that heavily would also be expected to have powerful UV emission, which is not seen in Mrk 463. That no enhanced UV emission is seen is presumably due to a high internal extinction. Mrk 463 is also a luminous X-ray source, another indication of strong AGN activity.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Mrk 463 as a merging double-nucleus LIRG and dual-AGN system, separating the spectra of Mrk 463E and Mrk 463W and detecting obscured AGNs with reflection components.

quotes.quote: Mrk 463 ( z = 0.0504 ) is a merging system consisting of Mrk 463E (east) and Mrk 463W (west), which show tidal tails as a result of the galaxy-galaxy interaction Mazzarella1991 . This is a dual AGN system with the third closest physical separation (3.8 kpc, corresponding to an angular separation of 3.8'') following NGC 6240 (1.5 kpc) and Mrk 739 (3.4 kpc) among the Swift /BAT dual-AGN sample compiled by Koss2012 , and hence is an ideal target to link earlier and later stage mergers.

We have analyzed the broadband spectra (0.4--70 keV) of the double-nucleus LIRG Mrk 463 for the first time, observed with NuSTAR , Chandra , and XMM-Newton . An analytical model utilizing the pexmon reflection code and a numerical model utilizing the e-torus model well reproduce the overall spectra. We have determined the absorption column densities of Mrk 463E and Mrk 463W to be 7.5 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 and 2.9 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 , respectively. Significant reflection components are detected in both AGNs, suggesting that their tori are well developed.

sources.summary: The paper presents J1356 + 1822 as a double-nucleus ULIRG and binary AGN with tidal tails, molecular gas inflow, ionized outflow, and no detected H I absorption.

quotes.quote: J1356 + 1822 This is an ULIRG (known as Mrk 463 or UGC 8850) with double nuclei (separated by 4 kpc) and tidal tails mazzarella1991 . The eastern nucleus is classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy and shows strong radio emission. The western nucleus is also an AGN, making this a binary AGN system bianchi2008 . Recently, treister2018 have conducted a detailed study of this system using optical, infrared and sub-mm integral field unit (IFU) spectroscopy.

They detect both outflowing ionized gas and inflowing molecular gas around the eastern nucleus. Interestingly, we do not detect any absorption ( \ 10^ 20 , for \ = 100 K and \ = 1) towards the radio source, which is co-spatial with the eastern nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 463 as a BASS-MUSE AGN host with biconical ionisation morphology and literature-consistent outflow rates, noting its late-stage merger may trigger the observed outflow.

quotes.quote: Several galaxies in the BASS-MUSE sample also show a bi-conical ionisation morphology such as IC 1657, NGC 1365 e.g., venturi18 , NGC 2992 e.g., veilleux01 and Mrk 463 treister18 . Often the outflow flux is concentrated towards the tip of this ionisation cone and falls off as a function of distance from the AGN.

The total instantaneous outflow rates ( M _ res ^ total ) derived from the resolved maps are in the range 0.2--275 M _ yr ^ 1 , which are nearly consistent with previously published values in the literature for some of the galaxies presented in this paper e.g., NGC 1365 and Mrk 463, venturi18, treister18 . Table table:outflow_properties also reports the mass outflow rate within the central 3 of these outflow rate maps, outflow rate obtained from fibre ( M _ fibre ) or slit ( M _ slit ) spectra and the outflow rate if the electron density is assumed to be 200 cm ^ 3 ( M _ 200 ), a commonly adopted value in the literature e.g., fiore17, davies20 . Table table:outflow_properties highlights that the outflow rate values are highly dependent on the method of spectral extraction.
Similarly, Mrk 463 is a late-stage merger and the observed outflow could be due to the accretion disk triggered by the merging activity see treister18 .

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 463e as an AGN-dominated infrared galaxy with unusually high [O IV]/IR output, weak PAH features, and little reprocessed infrared light.

quotes.quote: The one clear outlier in L [ O IV ] /L(IR) is Mrk 463e, for which we infer an L(AGN)/L(IR) ratio significantly above unity. Such a result is physically plausible if most of the AGN energy is not reprocessed into the infrared, but escapes via the X-ray/UV/optical. Indeed, the infrared luminosity for Mrk 463e is only 5 10^ 11 , below the ULIRG cutoff, but its total luminosity is considerably higher, well above the ULIRG threshold armus04 . In addition, Mrk 463e has remarkably weak PAH features, which argues that the starburst contribution is small. Thus, given that Mrk 463e is apparently an AGN--dominated ULIRG with a low ratio of reprocessed to intrinsic light, the high measured ratio of L [ O IV ] /L(IR) seems reasonable and physical.

sources.summary: The paper finds Mrk 463E is an obscured Seyfert-class AGN that is X-ray weak relative to UV indicators, has a high Eddington ratio, and likely hosts a rapidly growing SMBH triggered by the merger.

quotes.quote: The luminosity ratio between X-ray (2--10 keV) and [ O IV ] 25.89 m of Mrk 463E is smaller than those of normal Seyferts by a factor of 5 , indicating that the intrinsic AGN SED is X-ray weak with respect to the UV luminosity. In fact, the bolometric luminosity estimated from the L^ band (3.8 m), [ O IV ] 25.89 m, [ Ne V ] 14.32 m luminosities all yield large bolometric to X-ray luminosity ratios, _ 2-10 keV = 110--410. The Eddington ratio is high, _ Edd = 0.4-0.8 . The large _ 2-10 keV value and high Eddington ratio are consistent with the general trend in AGNs found by Vasudevan2007 . These results suggests that Mrk 463E contains a rapidly growing SMBH, whereas the nucleus is not deeply buried, unlike in typical local ULIRGs.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 463 as a previously reported X-ray dual-AGN merger, resolves double nuclear emission, but finds only one red infrared nucleus, implying possible hot-dust deficiency.

quotes.quote: Infrared properties of X-ray dual AGNs Among the 29 observed infrared luminous merging systems, signatures of dual AGNs with separations of > 0 5 were previously reported from X-ray observations for the following five sources: Mrk 463 bia08 , Mrk 739 kos11 , NGC 3393 fab11 , NGC 6240 kom03 , and IRAS 20210 + 1121 pic10 . Using our methods, we detect double nuclear emission in Mrk 463, Mrk 739, NGC 6240, and IRAS 20210 + 1121, but not in NGC 3393. Mrk 463, Mrk 739, and IRAS 20210 + 1121 show only one red ( K-L' 1.0 mag) nucleus, with a blue K-L' color ( < 1.0 mag) at the other nucleus.

If the detected X-ray emission from both nuclei originates in luminous AGNs, one nucleus in Mrk 463, Mrk 739, NGC 3393, and IRAS 20210 + 1121 could be a hot-dust-deficient AGN, in which case the contribution from AGN-heated hot dust emission to the observed L' band flux would be small, and the observed infrared K and L' band fluxes would be dominated by nuclear stellar-origin emission.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes X-ray spectra of Mrk 463, describing its best-fit components, Fe K emission or flux behavior, and comparison with previous modeling.

quotes.quote: Mrk 463 Extended emission was evident in the XMM-Newton field of view as indicated by the lower observed flux from the Chandra spectrum. Indeed, the XMM-Newton is likely contaminated by the double nucleus (see Bianchi et al. 2008), which the Chandra observation is able to resolve.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 463 as a two-nucleus galaxy whose PSPC profile suggests large-scale extended X-ray emission and whose spectrum fits a scattered power-law plus thermal model.

quotes.quote: Mrk 463 This galaxy has two nuclei, and one of them, Mrk 463E, has been observed to have broad optical lines in polarized light Mil90 .
The three-component model of a scattered power law and thermal emission fits Mrk 463.

The theoretical point spread function of the PSPC does not fit Mrk 463 well, suggesting that the X-ray emission is extended on scales of 48 kpc, but the observed radial profile is also consistent with a point source.

gmw_00001100.name: UGC 11185
gmw_00001100.overview: UGC 11185 is an interacting galaxy pair at an early merger stage, consisting of two Seyfert 2 galaxies separated by about 23 kpc [arXiv:1603.06589]. The system features a merger-induced tidal stream of ionized gas spanning between the components, with the northeastern galaxy hosting a 10-kpc-scale ionization cone [arXiv:1603.06589].

sources.summary: J1816NE is one member of the UGC 11185 merger; the paper finds AGN-photoionized narrow-line emission, a 10-kpc ionization cone, and extended clouds central to the analysis.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we focus on a local pair of AGNs, J181611.72+423941.6 and J181609.37+423923.0 (UGC 11185 NED02 and UGC 11185 NED01, and hereafter known as J1816NE and J1816SW) at z 0.04 (175 Mpc). Due to the wide separation between these interacting galaxies ( 23 kpc), observations of these objects provide a relatively rare glimpse of the concurrent growth of merging supermassive black holes at an early merger stage. J1816NE was included in a sample of objects with extended kpc-scale [ O 3 ] 5007-emitting clouds in keel2012 , who used optical spectroscopy for this object to describe the ionization and kinematics for some of the extended gas. Additionally, keel2015 used narrow- and medium-band Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) imaging and Fabry-Perot spectroscopy for the pair to provide evidence that the extended clouds in J1816NE were oriented in ionization cones potentially excited by the central AGN. Their data did not allow for a full exploration of the ionization as a function of spatial position across these cones, and the determination of this ionization structure is a central focus of this paper.

We use BPT line diagnostics to show that the full extent of the narrow line emission in both galaxies is photoionized by an AGN and confirm the existence of a 10-kpc-scale ionization cone in J1816NE, while in J1816SW the AGN narrow-line region is much more compact (1--2 kpc) and relatively undisturbed. Our observations also reveal the presence of ionized gas that nearly spans the entire distance between the galaxies which is likely in a merger-induced tidal stream.

sources.summary: J1816SW is the second UGC 11185 merger component, a Seyfert 2 galaxy whose AGN narrow-line region is compact, relatively undisturbed, and compared with J1816NE across the pair.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we focus on a local pair of AGNs, J181611.72+423941.6 and J181609.37+423923.0 (UGC 11185 NED02 and UGC 11185 NED01, and hereafter known as J1816NE and J1816SW) at z 0.04 (175 Mpc). Due to the wide separation between these interacting galaxies ( 23 kpc), observations of these objects provide a relatively rare glimpse of the concurrent growth of merging supermassive black holes at an early merger stage.
J1816SW has not been the focus of any detailed analysis, although both galaxies are listed as Seyfert 2 galaxies in the catalogue of veroncetty2010 .

We use BPT line diagnostics to show that the full extent of the narrow line emission in both galaxies is photoionized by an AGN and confirm the existence of a 10-kpc-scale ionization cone in J1816NE, while in J1816SW the AGN narrow-line region is much more compact (1--2 kpc) and relatively undisturbed. Our observations also reveal the presence of ionized gas that nearly spans the entire distance between the galaxies which is likely in a merger-induced tidal stream.

sources.summary: UGC 11185 is a serendipitously recognized EELR host with disturbed morphology, near-symmetric kinematics, and a weaker redshifted emission-line component near its nucleus.

quotes.quote: Of the remainder, Mkn 78 and Mkn 463 were selected in the targeted AGN sample, while Mkn 1498 and UGC 11185 were recognized only in the serendipitous survey.

Despite its very disturbed morphology, UGC 11185 shows near-symmetry in kinematics, with a very strong velocity gradient crossing the nucleus. Other systems in our sample show less ordered velocity slices.

UGC 11185 shows a second, weaker set of emission-line components near the nucleus, peaking about 1.8" to the east along our slit, roughly 600 km s ^ 1 to the red of the main peaks, and including about 1/4 of the nuclear [ O III ] flux within a 2 3 " aperture.

sources.summary: UGC 11185 has partially compromised IFU data but observed offset fields; the paper says its emission region could plausibly be biconical, with radial line-ratio changes stronger than azimuthal variation.

quotes.quote: Both UGC 11185 data runs were truncated by deteriorating conditions; we have only a single grating tilt for each IFU position, with 2 1200 second exposures, and these are affected significantly by cosmetic issues, so coverage of some spectral lines is incomplete and their flux scales are unreliable.

As listed in Table tbl-ifudata , we have observations centered on the nuclei of Mkn 1498 and NGC 5972, and sets of 2 overlapping offset fields for UGC 7342 and 11185; the table includes the requested offsets from the galaxy nucleus to the observed IFU center point.

In others, such as UGC 7342 and UGC 11185 Hainline , the entire emission region could plausibly show a biconical structure without any internal ionization differences. In these two systems, radial changes in the line ratio are greater than azimuthal variations at fixed projected radius.

gmw_00001101.name: SDSS 1430+13
gmw_00001101.overview: SDSS 1430+13, nicknamed the Teacup, is a bulge-dominated type-2 quasar [arXiv:2302.13884, arXiv:2301.10487] showing merger signatures like concentric stellar shells and tidal tails [arXiv:2111.13578, arXiv:2301.10487]. The galaxy hosts a compact radio jet [arXiv:2302.13884] and a giant nebula featuring ionized gas bubbles [arXiv:2309.02498, arXiv:2301.10487] and multiphase outflows [arXiv:2309.02498].

sources.summary: The paper uses ALMA observations of the Teacup to argue that its compact radio jet compresses and accelerates molecular gas, driving turbulent lateral outflow in this radio-quiet QSO2.

quotes.quote: In order to investigate the impact of radio jets on the interstellar medium (ISM) of galaxies hosting active galactic nuclei (AGN), we present subarcsecond resolution Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) CO(2-1) and CO(3-2) observations of the Teacup galaxy. This is a nearby ( D_ L =388 Mpc) radio-quiet type-2 quasar (QSO2) with a compact radio jet ( P_ jet 10 ^ 43 ) that subtends a small angle from the molecular gas disc. Enhanced emission line widths perpendicular to the jet orientation have been reported for several nearby AGN for the ionised gas. For the molecular gas in the Teacup, not only do we find this enhancement in the velocity dispersion but also a higher brightness temperature ratio ( ) perpendicular to the radio jet compared to the ratios found in the galaxy disc.

Among the QSO2s studied in blue RA22 , the Teacup (SDSS J143029.88+133912.0; J1430+1339) revealed a peculiar CO morphology and disturbed kinematics. This, in addition to the compact radio jet (extent of 0.8 kpc, PA=60 ) detected in Very Large Array (VLA) data at 0 25 resolution harrison15 , makes this object a promising target to study the jet-ISM interaction. It is hosted in a bulge-dominated galaxy showing signatures of a recent merger, including stellar shell-like features seen in the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) images keel15 . The nickname of the Teacup stems from the 12 kpc ionized gas bubble keel12 that is also detected in the radio with the VLA harrison15 , both shown in the left panel of Figure fig:hst .

sources.summary: The paper studies the Teacup as a type-2 QSO with ionised gas loop, radio bubbles, jet-driven ISM perturbation, multiphase outflows, and possible AGN-triggered star formation.

quotes.quote: The z 0.1 type-2 QSO J1430+1339, known as the ` Teacup ' , is a complex galaxy showing a loop of ionised gas 10 kpc in diameter , co-spatial radio bubbles, a compact ( 1 kpc) jet, and outflow activity. Its closeness offers the opportunity to study in detail the intricate interplay between the central supermassive black hole (SMBH) and the material in and around the galaxy, both the interstellar medium (ISM) and circumgalactic medium (CGM). We characterise the spatially resolved properties and effects of the galactic ionised gas outflow and compare them with those of the radio jet and with theoretical predictions to infer its acceleration mechanism. We used VLT/MUSE optical integral field spectroscopic observations to obtain flux, kinematic, and excitation maps of the extended (up to 100 kpc) ionised gas and to characterise the properties of stellar populations.

We detect a velocity dispersion enhancement ( 300 km/s) elongated over several kiloparsecs perpendicular to the radio jet, the active galactic nucleus (AGN) ionisation lobes, and the fast outflow, similar to what is found in other galaxies hosting compact, low-power jets, indicating that the jet strongly perturbs the host ISM during its passage. We observe a decreasing trend with distance from the nucleus for the outflow properties (mass outflow rate, kinetic rate, momentum rate). The mass outflow rate drops from around 100 M_ /yr in the inner 1--2 kpc to 0.1 M_ /yr at 30 kpc. The mass outflow rate of the ionised outflow is significantly higher ( 1--8 times ) than the molecular one, in contrast with what is often quoted in AGN.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1430, the Teacup, as a well-studied QSO2 with ionized outflow signatures, a 600 km/s velocity gradient, and misaligned ionized and molecular outflows.

quotes.quote: J1430 The maps corresponding to J1430 are shown in Fig.  fig:J1430_map . This QSO2 has been extensively studied in different wavelength ranges, and it is known as the Teacup. As mentioned in Section  extended , our kinematic maps are similar to those presented in Harrison14 , where Gemini/GMOS data were used, in Harrison15 , based on VLT/VIMOS data, in Ramos17 , based on VLT/SINFONI data, and most recently in Venturi23 , using VLT/MUSE observations. In the vel _p map, we see positive velocities to the north-east and negative to the south-west, having a total velocity gradient of 600  (see the top right panel of Fig.  fig:J1430_map ). Ramos17 reported a spatially resolved ionized outflow with a PA 72-74^ , which is comparable to the PA that we measure in this study ( 65^ ) for the approaching side of the outflow, which exhibits velocities reaching up to -1000 .

This is consistent with the blueshifted outflow reported by Harrison15 , oriented as the central small-scale radio jet. Venturi23 reported an enhancement of the velocity dispersion perpendicular to this radio jet, also found for the molecular gas ( Ramos22,Audibert23 ). Here we confirm these results and, furthermore, our observations reveal the receding side of the outflow, extending in the opposite direction, with a PA of approximately 200 ^ .

sources.summary: The Teacup AGN shows the strongest outflow signatures in the sample, a triangular higher-ionization region, and resolved line-profile asymmetries with blue and red wings extending up to 1000 km/s.

quotes.quote: Only in the Teacup AGN do we see outflow signatures of order 1000 km s ^ 1 .
Most notably, in the Teacup AGN there is a reasonably well-defined triangular region of higher ionization (where the ``hole" lies within it).

The Teacup AGN was shown to have resolved outflow signatures by . We find double or asymmetric profiles extending 2 \ north, 4 \ west, >1.5 \ south, and 2 \ east in a roughly circular zone. What appears as an [ O III ] peak in the center of the loop on the underlying HST image may be affected by residuals from cosmic-ray events. The Gemini data are better at detecting emission which is diffuse and at low surface brightness, as in the center of the loop, and show no distinct emission-line component there. Blue wings dominate east of the nucleus, red wings to the west; in many locations they extend to 1000 km s ^ 1 from the systemic velocity.

sources.summary: The Teacup AGN is a low-redshift type 2 quasar with an ionized-gas handle, bipolar radio superbubbles, a jet-like outflow feature, and a highly obscured luminous central quasar.

quotes.quote: One low-redshift Type 2 quasar ( z=0.0852 ) that has received attention in the literature is SDSS J143029.88 +133912.0. The object has been labelled the ``Teacup AGN'' due to a ``handle'' of ionized gas extending 10 kpc to the east of the galaxy core, initially identified in SDSS images by Galaxy Zoo (Massimo Mezzoprete, 2007; Lintott08 ) and later confirmed by HST imaging ( Keel12a,Keel15 ). Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array (VLA) data subsequently revealed that radio emission traces this ``eastern bubble'', and that this is just one of two bi-polar radio superbubbles, extending to distances of 10 kpc in opposite directions ( Harrison15 ). A 1 kpc small jet-like feature was also identified, spatially co-incident with a 700 km s ^ 1 outflow of warm ionised gas, along the same orientation axis as the eastern bubble ( Harrison15 ).

Modelling the and spectra of the central quasar, we find it to be highly obscured, with N_ H =(4.2 - 6.5) 10^ 23 , and intrinsically luminous, with L_ 2 10 keV = (0.8 - 1.4) 10^ 44 . The quasar is inferred to be currently accreting at a high bolometric luminosity ( L_ bol 10^ 45 - 10^ 46 ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1430, the Teacup, as a bulge-dominated post-interaction QSO2 with shells, dust lanes, jet-like continuum, and double-peaked CO shaped by feedback.

quotes.quote: SDSS J143029.88+133912.0 (J1430; The Teacup) Often referred to as the Teacup galaxy, J1430 has been widely studied in different wavelength ranges including the X-rays 2018ApJ...856L...1L , optical 2012MNRAS.420..878K,2015ApJ...800...45H,2018MNRAS.474.2302V , NIR 2017MNRAS.470..964R and radio 2019MNRAS.485.2710J . The host galaxy is bulge-dominated, showing clear signatures of a past interaction. These include a spectacular system of concentric shells and nuclear dust lanes (see Figure fig1 and 2015AJ....149..155K ).

Our ALMA data reveal continuum emission at 1.3 mm peaking in the middle of two CO(2 1) blobs, as can be seen from the top panel of Figure fig6aa . This continuum emission looks more jet-like than in the case of the other QSO2s in our sample and it has an almost E-W orientation, perpendicular to the CO emission. From the analysis of the continuum image we derive a deconvolved size of 0.37 0.23 (590 370 pc ^2 ) and PA=80 12 , almost coincident with the major axis of the 1.4 GHz continuum emission measured from FIRST (77 ; 2014MNRAS.441.3306H ).

sources.summary: The paper uses J1430+1339 as a representative compact radio source, finding a blue emission-line spectrum dominated by young/intermediate populations and evidence for recent star formation.

quotes.quote: In Fig. fig:fit1 we show the synthesis results for the source J1430+1339. The spectrum of this object displays a steep blue continuum and strong [ O ii 3727 emission, both features frequently employed as indicators of recent star formation activity K98,R02,We07 . Prominent nebular emission lines of H i , [ O iii ] 4959,5007, [ N ii ] 6548,6584, and [ O i ] 6300,6363 are also evident in the spectrum. As we can see from the right-side panel of Fig. fig:fit1 , young/intermediate-age stellar populations contribute with 75 of the integrated flux of the source, while old stars contribute with less than 10 .

The source have mean age Log t _L = 7.9 Gyr, solar metallicity ( Z = 0.02) and low internal reddening (A _v =0.21 mag). Such results along with the high star formation rate (19.2M _ /yr ) estimated in Section 4.2, suggest that J1430 + 1339 experienced recent episodes of star formation.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1430+1339 as a quasar host whose inner 1 kpc jet affects cold molecular gas; its CO emission remains central without extended structures.

quotes.quote: Near-infrared spectroscopy of J1430+1339 and J0945+1737 further reveals multiple ionized outflow components through different gas tracers ramosAlmeida17, speranza22 . Furthermore, for J1430+1339, there is evidence that the small scale inner 1 kpc jet ( harrison15 ) influences both the kinematics and excitation state of the cold molecular gas, as traced with CO (2--1) kinematics and CO (3--2)/CO (2--1) emission-line ratios ramosAlmeida22,audibert23 .
In two of the galaxies, J0945+1737 (panel a1) and J1430+1339 (panel d1), we see that the CO emission is only observed in a central, contiguous region.

Using this systematic approach, for J0945+1737 and J1430+1339, we do not identify any extended molecular gas structures away from the central emission. This confirms the observation made from the total CO emission-line images shown in Figure fig2: all4targets . These two quasars are hence not included in our analysis pertaining to the extended CO gas structures.

sources.summary: SDSS 1430+13, the Teacup AGN, is described as having an ionized-gas loop, photoionized gas, possible reversed central gradient, and radio structure matching the loop.

quotes.quote: This is illustrated in Fig. fig-sdssteacup , isolating the emission-line loop in SDSS 1430+13 (nicknamed the Teacup AGN because of this structure).

Using the IRAF task temden , which implements the algorithm of , and considering n_e < 100 cm ^ 3 , we find T_e values of 18,600 1000 in the SDSS 2201+11, 13,300 300 for Mkn 266, and 15,400 500 for the Teacup system. These confirm that the gas is photoionized rather than shocked; for comparison, temperatures in the [ O III ] zone of supernova remnants (including some with lower ionization levels than in these clouds) range from 20,000-69,000 K (e.g., Fesen82 , Wallerstein , Morse95 ).

SDSS 1430+13, the ``Teacup" AGN, is distinguished by a 5-kpc loop of ionized gas. The Faint Images of the Radio Sky at Twenty-one centimeters (FIRST) Very Large Array data at 20 cm show extended structure roughly coextensive with this feature, possibly indicating a related origin.

sources.summary: The paper observed J1430+1339 on two nights, but contradictory ANOVA behavior and limited comparison stars left insufficient evidence for microvariability.

quotes.quote: J1430+1339 was observed on March 28 and March 30, 2011. During the two observing nights, both the target and the comparison star exhibited very contradictory results from ANOVA. We were unable to find a comparison star that was not variable at = 0.001 , although the p- values were likewise too low to confirm variability. Unfortunately, the field around this particular target limited the choice of comparison stars we could have used for ANOVA. In conclusion, there is not enough evidence of microvariability in this source.

sources.summary: The Teacup is included to map its giant nebula and is described as having studied extended radio emission, kiloparsec-scale jet structure, 19 kpc AGN-related emission, and clear ionised bubbles.

quotes.quote: Although not in our VLA program, we also included the Teacup in this sample to map the morphology of the giant ( 110 kpc) nebula discovered by . The extended radio emission of this QSO2 has been studied in detail by and .

The jet sizes are in the range 1-few kpc (J0945+17, J1000+12, J1356+10, the Teacup), 10 kpc (J1517+33), several tens of kpc (J0841+01, J0853+38, J0907+46). Additional extended AGN related radio structures (e.g. lobes or radio bubbles, plumes, hot spots) are detected in J0853+38 (472 kpc), J0841+01 (40 kpc), J1000+12 (43 kpc), J1356+10 (160 kpc), the Teacup (19 kpc). The radio component at 11 kpc from the AGN in J0945+17 may be a jet or a lobe. The lobe emission from the 160 kpc radio source in J1356+10 is extremely faint, and additional observations are necessary to unambiguously confirm and accurately image this emission. The structure of the extended radio source in J1437+33 ( d_ max 2.5 kpc) is unknown, but, as we argue in Appendix results-objects2 , it is most likely related to the nuclear activity. Therefore, this sample shows a high fraction of AGN related radio sources that extend for at least typical effective radii of the spheroidal component of galaxies ( Urbano2019 ) and often much more. Size, line luminosity and nature of the large scale ionised gas We have found that QSO2 undergoing interaction/merger events appear to be invariably associated with ionised gas spread over large spatial scales, often well outside the main body of the galaxies, with maximum distances from the AGN in the range r_ max 12-92 kpc, with a median value of 46 kpc. The most spectacular case is the Teacup, which is associated with a 115 87 kpc nebula (Fig. figTeacup ).

sources.summary: The paper describes the Teacup galaxy as a type-2 quasar with a giant EELR, ionized-gas bubbles, shell and tidal-tail merger signatures, and uncertain AGN fading.

quotes.quote: One of the largest EELR among low-redshift radio-quiet AGN was recently found around Teacup galaxy (SDSS J143029.88+133912.0). The galaxy was discovered by volunteers of the Galaxy Zoo project, its EELR was confirmed in follow-up spectroscopic observations Gagne2011AAS...21714212G . This type-2 quasar was nicknamed due to the morphology of ionized gas bubbles that extend 10 kpc of the galactic centre in the shape of `handle'. Keel2017ApJ...835..256K considered Teacup as a fading AGN, whereas X-ray data have indicated that it is possible that no fading is required Lansbury2018ApJ...856L...1L . The Hubble Space Telescope (HST) imaging shows that the Teacup is a bulge-dominated galaxy, with a shell-like structure and a tidal tail, which have been interpreted as an indicative of merger 1--2 Gyr ago with a cold disk system and 1/10 mass ratio Keel2015AJ....149..155K .

sources.summary: J1430+1339 uses archival CO(3-2), is the only high-J CO detection among seven sources, and has enhanced CO temperature ratios perpendicular to the radio jet.

quotes.quote: For the one remaining target, J1430+1339, we utilise archival CO(3-2) ACA observations (proposal ID: 2016.1.01535.S [ PI: Lansbury ] ) taken on 03 November 2016.

In addition to the literature samples mentioned previously we also include high- z quasars ( z 1 -- 6) compiled by , to make comparisons to the low- z counterparts in our sample. b 0.49 b 0.49 Out of the 7 sources observed, 6 show non-detections and only 1 source, J1430+1339, has a detection in either CO(6-5) or CO(7-6), based on the same criteria stated in Section sec:spectra .

Further supporting this idea, two of this sample have been studied in a resolved way in which the CO velocity dispersion was observed to be effected perpendicular to radio jets J1316+1737 Girdhar22 and CO temperature ratios were enhanced perpendicular to the radio jet J1430+1339 Audibert23 .

gmw_00001102.name: SDSS 2201+11
gmw_00001102.overview: SDSS 2201+11 is an active galaxy featuring photoionized gas with high electron temperatures [arXiv:1110.6921] and an ionizing-energy shortfall exceeding a factor of 100 [arXiv:1612.06006]. The system displays symmetric rotating gas velocities [arXiv:1110.6921] and a dust lane extending into its northern cloud [arXiv:1612.06006].

sources.summary: SDSS 2201+11 has emission-line structures traced by imaging, photoionized gas with high electron temperature, substantial radial ionization changes, and symmetric rotating gas velocities.

quotes.quote: These data are particularly helpful in tracing the emission-line structures of UGC 7342 (Fig. fig-sarapix1 ) and SDSS 2201+11 (Fig. fig-sarapix2 ).

Using the IRAF task temden , which implements the algorithm of , and considering n_e < 100 cm ^ 3 , we find T_e values of 18,600 1000 in the SDSS 2201+11, 13,300 300 for Mkn 266, and 15,400 500 for the Teacup system. These confirm that the gas is photoionized rather than shocked; for comparison, temperatures in the [ O III ] zone of supernova remnants (including some with lower ionization levels than in these clouds) range from 20,000-69,000 K (e.g., Fesen82 , Wallerstein , Morse95 ).
Despite the angular offset from the edge-on disk, the gas velocities in SDSS 2201+11 are continuous with the pattern in the inner rotating section, and closely symmetric.

sources.summary: SDSS 2201+11 has an ionizing-energy shortfall above a factor of 100, a narrow 25-degree ionization structure, and a dust lane showing its northern cloud lies on the front side.

quotes.quote: For UGC 7342 and SDSS 2201+11, the shortfall exceeds a factor 100.
In SDSS 2201+11, we derive 25 ^ , although this may be affected by obscuration within the host galaxy on one side of each cloud.

In SDSS 2201+11, a dust lane is continuous with the emission-line material into the northern cloud, showing that the northern cloud must be on the front side of the system in order to give significant dust attenuation.

gmw_00001103.name: PKS 0426-380
gmw_00001103.overview: PKS 0426-380 is a distant flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:2305.06729, arXiv:2310.10206] at a redshift of approximately 1.11 [arXiv:1410.1556, arXiv:1004.3767] that exhibits a core-jet structure pointing west-southwest [arXiv:2310.10206]. The blazar displays strong gamma-ray variability [arXiv:1607.02053] and has shown two distinct optical-infrared color behaviors [arXiv:1504.00837].

sources.summary: The paper reevaluates PKS 0426-380 as a blazar with reported high-energy QPV; unlike most targets, no possible local-significance QPV is found in its updated light curve.

quotes.quote: At high energies (HE; E>100 MeV) five blazars (PG 1553+113, PKS 2155-304, PKS 0426-380, PKS 0537-441 and PKS 0301-243) have been reported to show year-like quasi-periodic variations (QPVs) with significance >3 . As these findings are based on few cycles only, care needs to be taken to properly account for random variations which can produce intervals of seemingly periodic behaviour. We present results of an updated timing analysis for six blazars (adding PKS 0447-439 to the above), utilizing suitable methods to evaluate their longterm variability properties and to search for QPVs in their light curves. We generate ray light curves covering almost ten years, study their timing properties and search for QPVs using the Lomb-Scargle Periodogram and the Wavelet Z-transform.

(1) Comparing their probability density functions (PDFs), all sources (except PG 1553+113) exhibit a clear deviation from a Gaussian distribution, but are consistent with being log-normal, suggesting that the underlying variability is of a non-linear, multiplicative nature. (2) Apart from PKS 0301-243 the power spectral density (PSD) for all investigated blazars is close to flicker noise (power-law slope 1 ). (3) Possible QPVs with a local significance 3 are found in all light curves (apart from PKS 0426-380 and PKS 0537-441), with observed periods between (1.7-2.8) yr. The evidence is strongly reduced, however, if evaluated in terms of a global significance.

sources.summary: PKS 0426-380 is described as a reclassified FSRQ with very-high-energy gamma-ray detections, possible quasi-periodicity, and an IC-190504A error-ellipse association.

quotes.quote: PKS 0426-380 is a reclassified FSRQ at redshift z 1.111 Heidt2004,Sbarufatti2005 , associated with the Fermi LAT source 4FGL J0428.6-3756.
