This source is located within the 90 error ellipse of the neutrino IC-190504A. Tanaka2013 reported the Fermi LAT detection of two very-high-energy ( E 100 GeV) gamma-ray photons from the directional vicinity of PKS 0426-380. Zhang2017b found a possible gamma-ray quasi-periodicity of this blazar with a period of 3.35 0.68 yr at the significance level of approximately 3.6 .

PKS 0426-380 is also located within the 90 error ellipse of IC-190504A ( T_ = MJD 58607.77 ), 1.47 away from its best-fit sky positions. We show the one-year long likelihood light curve of PKS 0426-380 in Fig fig:pks0426 along with the photon index as function of time. The neutrino came in the Fermi LAT bin MJD 58607.8 2.7 , that has an average flux of (1.97 0.41) 10^ 7 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper includes PKS 0426-380 as a distant LBL or possible FSRQ with Fermi LAT photons above 100 GeV, modeling lobe photon-ALP mixing at low energies.

quotes.quote: PKS 0426-380 This distant LBL object (or possibly FSRQ, ) is located at a redshift of z = 1.111 . No measurement of the source with any IACT is reported in the literature so far. Nevertheless, it has been observed with the Fermi LAT and a dedicated analysis has revealed two photons with energies above 100 GeV ( > 1 ) that can be associated with the source with high significance . The blazar is variable and the averaged observed spectrum during the flaring episodes can be described with a power law with index _ obs = 2.72 0.17 above 8 GeV with mild evidence for a spectral flattening above 10 GeV .
This source is included in order to test the sensitivity to a ray boost at low energies. The large redshift leads to an energy for which = 4 of E_ = 4 235 GeV.

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 0426-380 as a high-redshift BL Lac with VHE photons, possible microlensing, strong variability, and expected observability with CTA under current EBL models.

quotes.quote: A second approach uses 5 sources (PKS 0426-380, 4C +55.17, Ton 116, PG 1246+586 and RBS 1432) which were found to exhibit very high energy emission ( E_ >100 GeV). Through Monte Carlo simulations it is shown that the observation of VHE photons, despite the large distances of these objects, is consistent with current EBL models. Many of these sources would be observable with the upcoming ground based observatory the Cherenkov Telescope Array (CTA), leading to a better understanding of the EBL.

DBSCAN VHE Sources PKS 0426-380: Classed as a BL Lac in the 3FGL and situated in the southern hemisphere at RA=67.18 ^ , Dec=-37.93 ^ . The 2FHL redshift is quoted as z=1.111 as found in , where the existence of a closer source at z=0.559 implies micro-lensing may be present. This source has previously been noted for producing VHE photons in and , which both use Pass 7 data.

sources.summary: The VLT spectrum gives PKS 0426-380 multiple emission-line redshifts near z=1.1, notes a large MgII velocity offset, and finds the strongest MgII luminosity in the sample.

quotes.quote: PKS 0426-380 The only spectral feature detected by stickel93 is the intervening system at z=1.030. Consistently with the high redshift urry00 did not detect the host galaxy in their HST image. heidt04 proposed z=1.111 based on the detection of a single emission line identified with the MgII 2798 . In our spectrum we detect MgII 2798 \ at z=1.112, and also CIII ] and [ OII ] 3727 \ at z=1.098 and 1.099, respectively. There is a velocity difference of 1800 km s ^ 1 between MgII and the other lines.
This source exhibits the largest luminosity for the MgII emission line of the objects examined here.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0426-380 as a z=1.11 bright GeV source with a soft gamma-ray spectrum, SSC/EC interpretation, and difficult ground-based detectability.

quotes.quote: PKS 0426-380 at z=1.11 is a bright source in the GeV energy band, with the flux reaching 10^ 10  erg/cm ^2 s. Its \ spectrum is relatively soft and is consistent either with a powerlaw with photon index 2.5 or with a cut-off powerlaw type spectrum. The \ measurements are compared to the multiwavelength data on the source in Fig. fig:0426 . Similarly to the case of RBS 76, the overall spectrum of the source could be interpreted in terms of the synchrotron self-Compton or synchrotron external-Compton type models with radio-to-optical emission being produced via the synchrotron mechanism and X-ray-to- \ emission being produced via inverse Compton scattering. Extremely high redshift of the source and possible presence of the high-energy cut-off in the spectrum might make detection of the source with ground-based \ telescopes at the energies significantly larger than 100 GeV difficult.

sources.summary: PKS 0426-380 shows two distinct optical-infrared color behaviors, with separate J-R light-curve traces and intersecting color-magnitude curves.

quotes.quote: A few sources show two or more different strips in the Magnitude-Magnitude or Color-Magnitude diagrams. PKS 0426-380 shows two clear different behaviors. In J-R diagram, it seems to be a gap of two light curves with different traces. In the Color-Magnitude diagram, there is also an intersection of two curves. One exhibits BWB trend, and another exhibits RWB trend.

sources.summary: The paper reports PKS 0426-380 as a gamma-ray flaring blazar with one 2010 XMM-Newton observation below the detection limit for genuine IDV.

quotes.quote: PKS 0426-380 The blazar shows high variation in GeV band on long time scales (Neronov et al. 2015) and the source flux enhanced by a factor of 2--3 during its flaring activity. This is the most distant HE gamma-ray emitting source so far which is reported in Tanaka et al. (2013) detected by Fermi LAT instrument in January 2013. During this period the source was in flaring state in gamma-ray. This source has single observation with XMM--Newton taken in 2010. The observation has SNR < 2 i.e. the source was below the detection limit and no genuine IDV can be reported.

sources.summary: The paper classifies PKS 0426 380 as an FSRQ TeV blazar and reports a log-parabolic XMM-Newton spectrum with significant concave curvature near 2.1 keV.

quotes.quote: PKS 0426 380: The source is classified as a FSRQ (i.e. Ghisellini et al. 2011; Sbarrato et al. 2012). Tanaka et al. (2013) reported the discovery of the Very High Energy ray emission from this blazar. We analyzed the XMM Newton pointing of the blazar in 2012 and found it to be well described with a log-parabolic model which showed significant concave spectrum. The spectrum is also fitted with the broken power law and the upturn break energy is found to be at around 2.1 keV. We fit the spectra by making N_ H as free parameter but did not get any significant improvement in ^ 2 values. This the first occasion where the spectral flattening is found for this blazar.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes PKS 0426-380 as a distant FSRQ, finding a gamma-ray quasi-periodicity of 3.35 +/- 0.68 years at 3.6 sigma significance.

quotes.quote: In this work we analyze the gamma-ray data from the distant FSRQ PKS 0426-380 in the interval between 2008 August and 2016 December. We find a significant quasi-periodicity in the ray flux. The significance of the 3.35 0.68 years period is 3.6 \ .

sources.summary: The paper calls 0426 380 the furthest known VHE-emitting FSRQ, notes its TANAMI X-band history, and finds a west-southwest S-band core-jet structure.

quotes.quote: 0426 380: 0426 380 is currently the furthest known VHE emitting FSRQ at z=1.1 tanaka13 . The source has been part of the TANAMI X-band sample since 2009 , and first images were published in müller18 . 0426 380 shows a core--jet structure with the jet pointing towards the west--southwest.

gmw_00001104.name: 3C 270.1
gmw_00001104.overview: The radio-loud quasar 3C 270.1 is a kinetically dominated active galactic nucleus featuring a one-sided relativistic jet [arXiv:1507.01579, arXiv:2011.03130]. It is surrounded by an overdensity of red neighboring galaxies [arXiv:1605.03196, arXiv:1710.02360] and exhibits asymmetric radio morphology with strong core and hot spot emission [arXiv:1605.03196, arXiv:2011.03130].

sources.summary: The paper presents the first EUV spectrum of the kinetically dominated quasar 3C 270.1, finds a steep EUV continuum, corrects its redshift, and rejects BAL classifications.

quotes.quote: This letter presents the first extreme ultraviolet (EUV) spectrum of a kinetically dominated quasar, 3C 270.1. The EUV continuum flux density of 3C 270.1 is very steep, F_ ^ _ EUV , _ EUV =2.98 0.15 . This value is consistent with the correlation of Q /L_ bol and _ EUV found in previous studies of the EUV continuum of quasars, the EUV deficit of radio loud quasars.

The quasar 3C 270.1 contains one of the most powerful jets of any known active galactic nucleus. The jet power greatly exceeds any other associated with a quasar that has been claimed to have an ultraviolet (UV) broad absorption line. These extreme properties of 3C 270.1 offer a unique laboratory for exploring the mechanism of relativistic jet formation.

In the second section, it was shown that the CIV and OVI absorption had BI =0 . Thus, 3C 270.1 is not a broad absorption line quasar as classically defined, but displays the well known associated absorption that occurs in many RLQs.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 270.1 as a radio-loud quasar with a 374 kpc jet, strong core and hot spots, significant submillimeter and X-ray non-thermal emission, and associated C IV absorption.

quotes.quote: -3C 270.1 has a jet of 374 kpc and a relatively high core contribution (22 ) at 5 GHz. Multi-frequency radio images of 3C 270.1 indicate the presence of strong core and hot spots Liu1992 and a one-sided jet Hilbert2016 . The radio emission is best fitted with a double power-law with a cutoff at FIR wavelengths resulting in significant non-thermal contribution at submm wavelengths. The emission from the torus is best described with a combination of clumps with a small filling factor and a homogeneous disk with the highest acceptable opacity in the torus library. This combination with a 52^ results in little reddening of the accretion disk emission.

3C 270.1 spectra indicate the presence of a strong absorption complex ( >6.17 ) associated with C 4 absorption line Anderson1987 . The best-fitted accretion disk model indicates the presence of significant non-thermal emission at X-ray waveband. Chandra observations find extended X-ray emission that is co-spatial with the radio lobe and peaks at the position of the hot spots Wilkes2011 .

sources.summary: 3C 270.1 is a bright unresolved QSO surrounded by red neighboring sources, with asymmetric radio morphology and core-plus-lobe structure.

quotes.quote: 3C 270.1 (z=1.528) 3C 270.1 appears as a bright unresolved source surrounded by many dimmer sources of various morphologies. There are at least 5 sources within 4 of the target. Of these, only one is detected in our optical image. Most of the sources in the region surrounding the object are very red. The radio morphology is not symmetric with respect to the center of the QSO.

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C 270.1 as an individual environment case where IRAC and optical/IR studies show red galaxy overdensities around the z~1.5 radio source.

quotes.quote: A more elaborate study by Haas09 of 3C 270.1 at z=1.54 involves Spitzer /IRAC observations in combination with deep z and y band imaging ( 26 AB mag) with the 6.5 m MMT at Mt. \ Hopkins McLeod06, Brown08 . This strategy, which brackets the Balmer break at the expected cluster redshift, is relatively insensitive to photometric errors in the IRAC bands, which easily may exceed 10 . Fitting zy +IRAC photometry with two simple galaxy templates (elliptical and ULIRG) revealed a central overdensity of intrinsically red z 1.54 galaxies around the radio source.

Clues on the limited sensitivity of our methods may come from the individual case of 3C 270.1 at z=1.5 , where the PSO-IRAC color cut failed to reveal a central (30 ) overdensity, the IRAC cut yields a marginal overdensity, and both cuts find an extended overdensity in the radial bin at 30 and 50 (Fig. fig_single_radial_densities_without_overdensity , Table table_overdensities ). Both the elaborated IRAC environment study of 3C 270.1 with supplementary deep z and y band imaging ( 26 AB mag) and photometric redshift determination from SED fitting of all sources in the field Haas09 and the IRAC1/2 counts-in-cells analysis of the 3C 270.1 field Galametz12 show an overdensity within 50 and 60 radius, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 270.1 as a bright BAL quasar with little intrinsic absorption, a core-dominated FR II radio structure, a one-sided parsec-scale jet, and X-rays dominated by inverse-Compton jet emission.

quotes.quote: The brighter of our two quasars, 3C 270.1, shows no evidence of intrinsic absorption, but the Fe K line is marginally detected wilkes .
3C 270.1 is a core-dominated FR II object on VLA scales. The high-resolution 8.4 and 15.4 GHz VLBA observations resolve the core of 3C 270.1 into a one-sided core-jet structure.
We argue that in the case of radio luminous 3C 270.1 a non-thermal, inverse-Compton emission from the innermost parts of the radio jet can account for a large amount of the observed X-ray emission.

sources.summary: The paper describes J122033.87+334312.0 as 3C 270.1, a double-lobed steep-spectrum radio-loud quasar with known low-velocity C IV absorption and archival Chandra coverage.

quotes.quote: J122033.87+334312.0 (also 3C 270.1) is a double-lobed steep-spectrum RLQ with the second-highest radio-loudness ( R^ * =4.2 ) in the snapshot or archival BAL RLQ sample. Low-velocity C IV absorption has been known to be present in this object for some time (e.g., Anderson et al. 1987) although it has not necessarily been described as a BAL quasar; however, the balnicity index measured from the SDSS data is non-zero ( BI=52.5 ; Gibson et al. 2009). J122033.87+334312.0 was observed serendipitously in a 3 ks ACIS-S exposure (ObsID 2118; PI Cagnoni).

gmw_00001105.name: J0126 0839
gmw_00001105.overview: J0126 0839 is a changing-look quasar host galaxy with a De Vaucouleurs morphology [arXiv:1903.08122]. It is surrounded by a shell-like structure and features a large tidal tail connected to a region of diffuse emission [arXiv:1903.08122].

sources.summary: J0126 0839 is modeled as a CL quasar host with shell-like structure, diffuse emission connected to a tidal tail, and De Vaucouleurs-favored morphology.

quotes.quote: J0126 0839 This galaxy appears to be surrounded by a shell-like structure, which could be indicative of past mergers 1992ApJ...399L.117H . This galaxy appears to have a large patch of diffuse emission to its Northwest (circled in Figure fig:3_colour ). This diffuse emission region is spatially separated from the bulge and disk of the host galaxy, but appears to form the end of a large tidal tail connected to the galaxy in the residuals of all three bands.

gmw_00001106.name: J0159+0033
gmw_00001106.overview: SDSS J0159+0033 is a changing-look active galactic nucleus at redshift z=0.31 [arXiv:1412.2136, arXiv:2303.02289] that transitioned from a Type 1 quasar to a Type 1.9 AGN [arXiv:1412.2136, arXiv:2303.02289]. The host galaxy has an irregular shape with faint southwest emission that may represent tidal debris [arXiv:1903.08122].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J015957.64+003310.5 as a z=0.31 changing-look quasar whose optical and X-ray continuum dimmed without evidence that absorption caused the transition.

quotes.quote: SDSS J015957.64+003310.5 is an X-ray selected, z=0.31 AGN from the Stripe 82X survey that transitioned from a Type 1 quasar to a Type 1.9 AGN between 2000 and 2010. This is the most distant AGN, and first quasar, yet observed to have undergone such a dramatic change. We re-observed the source with the double spectrograph on the Palomar 5m telescope in July 2014 and found that the spectrum is unchanged since 2010. From fitting the optical spectra, we find that the AGN flux dropped by a factor of 6 between 2000 and 2010 while the broad H emission faded and broadened.

Serendipitous X-ray observations caught the source in both the bright and dim state, showing a similar 2-10 keV flux diminution as the optical while lacking signatures of obscuration. The optical and X-ray changes coincide with g band magnitude variations over multiple epochs of Stripe 82 observations. We demonstrate that variable absorption, as might be expected from the simplest AGN unification paradigm, does not explain the observed photometric or spectral properties.

sources.summary: The manuscript models SDSS J0159 as a TDE candidate in an AGN host, deriving a lower central black-hole mass from host stellar features and long optical variability.

quotes.quote: In this manuscript, central BH mass is determined in the tidal disruption event (TDE) candidate SDSS J0159, through the nine years long variabilities, in order to check whether the virial BH mass is consistent with the mass estimated by another independent methods. First, host galaxy spectroscopic features are described by 350 simple stellar templates, to confirm the total stellar mass about 7 10^ 10 M_ in SDSS J0159, indicating the virial BH mass about two magnitudes larger than the BH mass estimated by the total stellar mass. Second, based on an efficient method and fitting procedure, through theoretical TDE model applied to describe the SDSS ugriz band light curves of SDSS J0159, central BH mass can be determined as M_ BH 4.5_ 1.1 ^ +1.3 10^6 M_ , well consistent with the M-sigma relation expected BH mass and the total stellar mass expected BH mass.

SDSS J0159 (=SDSS J015957.64+003310.5) at redshift z=0.312 (corresponding luminosity distance about 1625Mpc) is an interesting object in the literature. lc15 have reported the SDSS J0159 as the first changing-look quasar transitioned from a Type 1 quasar (both apparent broad H and H in optical spectrum) to a Type 1.9 AGN (Active Galactic Nuclei) (only weak broad H in optical spectrum) between 2000 and 2010, and reported the dimming of the AGN continuum as intrinsic physical reason to explain the changing-look properties. Meanwhile, md15 have reported the SDSS J0159 as a candidate of Tidal Disruption Event (TDE) due to the very rapid rise and the decay trend of the long-term optical variabilities well described by t^ 5/3 expected by theoretical TDE model, indicating TDEs can be well treated as one explanation to changing-look AGN as discussed in yw18, zh21b .
Therefore, the central TDE in SDSS J0159 is a central TDE in AGN host galaxy, quiet different from the vast majority of the reported optical TDEs candidates in quiescent galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper discusses SDSS J0159 as a changing-look AGN or TDE candidate with composite SF+AGN line ratios, lower absorption-line black hole mass, and double narrow-line components.

quotes.quote: Two other sources, namely PGC 015953 and SDSS J0159 (see also Merloni2015 and Section sec:individualobjects ), are unambiguously identified as composite SF+AGN nuclei, while the rest is in the SF region of the diagrams.

SDSS J0159 SDSS J0159 was identified by Lamassa2015 as a changing-look AGN, changing appearance in its optical spectrum with the broad Balmer lines disappearing as the X-ray luminosity and AGN continuum flux decreased by a factor of 6. These authors derive a black hole mass based on the FWHM of the broad component of the H line of 2 10 ^8 M _ . On the other hand, Merloni2015 argue that this event is consistent with a TDE based on the lightcurve evolution and energetics. Here we determine the velocity dispersion based on absorption features, which implies a black hole mass of 10 ^ 7.2 M _ , significantly lower than the broad line estimate of Lamassa2015 . This could suggest that the broad transient emission lines were the optical signature of a TDE, in which case no clear correlation between M _ BH and the emission line FWHM would be expected.

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J0159 as a changing-look AGN and TDE host, measuring about 81 km/s stellar velocity dispersion and arguing stellar debris can explain its overestimated virial black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: In this Letter, properties of black hole (BH) mass are well checked for the interesting object SDSS J0159, a changing-look AGN and also a host galaxy of a tidal disruption event (TDE). Through spectral absorption features, the stellar velocity dispersion of SDSS J0159 can be well measured as 81 km/s , leading to SDSS J0159 being an apparent outlier in the space, because of the BH mass estimated through the relation about two magnitudes lower than the reported virial BH mass of about 10^8 M_ . After considerations of contributions of stellar debris from the central TDE to broad line emission clouds, the over-estimated virial BH mass could be well explained in SDSS J0159.

Therefore, we finally accepted =(81.4 26.7) km/s as the best estimate to the stellar velocity dispersion of SDSS J0159 through the direct SSP method, even though the spectrum has lower spectral quality. Therefore, with full considerations of existence of the power law continuum emissions, STARLIGHT code also leads to 80 km/s of SDSS J0159. The two different methods lead to the similar stellar velocity dispersion after considerations of the truely existed power law continuum emissions in the spectrum, indicating the measured value should be reliable to some extent.

sources.summary: The paper studies SDSS J0159+0033 as a massive star-forming galaxy whose changing-look flare is interpreted as a tidal disruption event with residual AGN-like accretion.

quotes.quote: Source identification and long term evolution The main focus of this paper is the object SDSS J015957.64+003310.5, hereafter SDSS J0159+0033. SDSS J0159+0033 first came to our attention (before being independently reported by lamassa15 ) whilst creating a reference sample of bright X-ray sources to guide target selection for the SDSS-IV/SPIDERS survey. Within this subset of 35 objects there was a single one, SDSS J0159+0033, for which the automated classification derived from the SDSS-I/II spectrum ( CLASS=`QSO' ) disagreed with that derived from the SDSS-III spectrum ( CLASS=`GALAXY' ).

The pipeline measurements derived from the SDSS photometry found SDSS J0159+0033 to be a marginally resolved galaxy having de-reddened ugri colours ( u-g=0.53 , g-=0.79 , i=0.31 ) that placed it outside the main stellar locus, blueward of the colour regions used to reject `normal' galaxies, and also outside additional colour boxes designed to reject rarer stellar contaminants. On 2010 January 5 (MJD 55201), SDSS J0159+0033 was observed spectroscopically by the Baryon Acoustic Oscillations Survey BOSS, dawson13 within the SDSS-III project, as part of a small random sub-set of known broad line AGN from SDSS that were re-observed in order to define QSO spectral templates for BOSS (targeting flag TEMPLATE QSO SDSS ).

sources.summary: J0159+0033 lacks dramatic tidal tails but has irregular shape and faint southwest emission after continuum subtraction, possibly tidal debris.

quotes.quote: J0159+0033 J0159+0033 does not show the dramatic features (tidal tails, shells) indicating recent mergers that the other galaxies in our sample do. The overall shape of the galaxy appears slightly irregular, concave toward the Southeast. Subtraction of the galaxy's continuum reveals faint emission extending 20 kpc to the Southwest in and i (circled in Figure fig:3_colour ) that is responsible for its apparent shape. There is some evidence for this feature in the g residuals, but it is noticeably fainter. Given that the g image contains one more exposure than this is unlikely to be an effect of differing depths, and, while not a voorwerp, may be evidence for tidal debris.

sources.summary: The paper argues that SDSS J0159 is a changing-look AGN/TDE candidate whose decade-scale broad H variability is best explained by accretion-disk emission from tidal debris.

quotes.quote: In this Letter, we report further evidence to support a tidal disruption event (TDE) in the known changing-look AGN SDSS J0159, through the unique variabilities of asymmetric broad H : broader but redder in 2010 than in 2000. Accepted the broad H emission regions tightly related to accreting debris in a central TDE, the well-known relativistic accretion disk origination can be applied to well explain the asymmetric broad H variabilities in SDSS J0159. Moreover, the model determined broad H emission regions have the sizes not follow the empirical R-L relation valid in normal broad line AGN, but have locations basically similar to the regions of accreting debris from a central TDE in SDSS J0159, indicating tight connections between the broad H emission materials and central TDE debris.

md15 have shown the observed broad H in SDSS J0159 (=J015957.64+003310.5) in 2000 and 2010, and reported interesting variabilities in ten years: broad H in 2010 with FWHM (Full Width at Half Maximum) about 6167 km/s wider than that in 2000 with FWHM about 3408 km/s and central wavelength in 2000 with _0 about 6560 \ bluer than that in 2010 with _0 about 6582 . The special variability properties of broad H in SDSS J0159, especially the different central wavelengths, can not be clearly explained by common variabilities of broad emission lines coming from normal BLRs in normal AGN, however, accretion disk origination of broad H related to TDEs could provide reasonable explanations to the observed broad H variabilities in SDSS J0159.

gmw_00001107.name: J1011+5442
gmw_00001107.overview: J1011+5442 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy with a highly irregular structure that required complex modeling to fit its stellar light [arXiv:1903.08122]. It has recently undergone a merger of disrupted galaxies, leaving behind tidal streams that indicate the planes of motion of each galaxy were likely perpendicular to each other [arXiv:1903.08122].

sources.summary: J1011+5442 is the most irregular host in the sample, requiring complex modeling and showing tidal streams from a recent merger of disrupted galaxies.

quotes.quote: J1011+5442 This is this is the most obviously irregular galaxy in our sample. It is evident that this galaxy has recently undergone a merger that has resulted in a significantly disrupted galaxy. The tidal streams (regions B and D in Figure fig:3_colour ) indicate that the planes of motion of each galaxy in the merger were likely perpendicular to each other. While the [ O III ] emission of this galaxy is significant, the tidal features and disruption make it difficult to distinguish any voorwerpjes or EELR surrounding it.
To fit the stellar light from the galaxy, more components were required than the double-S ersic profile employed for the other galaxies of our sample.

gmw_00001108.name: J2336+0017
gmw_00001108.overview: J2336+0017 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy [arXiv:1903.08122] that exhibits large-scale tidal streams or shells [arXiv:1903.08122]. Over multiple observation epochs, the central active nucleus dimmed by 60 percent [arXiv:2206.10056] while showing variations in luminosity and virial black hole mass [arXiv:2206.10056].

sources.summary: The paper tracks five SDSS epochs for SDSSJ233602.9+001728, reporting large changes in virial black-hole mass, AGN luminosity, and Eddington ratio, with the source dimming by 60 percent.

quotes.quote: In the first case, i.e., SDSSJ233602.9+001728, we have 5 epochs of spectral information from which we estimate the and per epoch. The source starts (MJD = 51783) from a relatively high BH mass, log = 8.73 0.1 (in units of ), and high AGN luminosity, log = 43.24 0.08 (in units of erg s ^ 1 ). The corresponding Eddington ratio is log = -2.66 0.13. Then, in the next epoch (MJD = 51877, MJD = 94 Here, the MJD refers to the time difference between the discussed epoch and the first epoch of spectroscopic observation for a given source. ), the BH mass reduces by nearly half an order, log = 8.29 0.03, accompanied with a rise ( 1.5 times) in the AGN luminosity, log = 43.42 0.02. This leads to an increase in the Eddington ratio, log = -2.05 0.04.

In the last epoch (MJD = 55449, MJD = 3666), the BH mass attains its highest value, log = 9.16 0.12, with an increase in the AGN luminosity, log = 42.86 0.03. The resulting Eddington ratio is at its lowest for this source, log = -3.47 0.13. Overall, this source has dimmed by 60 relative to the first epoch (see also the last column in Table tab:table-pyqsofit ).

sources.summary: J2336+0017 shows large-scale tidal streams or shells, no obvious EELR candidate, a redder-than-red-sequence position, and a disk-like lower Sersic index.

quotes.quote: J2336+0017 Similar to J0126 0839, this galaxy shows evidence for large-scale tidal streams, or shells in all three bands. In this case, there is a significant difference in depth between the g band image and the and i band images. Nevertheless, there are no obvious features which appear in that are strong candidates for extended emission line regions.
J2336+0017 (indicated by the purple diamond in Figure fig:colour_mag ) sits above the red sequence in the rest frame, but this is not unheard of for quasar host galaxies.

Though our sample is small, of our three galaxies well described by S ersic profiles, J2336+0017 which has n = 1.88 is approximately 0.5 dex fainter in its bright state than J0126 0839 and J0159+0033, which have n 3.2 .

gmw_00001109.name: S3 1227+25
gmw_00001109.overview: S3 1227+25 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1508.07251, arXiv:0711.1158] and blazar [arXiv:1508.07251, arXiv:2305.02860] that exhibits a curved, polarized radio jet [arXiv:0711.1158]. The source has undergone multiple hard-spectrum GeV flares [arXiv:2305.02860] and has been detected in very-high-energy gamma rays [arXiv:1508.07251, arXiv:2305.02860].

sources.summary: The paper describes S3 1227+25 as a Fermi LAT IBL whose 2015 flares triggered VERITAS observations, including a later campaign with a 13-sigma VHE excess.

quotes.quote: S3 1227+25 (ON 246) is a Fermi LAT-detected IBL at a redshift of z = 0.135 . Its MeV-GeV properties ( _ 3FGL 2.2 , _ 1FHL 3.3 ) suggest it is an unlikely VHE emitter during its baseline emission state. In January 2015, the object flared initiating alerts from both the LAT team and the automated VERITAS pipeline. In response, VERITAS acquired 3 h of good-weather ToO observations, but did not detect the source despite the reports of 30 times brighter MeV-GeV flux ( _ LAT = 2.2 ). In May 2015, an even brighter flare ( 40x flux, _ LAT = 1.9 ) of this blazar initiated similar alerts, triggering another VERITAS campaign lasting about one week.
Overall, an excess of 420 rays ( 13 ) was detected from S3 1227+25.

sources.summary: VERITAS detects VHE gamma-ray emission from S3 1227+25; the paper adopts z=0.325 and models its compact jet emission with a one-zone SSC scenario.

quotes.quote: A gamma-ray source positionally consistent with S3 1227+25 was first identified in Fermi LAT data and is listed in the Fermi LAT one-year general point source catalog and subsequent updates, most recently in the 12-year catalog (4FGL-DR3, 4fgl_dr3 ). The source was also included in the first Fermi LAT catalog of sources detected above 10 GeV (1FHL, 2013ApJS..209...34A ), although it was not identified as a potential VHE emitter given its low flux (about 1 of the Crab Nebula flux above 10 GeV) and a relatively soft, albeit uncertain, spectral index, _ 1FHL = 3.29 0.95 . While the blazar was not listed in the second catalog of Fermi LAT sources detected above 50 GeV (2FHL, 2016ApJS..222....5A ), it was included in the third Fermi LAT catalog of sources significantly detected in the 10 GeV -- 2 TeV energy range (3FHL, 3fhl ), with a spectral index, _ 3FHL = 2.69 0.22 . Since its first detection, several hard-spectrum GeV flares from S3 1227+25 have been observed with the Fermi LAT 2015ATel.7596....1B, 2015ATel.6982....1C .
Based on the above discussion, a redshift z=0.325 , was assumed for the scientific results presented in this work.
Finally, the multiwavelength spectral energy distribution is well described by a simple one-zone leptonic synchrotron self-Compton radiation model.

sources.summary: The paper rejects a literature redshift for S3 1227+255, noting failed host detection, previous featureless spectra, and a new featureless optical spectrum.

quotes.quote: S3 1227+255 : nass1996 reported z = 0.135 but no information on the detected spectral lines were provided. In spite of the alleged low redshift, high quality images failed to detect the host galaxy nilsson2003 . shaw2013a did not confirm this redshift and no spectral features were found. Our optical spectrum (SNR 250) is featureless down to EW = 0.1-0.2 . Therefore we do not confirm the literature redshift and we set a redshift lower limit of z > 0.10.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1227+255 as a ROSAT/Hamburg Quasar Survey BL Lac and reports a curved VLBI jet with core and jet polarization plus a longitudinal-field sheath.

quotes.quote: 1227+255 This BL Lac object was identified during the correlation of the ROSAT All-Sky Survey (RASS) with the Hamburg Quasar Survey NassBade96 . Our VLBI image (Fig.  fig:1227 ) shows a jet that initially emerges to the southwest, then curves toward the south. Polarization was detected in both the pc-scale core and jet. The vectors in the inner jet are roughly aligned with the jet direction, implying a transverse B field, and a `sheath' of longitudinal B field is observed along the northern edge of the jet, where the jet appears to be bending.

sources.summary: The paper reports S3 1227+25 as a z=0.135 LBL/IBL detected by VERITAS at 10 sigma in six hours despite its soft spectrum and weak prior candidacy.

quotes.quote: Finally, S3 1227+25 (z=0.135) is an LBL/IBL that was detected at 10 in 6 hours of VERITAS exposure . Unlike other recent flare detections, this source is rather soft and was not assumed to be a good VHE candidate ( _ 1FHL 3.3 ). VERITAS observations were triggered by an email alert from the Fermi LAT team.

gmw_00001110.name: UGCA 438
gmw_00001110.overview: UGCA 438 is a dwarf irregular galaxy located slightly beyond the boundary of the Local Group [arXiv:0803.3728]. Near-infrared observations of its central region resolve evolved stellar populations, including asymptotic giant branch and red giant branch stars, which reveal a star formation episode at intermediate ages [arXiv:0803.3728].

sources.summary: The paper uses MAD near-infrared adaptive-optics imaging of UGCA 438 to resolve evolved stellar populations and infer an intermediate-age star-formation episode in the observed region.

quotes.quote: We present a study aimed at deriving constraints on star formation at intermediate ages from the evolved stellar populations in the dwarf irregular galaxy . These observations were also intended to demonstrate the scientific capabilities of the multi-conjugated adaptive optics demonstrator (MAD) implemented at the ESO Very Large Telescope as a test-bench of adaptive optics (AO) techniques. We perform accurate, deep photometry of the field using J and K_s band AO images of the central region of the galaxy. The near-infrared (IR) colour-magnitude diagrams clearly show the sequences of asymptotic giant branch (AGB) stars, red supergiants, and red giant branch (RGB) stars down to 1 mag below the RGB tip. Optical--near-IR diagrams, obtained by combining our data with Hubble Space Telescope observations, provide the best separation of stars in the various evolutionary stages. The counts of AGB stars brighter than the RGB tip allow us to estimate the star formation at intermediate ages. Assuming a Salpeter initial mass function, we find that the star formation episode at intermediate ages produced 6 10 ^5 M_ of stars in the observed region.

In this context, as part of a Guaranteed Time Observations program, we obtained MAD near-infrared (IR) images of the dwarf irregular galaxy (UGCA 438) . We chose this galaxy from a list of targets selected according to various criteria: favourable position on the sky with respect to the availability of stars to perform the AO correction; low Galactic latitude ( b=-70 9 ), to minimise the contamination by foreground Galactic stars ; location slightly beyond the boundary of the LG, so as to maximise the physical area sampled within the 1 field-of-view (FoV) while still detecting stars at the tip of the red giant branch (TRGB) with an adequate S/N; existence of images of the same field in HST and/or ESO archives; and the presence of a relatively strong intermediate age component.

sources.summary: The paper derives a JAGB distance for UGCA 438 from VLT adaptive-optics near-infrared imaging and compares it with six homogenized TRGB distances.

quotes.quote: They average to < _o (TRGB)> = 25.12 0.14 mag. UGCA 438 = ESO 2323-326 JAGB stars: Gullieuszik et al. (2008) obtained near-IR JK images of UGCA 438 (UKS 2323-326) using an adaptive-optics system (MAD) deployed on the VLT . The CMD based on these data is shown in Figure fig:CMDmontage , from which we determine a JAGB apparent distance modulus of 26.85 0.06 (stat) mag, based on 24 JAGB stars.

gmw_00001111.name: UGC 5750
gmw_00001111.overview: UGC 5750 is a low surface brightness galaxy [arXiv:1508.03339] that features a bar-like structure [arXiv:astro-ph/9805120] and sparse hydrogen-alpha emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0604576]. It is characterized by an underdense dark matter halo [arXiv:1611.02716] and a very slowly rising, shallow rotation curve [arXiv:1706.04202].

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 5750 as a low-velocity, low-density extreme whose rotation curve prefers a relatively large SIDM cross section and has negligible disk influence.

quotes.quote: In Fig.  fig:examples , we show SIDM fits for UGC 5721 , NGC 1560 , and UGC 5750 . All have similar V_f 80  km/s , but the shapes of the rotation curves are very different in the inner regions. UGC 5721 and UGC 5750 are at opposite extremes for the rotation curve diversity in this mass range. Despite the diversity, the SIDM halo model provides an impressive fit to the rotation curves.

We find that NGC 1560 has a median halo, UGC 5721 has a denser halo, and UGC 5750 has an underdense halo, but all within 2 of the median expectation. The observed (2  ) is close to 20  km/s for UGC 5750, while the corresponding CDM halo has (2  ) 30  km/s even with a concentration 2 lower than the median value. The effect of the disk is most significant in UGC 5721, resulting in a SIDM halo similar to the CDM one and a flat V_ cir even at 2  kpc . The effect becomes mild in NGC 1560 and negligible in UGC 5750, consistent with their luminosities.

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 5750 as a very shallow rotation-curve outlier; unlike IC 2574, non-circular-motion concerns do not apply, and NIHAO feedback models can reproduce it.

quotes.quote: The R-axis is truncated at 12 kpc for clarity, but we note that galaxy UGC 5750 extends flat out to 23 kpc. This subset of galaxies is compared to the sample of NIHAO galaxies within the same V_ Rlast range, 47-90 , shown as grey lines in Fig. fig:rcnot . All the NIHAO galaxies in this range form cores of various sizes, matching very well the remaining 31 observed galaxies in this velocity region which represent the majority of observed SPARC galaxies here ( 74 per cent). Apart from galaxies IC 2574 and UGC 5750, which have a very shallow RC, all the remaining 9 outliers do not match NIHAO expectations since their RCs are steeper, rising faster from the galactic centre, and presenting a sharp discontinuity to flat rotation.

Galaxies IC 2574 and UGC 5750 have been extensively studied elsewhere in the context of \ e.g., Oman15,Oman16,Creasey17 : their RCs are so slowly-rising that their implied DM core is even larger than that predicted by other hydro-simulations that form cores. While issues about non-circular motions have been raised by and for the case of IC 2574, such concerns do not apply to UGC 5750. Therefore, it has been argued that these galaxies pose a problem to the core-formation scenario Oman15 . We show here that this is not the case.

sources.summary: UGC 5750 is described with sparse emission in about half its fibers, good agreement with previous long-slit rotation curves, and NFW concentrations too low for CDM expectations.

quotes.quote: UGC 5750: There was one pointing for this galaxy. The fiber positions are shown on an \ image (W.J.G. de Blok 2005, private communication). The average of the , , \ and \ lines was taken as the fiber velocity. \ emission was sparse in this galaxy and only roughly half of the fibers had a detection. The position angle was fixed to the value listed in MRdB and dBB .
There is good agreement with the long-slit \ rotation curves of both dBB and dBMR .

sources.summary: UGC 5750 is a challenging MOND case: good fits demand a lower inclination, possibly requiring a hidden face-on LSB disk beneath the bar-like structure.

quotes.quote: UGC 5750 Good solutions are only found for inclinations between of 38 and 42 degrees. The value of 64 degrees from van der Hulst et al. \ (1993) is ruled out. The new low value can only be consistent with the optical axis ratio if there is a LSB face-on disk underlying the observed bar-like structure. This might be consistent with the H i column density map in van der Hulst et al. (1993) where one can derive an inclination of 35 degrees for the outermost column density contours. Deeper observations of this galaxy should be able to test this.

sources.summary: The paper models UGC 5750 as a low-surface-brightness galaxy whose SIDM fit prefers a large cross section, made consistent by allowing lower halo concentration.

quotes.quote: To measure DM self-interactions at small-to-intermediate scales, we consider rotation curves of five dwarf galaxies (IC 2574, NGC 2366, Ho II, M81 dwB, DDO 154) in the THINGS sample and seven LSB galaxies (UGC 4325, F563-V2, F563-1, F568-3, UGC 5750, F583-4, F583-1) from Kuzio de Naray, et al. . Two galaxies have been omitted from each of these samples for which was not well-determined. To model these galaxies, we include the contributions to the rotation curve from DM, gas, and stars, with _* allowed to vary uniformly by 0.3 dex from the quoted population synthesis values .

If we fix the _s r_s relation to its median in CDM cosmology in our analysis, the galaxies UGC 5750 and IC 2574 prefer the largest cross sections, /m 10 cm^2/g , while M81 dwB prefers the smallest cross sections, /m 0.1 cm^2/g . However, if UGC 5750 and IC 2574 halos are 2 less concentrated and M81 dwB halo 2 more concentrated than the median halo, the inferred /m become consistent, within errors, with 1 cm^2/g .

sources.summary: The paper says U5750 was independently observed with agreeing data, is difficult for a standard NFW model, and needs its outermost rotation-curve point to constrain the fit.

quotes.quote: U5750 This curve was observed both by us and de Blok Bosma (2001), and the two data sets show good agreement (see Paper I). This curve is difficult to model with a standard NFW profile, but the pseudo-isothermal model provides a good fit. The outermost point is taken from the curve, and provides an important constraint for the NFW model. Without this point the fit produces 0 and an impossibly large V_ 200 , a result of the fitting program trying to make V(R) R^ 1/2 look like V R .

gmw_00001112.name: NGC 1705
gmw_00001112.overview: NGC 1705 is an isolated, starburst blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1110.1148, arXiv:1802.06792] that hosts a dense dark matter halo [arXiv:1301.2889] and a strongly warped, rotating neutral hydrogen disk [arXiv:1404.6252, arXiv:1802.06792]. The galaxy features a central super star cluster [arXiv:1402.4075], offset dust clouds [arXiv:2305.03443], and a starburst-driven galactic wind outflow [arXiv:astro-ph/0102283, arXiv:1311.2227].

sources.summary: The review highlights NGC1705 as a resolved dwarf whose HST photometry traces radial stellar-population differences and a star-formation history active for at least 5 Gyr.

quotes.quote: The case of NGC1705 is particularly instructive. The HST/WFPC2 photometry was deep and good enough to let us resolve individual stars from the most central regions to the extreme outskirts . We have been able to divide the galaxy in roughly concentric regions, all sufficiently populated by resolved stars, and derive the SF history of each region. Young massive stars are concentrated at the center and their percentage rapidly decreases outwards, while faint red stars are increasingly visible towards the outer regions.

By applying to each region the synthetic CMD method, have inferred their SFHs, summarized in Fig. sf1705 , where the SFR per unit area is plotted as a function of age. It can be seen that, except for the innermost region, where crowding does not allow us to reach old lookback times, all the regions have been forming stars since at least 5 Gyr. On average, the SF appears to have been rather continous: there are evidences for interruptions in the SF activity, but always shorter than a few Myr or tens of Myr, at least in the age range where we do have this time resolution (i.e. in the last 1 Gyr or so). Quiescent phases of 100 Myr or longer would have appeared as gaps in the empirical CMDs of stars younger than 1 Gyr, and such gaps are absent.

sources.summary: The paper presents ATCA H I observations of the dwarf galaxy NGC 1705, deriving mass models that indicate a compact, dense dark matter halo and negligible companion interactions.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The position of the optical centre of the system is _ 2000 = 04 ^ h 54 ^ m 14. ^ s 4, _ 2000 = 53^ 21 ' 38 ''

quotes.quote: Australia Telescope Compact Array line observations of the nearby dwarf galaxy NGC 1705 are presented. The data are used to trace the gravitational potential of the galaxy out to several stellar disc scale lengths. A rotation curve is derived for the system and used to generate mass models. Dark matter dominates the gravitational potential at nearly all galactocentric radii. The models suggest NGC 1705 to have a dark matter halo that it much denser and more compact than previously thought.

A nearby dwarf galaxy with an unusually large \ disc extending out to several optical scale lengths is NGC 1705. Given the proximity of NGC 1705, the \ disc serves as a useful tracer of the system's gravitational potential. Besides being famous for its dark matter content, NGC 1705 is also well-known for hosting one of the most powerful starbursts (relative to its mass) in the local Universe.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1705 as a dwarf starburst outlier with weak FIR lines relative to SFR, off-nuclear dusty regions, and east-west differences in radiation field and cooling.

quotes.quote: A few galaxies (NGC 1705, NGC 2366 and NGC 4861) show diverging behavior in some of the SFR relations. NGC 1705 has a peculiar behavior in the SFR- L_ [ CII ] and SFR- L_ [ OIII ] relations with weaker line emission relative to its star formation rate. NGC 1705 is a dwarf starburst galaxy dominated by a central super star cluster (SSC) straddled by two dusty off-nuclear regions offset by 250 pc that dominate the H , mid- and far-infrared emission of the galaxy 2006ApJ...652.1170C . The chemistry and heating of gas in the off-nuclear positions do not seem to be regulated directly by the central SSC 2006ApJ...652.1170C , but rather exposed to the emission of young, massive stars, produced during a second starburst about 3 Myr ago which was presumably induced by the expanding shell after the first central starburst 2009AJ....138..169A .

The weak [ O iii ] _ 88 emission originates from the eastern dust complex which shows bright [ C ii ] and PAH emission, which suggests that the deviation for the eastern regions of NGC 1705 could be due to a radiation field not strong enough to excite [ O iii ] _ 88 (requiring massive O6 and earlier-type stars). The western dust region does show bright [ O iii ] _ 88 emission, but has weak [ C ii ] and PAH emission. We, therefore, argue that the western dust complex is exposed to a harder radiation field, capable of destroying PAHs and ionizing the majority of the gas, which makes [ O iii ] _ 88 a more efficient coolant. The non-detection of [ O i ] _ 63 (which is an efficient coolant of dense PDRs, see Table critical ) might suggest rather diffuse ISM regions in the western and eastern dust emission complexes.

sources.summary: The paper uses FUSE spectroscopy to show NGC 1705 has blueshifted OVI in a dwarf-starburst outflow, arguing that a superbubble is blowing out of its ISM.

quotes.quote: We report FUSE far-UV spectroscopy of the prototypical dwarf starburst galaxy NGC 1705. These data allow us for the first time to directly probe the coronal-phase ( T = few 10 ^5 K) gas that may dominate the radiative cooling of the supernova-heated ISM and thereby determine the dynamical evolution of the starburst-driven outflows in dwarf galaxies. We detect a broad ( 100 km s ^ 1 FWHM) and blueshifted ( v = 77 km s ^ 1 ) OVI 1032 absorption-line arising in the previously-known galactic outflow. The mass and kinetic energy in the outflow we detect is dominated by the warm ( T 10 ^4 K) photoionized gas which is also seen through its optical line-emission. The kinematics of this warm gas are compatible with a simple model of the adiabatic expansion of a superbubble driven by the collective effect of the kinetic energy supplied by supernovae in the starburst.

However, the observed properties of the OVI absorption in NGC 1705 are not consistent with the simple superbubble model, in which the OVI would arise in a conductive interface inside the superbubble's outer shell. The relative outflow speed of the OVI is too high and the observed column density ( logN_ OVI = 14.3) is much too large. We argue that the superbubble has begun to blow out of the ISM of NGC 1705.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1705 as a blue compact dwarf with intense central starburst activity, two stellar populations, a large embedded disk, and a complex recent star-formation history.

quotes.quote: NGC 1705, another blue compact dwarf, is well-known for hosting one of the most intense starbursts (relative to its mass) in the local universe. tosi_et_al_2001 used the HST to resolve the brightest red giant stars in the galaxy, thereby estimating a distance of 5.1 0.6 Mpc. The system has a B band absolute magnitude of M_B=-15.6 0.2 marlowe_1999 . meurer_1705_1992 identified two stellar populations in NGC 1705 which they refer to as the high and low surface brightness populations.

The young stellar disk has a B band 25 th magnitude isophotal radius of 1.2 kpc for the here adopted distance of 5.1 Mpc. The galaxy's intense star formation activity is concentrated in its central high surface brightness stellar population, and is driven mainly by a powerful super star cluster, NGC 1705 - 1 sandage_1978,melnick_1985 , which contributes almost half of the total ultra-violet luminosity of the galaxy meurer_1705_2 . Annibali_2003 found evidence for star formation activity that commenced at least 5 Gyr ago. The authors also showed the star formation history of NGC 1705 to be very complex, confirming the existence of a recent burst of star formation between 10 and 15 Myr ago.

sources.summary: The study classifies NGC 1705 as a regularly rotating but strongly warped starburst dwarf disk, with a beam-smearing-corrected rotation curve and dark-matter-dominated previous models.

quotes.quote: Nine galaxies in our sample (50 ) have a regularly rotating disk: NGC 1705, NGC 2366, NGC 4068, NGC 4214, NGC 6789, UGC 4483, I Zw 18, I Zw 36, and SBS 1415+437. For these objects, we derived rotation curves following a procedure similar to Swaters2009 . As a first step, we obtained initial estimates of the geometrical parameters and of the rotation curve by fitting a tilted-ring model to the VF Begeman1987 .

NGC 1705 has a strongly warped disk. Our rotation curve rises more steeply than those of Meurer1998 and Elson2013 because we applied a beam-smearing correction to the inner velocity-points using 3D disk models (see Fig. fig:n1705 ). Meurer1998 and Elson2013 decomposed their rotation curves into mass components and found that DM dominates the gravitational potential at all radii. We did not build a detailed mass model because the optical and kinematic centers differ by 550 pc, while PA _ opt and PA _ kin differ by 45 ^ .

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 1705 as a blue compact dwarf with lower N/O than NGC 1569, requiring reduced nitrogen production and stronger nitrogen loss in galactic winds.

quotes.quote: The bottom-right panel show instead the predictions by RTM06 for the starburst dwarf NGC 1569 (always the line with higher N/O for each line-type; blue in the colour version of the figure) and the Blue Compact dwarf NGC 1705 (always the line with lower N/O for each line-type; red in the colour version of the figure). The big symbols with error bar indicate the corresponding values derived from HII regions in NGC 1569 (blue square) and NGC 1705 (red diamond).

In spite of the different classification, NGC 1569 and NGC 1705 have quite similar properties see e.g., and references therein An03,An05 : similar gas and star masses, metallicity, IMF, SF history with very strong recent activity, and observational evidence of similarly strong galactic winds. Yet, they have quite different N/O ratios, which require different evolutionary assumptions. The long-dashed lines in the bottom-right panel of Fig. nitrogen show the model predictions based on the standard yields by vdG97 for intermediate mass stars. Clearly a standard N production may be consistent with the N/O ratio observed in NGC 1569, but un-reconcilable with the low N/O of NGC 1705. If we consider yields with smaller N production, even in the extreme case of those by MM02 where the hot-bottom burning phase has not been computed, the models overpredict the N/O ratio observed in NGC 1705 (short-dashed lines). A better fit to the observed abundances is obtained (RTM06) if not only the nitrogen production in intermediate mass stars is relatively low, as predicted for instance by the minimal hot-bottom burning case proposed by vdG97 , but also a higher efficiency of nitrogen loss in the galactic wind is allowed in NGC 1705 than in NGC 1569 (solid lines).

sources.summary: The review presents NGC1705 as an active 5.1 Mpc BCD with galactic winds, resolved RGB stars, multi-Gyr star formation, and recent burst episodes after gas cooling.

quotes.quote: Fig. n1705 shows the case of NGC1705, a very active BCD at 5.1 Mpc, with evidence of galactic winds. The most central region is very crowded and only young and intermediate-age stars can be resolved, but elsewhere RGB stars are easily resolved and become the most prominent CMD feature in the outer regions. We can then derive the SFH of most regions back to several Gyr ago, as shown in the right-hand panel of Fig. n1705 . It is immediately apparent that in this BCD we are not seeing a bursting regime, with short episodes of SF activity separated by long periods of quiescence.

It is also interesting to notice that the strong SF episode of 15 Myr ago coincides with the epoch of both the formation of the central super star cluster and the onset of the galactic wind. The wind is probably the cause of the sudden halt of the SF activity everywhere in NGC1705. The gas, however, must have been quick in cooling and falling down, since only 6-7 Myr later another, stronger, SF burst appears to occur in all the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 1705 as an irregular dwarf test case, noting its compact nucleus, large rotation-curve discrepancy, and successful amplified model fit.

quotes.quote: NGC 1705 The four galaxies discussed in the preceding sections are all characterized by regular disk shapes. The irregular dwarf galaxy NGC 1705 we want to include in our sample in order to test the applicability of our procedure on a broader scale. This compact galaxy is much smaller and contains in its nucleus a luminous super star cluster with a mass of 2 10^ 35 (i.e. 10^5 ) and a significant cold dust component Tosetal,Annetal,OHaetal .

The RCs in Fig. fig:1705 demonstrate that there is a very large discrepancy between V_ bar and V_ obs . A conclusion of Sanetal on the shapes of dwarf galaxy RCs is - stated explicitly for NGC 1705 - that they are difficult to reproduce with DM scenarios, if the central baryon component is small. If we use the same procedure for this galaxy as for the other ones, we find a very large g_ fit and a small _0 relative to 2 r_ b,half .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1705 as a blue compact dwarf with a strong galactic wind, retained neutral gas disk, rotating dark-matter-dominated dynamics, and unusually high central halo density.

quotes.quote: NGC 1705 displays a strong galactic wind in . There is a spur of \ emission obliquely jutting out from its \ disk that appears to be a neutral ISM extension of this wind. If so, then NGC 1705 has ejected about 8 \ of its neutral ISM at a mass loss rate at least comparable to the star formation rate.

Although both galaxies have some kinematic irregularities their dominant structures are extended rotating disks which are strongly centrally peaked. These are typical properties of BCDs imaged in \ . The disk of NGC 2915 is so extended that it has the \ appearance of a late type barred spiral. Similar galaxies include IC 10 and NGC 4449 . The rotation curves of both NGC 1705 and NGC 2915 show a fairly steep rise over the optical face of the galaxy which then becomes flat out to the edge of the \ distribution.
In both galaxies, DM dominates the mass distribution, even within the optical radius of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The ADF-S paper identifies NGC 1705 as a starburst blue compact dwarf and uses infrared/Spitzer photometry to classify it as warm and actively star-forming.

quotes.quote: One of the sources corresponds to the extragalactic X-ray source which is related to a starburst galaxy NGC 1705, identified as the fourth brightest source in the 10 sample. Among the remaining objects there are 7 radio sources of an unknown nature, one quasar at z 1.23 , one X-ray source related to the starburst galaxy NGC 1705, which is the fourth brightest source in our 10 data set, and one double stellar system of B 6 .

The highest stellar temperature we have fitted for a starburst blue compact dwarf NGC 1705. The dust temperature of this galaxy is also significantly higher than average. One of the compact galaxies, the starburst blue compact dwarf NGC 1705, similarly to 60 of spiral galaxies, is classified as warm and actively star forming.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC1705 as an amorphous host galaxy with a nearby bright super star cluster and subsolar global metallicity comparable to the Magellanic Clouds.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present new observations of the super star cluster in the amorphous galaxy NGC1705 melnick85a . NGC1705--1 is one of the brightest clusters M _ V =-15.4 ma01 . It is also one of the closest, at a distance of 5.1 0.6 Mpc tosi01 . The metallicity of the host galaxy NGC1705 is subsolar with Z=0.35Z _ ls04 .

This is consistent with the study of meurer92 and storchi94 who report a sub--solar global metallicity for the entire galaxy NGC 1705: the former derive 12+log(O/H)=8.46, while the second give 8.36. These values are similar to the LMC (respectively SMC) metallicity.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1705 as an isolated starburst dwarf with an extended warped disk, strong offsets between optical and kinematic axes, and a northeastern peculiar extension.

quotes.quote: Several galaxies have heavily disturbed distributions, characterized by large-scale asymmetries, long filaments, and/or a large optical- offset (NGC 4449, I Zw 18, NGC 1705, UGC 6541, NGC 1569, and I Zw 36).

NGC 1705 has an extended, warped disk. The disk shows relatively regular morphology and kinematics, but it is strongly offset with respect to the stellar component: the optical and kinematic centers differ by 550 pc, while the optical and kinematic PAs differ by 45 ^ (see LVF14). To the North-East, there is also a small extension with peculiar kinematics, that may be associated with the H wind see Meurer1998, Elson2013 . NGC 1705 appears very isolated: the two nearest objects (LSBG F157-089 and MRSS 157-121650) are at a projected distance of 0.5 Mpc, but may be members of the Dorado group at D 18 Mpc (see Firth2006 and Evstigneeva2007 , respectively).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1705 as the most isolated, gas-poor BCD in the sample, with a warped H I disk and a recent star-formation peak that remains ongoing.

quotes.quote: NGC 1705 is the most isolated of the three galaxies, since its closest neighbour is at more than 500 kpc (LSBG F157-089). Despite this, NGC 1705 has a warped HI disk ( meurer98 ) that is significantly off-set with respect to the stellar component, a possible signature that the outer HI gas is still in the process of settling down.

Overall, the activity of NGC 1705 has been slightly declining for the past 100 Myr. This behaviour changed drastically 10 Myr ago, when the SFR increased by a factor of two or more over the 100 Myr-averaged-SFR. After the peak the SFR remained constant and no drop is detected.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1705 as a metal-poor dwarf starburst with recent bursts, a super star cluster, low-velocity outflow, regular HI disk, and offset dust clouds.

quotes.quote: NGC 1705 had more recent starburst episodes than NGC 625, with the older of two bursts of star formation having occurred 10-15 Myr ago, and the younger one much more recently, 3 Myr ago annibali09,cignoni18 . NGC 1705 is rich in star clusters billett02 , with the most massive one being a Super Star Cluster SSC, oconnell94,vazquez04,martins12 , that has roughly the 15 Myr age of the oldest starburst event. There is evidence for a low-velocity outflow seen through UV absorption heckman01 , but, unlike in NGC 625, the \ is configured in a regularly rotating disk elson13,lelli14 . With \ observations of NGC 1705, cannon06 find that the far-infrared dust morphology differs dramatically from the optical, with two dust clouds 250 pc approximately east and west of the central SSC, and apparently unrelated to it. These two dust clouds also show 8 \ emission, and the central SSC is coincident with an 8 \ peak.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1705 as a nearby starburst with a strong galactic wind, low mass, measured metallicity, and recent bursty star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 1705 is a nearby starburst galaxy with a stunning starburst-driven galactic wind b:Meurer_aj92 . NGC 1705 has a combined stellar and gas mass of 2.7 10^8 \ b:Meurer_aj92 . The metallicity measurements range from 12+log(O/H) = 8.21 to 8.46 b:Lee_apj04,b:Storchi-Bergmann_apj94,b:Meurer_aj92 . Like similar dwarf starbursts, NGC 1705 has a bursty star formation history with two recent star forming epochs in the last 15 Myr b:Annibali_aj03,b:Annibali_aj09 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC1705 as a dwarf starburst with low line luminosity, Wolf-Rayet evidence, weak radio emission, and no likely genuine active nucleus.

quotes.quote: The remaining candidate, NGC1705, is a dwarf starburst galaxy with an luminosity <10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 , smaller than any of the RSA Seyferts. Ultraviolet spectra of this galaxy show evidence for Wolf-Rayet stars meu92 that could explain its observed / ratio e.g., sch99 . It is also anomalously weak in the radio, even for a star-forming galaxy can06 , so we conclude that it is not likely that NGC1705 contains a genuine active nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1705 as a high-V2kpc outlier with a strongly warped HI disk, possible central radial motions, an off-centred stellar component, and starburst activity.

quotes.quote: Galaxy NGC 1705 has a regularly rotating H i disc which is strongly warped, and may be out of equilibrium due to its off-centred stellar component. Moreover, its H i disc may indicate the presence of radial motions (15 ) near the centre Lelli14b . NGC 1705 is a starburst galaxy Annibali03 , with a birthrate parameter close to 6 Lelli14b , where the McQuinn09 definition of starburst is followed, as a galaxy that has a birthrate parameter b=SFR(last 1 Gyr)/SFR(mean)_ 0-6 > 3 , where SFR(mean) _ 0-6 is the mean SFR over the past 6 Gyr.

gmw_00001113.name: UGC 8490
gmw_00001113.overview: UGC 8490 is a nearby compact Magellanic-type irregular galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0210414, arXiv:1706.04202] that features a strongly warped disk [arXiv:astro-ph/9902227, arXiv:0901.4222] and a central bulge [arXiv:1804.07594]. It is classified as a starburst galaxy [arXiv:1504.02084] with a high specific star formation rate [arXiv:1808.05617] and hosts an off-nuclear ultraluminous X-ray source [arXiv:astro-ph/0105307].

sources.summary: The paper derives NGC 5204's HST-based star-formation history, finding a compact dwarf starburst with elevated recent SFR for over 100 Myr and a strongly warped disk.

quotes.quote: The data for both NGC 4190 and NGC 5204 consist of archival HST imaging obtained using the Wide Field Planetary Camera 2 (WFPC2) with the F606W and F814W filters. Total integration times per filter were 2200 s for NGC 4190 and 600 s for NGC 5204. Table  tab:properties summarizes the observations and basic properties of the systems. Figure  fig:images shows composite color images of both galaxies produced from the Hubble Legacy Archive pipeline. Visible in both galaxies are blue, presumably young, clustered, star-forming regions as well as a more homogeneously distributed redder, presumably older stellar population.

As seen in Figure  fig:sfhs , both NGC 4190 and NGC 5204 have SFRs at recent times that are higher than the b_ recent threshold of 2 and, thus, can be considered starbursts. Moreover, the disc-averaged recent SFRs for both galaxies are elevated for at least 100 Myr, and continue to be higher than the past averages at the present day. The durations of the starbursts, shaded in grey, are >380 130 Myr for NGC 4190 and >100 30 Myr for NGC 5204.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes UGC 8490 rotation curves to estimate baryonic, total, and dark matter mass under several halo geometries, finding cored profiles and spherical or oblate models favored over prolate halos.

quotes.quote: As an illustration of the potentiality of the determined circular velocity expression we perform the rotation curves analysis of UGC 8490 and UGC 9753 and we estimate the total and dark matter mass of these two galaxies under the assumption that their respective dark matter halos have spherical, prolate and oblate spheroidal mass distributions. We employ stellar population synthesis models and the total HI density map to obtain the stellar and HI+He+metals rotation curves of both galaxies. The subtraction of the stellar plus gas rotation curves from the observed rotation curves of UGC 8490 and UGC 9753 generates the dark matter circular velocity curves of both galaxies. We fit the dark matter rotation curves of UGC 8490 and UGC 9753 through the newly established circular velocity formula specialised to the spherical, prolate and oblate spheroidal mass distributions, considering the Navarro, Frenk and White, Burkert, Di Cintio, Einasto and Stadel dark matter halos.

Our principal findings are the following: globally, cored dark matter profiles Burkert and Einasto prevail over cuspy Navarro, Frenk and White and Di Cintio. Also, spherical/oblate dark matter models fit better the dark matter rotation curves of both galaxies than prolate dark matter halos.

The morphological type of UGC 8490 (NGC 5204) is SA(s)m according to NED, Hyperleda http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr/ Makarov2014 and Simbad Wenger2000 astronomy database. The GALFIT Peng2002, Peng2010 2D isophotal analysis detected two principal photometric components: a disc and a bulge.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 5204 in the seven-dwarf sample, using its HI map, central nuclear cluster, and broadly agreeing stellar- and gas-traced rotation curves in the dark-matter analysis.

quotes.quote: We present new constraints on the density profiles of dark matter (DM) halos in seven nearby dwarf galaxies from measurements of their integrated stellar light and gas kinematics. The gas kinematics of low mass galaxies frequently suggest that they contain constant density DM cores, while N-body simulations instead predict a cuspy profile. We present a data set of high resolution integral field spectroscopy on seven galaxies and measure the stellar and gas kinematics simultaneously.

For UGC 2259, we have used the radial profile of Figure 5 in . NGC 2552, NGC 5204, and UGC 11707 have their HI maps presented in , and the authors have shared the images. The standard flux-mass equation, with M_ HI in units of M_ , D as distances in Mpc, and S as the HI flux integral in units of mJy km s ^ 1 , is used as: M_ HI =236 D^2 S.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 5204 as a Magellanic spiral with high sSFR, dominated observationally by ULX X-1 whose broadband spectrum favors super-Eddington stellar-mass black-hole accretion.

quotes.quote: NGC 5204 is a Magellanic spiral galaxy that is part of the M101 group of galaxies and has a large sSFR comparable to that of M82. The ULX X-1 originally discovered by \ fabbiano-82 has been the motivation for most X-ray/multiwavelength observations of NGC 5204. roberts10-06 used a 2 month \ monitoring campaign to study the variability of X-1 and found that its spectrum became harder (e. \ g. \ heating of the corona) as its flux increased. They found no evidence supporting the presence of an intermediate-mass BH in X-1. mukherjee07-15 used two epochs of / \ coverage of X-1 to study its 0.3-20 keV properties. No significant spectral variations were observed for the 5 10^ 39 \ source and the broadband spectrum was consistent with super-Eddington accretion on to a stellar-mass BH.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5204 as a Magellanic-type galaxy hosting ULX X-1, with optical counterpart candidates and a newly found large H-emitting bubble around the source.

quotes.quote: mwpakull-E3:ro01 have recently described Chandra and ground based optical observations of the ULX in the Magellanic type galaxy NGC 5204 . They proposed a comparatively bright V=19.7 mag stellar object to be the optical counterpart, but on the basis of an HST image the same authors ( ) now find several more, fainter stellar images which could also be associated with the X-ray source. Our multicolor images shown in Figure mwpakull-E3_fig:fig4 reveal that the B=21.9 mag star located on the eastern rim of the error circle has very blue colors and should thus be considered a prime candidate. Perhaps more interesting is our discovery of a H emitting bubble with a diameter of some 360 pc. The ring is much larger then the "cavity" in the ionized gas reported by mwpakull-E3:ro01 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 5204 as a CVn I galaxy observed with WFPC2, describing its stellar population or morphology and deriving a TRGB-based distance.

quotes.quote: NGC 5204 is an irregular galaxy of Magellanic type, which is located at the northern edge of the CVn I cloud. Its distance, 4.9 1.0 Mpc, was estimated by Karachentsev et al.(1994) via the brightest blue and red stars. The CM diagram (Fig. 2) shows a mixed stellar population with a prominent RGB. The derived TRGB distance, 4.65 0.53 Mpc, is in good agreement with the previous estimate.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5204 as an M101 companion with a strongly warped HI disk, significant dark halo contribution, high normalized cluster frequency, and no current new-cluster formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 5204 NGC 5204 is a companion to the giant Sc spiral M101. The structure of the HI in this galaxy is that of a strongly warped disk (Sicotte Carignan 1997), and one could speculate that this is related to tidal interaction effects with M101. Sicotte Carignan (1997) also find that the dark matter halo of NGC 5204 contributes significantly to the mass even in the inner parts. The high T_N value of this galaxy is a consequence of its very low M_B rather than a high absolute number of clusters - we found only 7 clusters in this galaxy. Curiously, all of the 7 clusters belong to the ``red'' class, suggesting that no new clusters are being formed in NGC 5204 at the moment.

sources.summary: The paper describes 8490 as strongly warped, with large position-angle and inclination changes that make the outer rotation curve especially uncertain.

quotes.quote: 8490 is strongly warped. The position angle changes from 160^ to 225^ , the inclination changes from 50^ to 30^ , and then rises again to about 50^ . This system was studied in detail by Sicotte et al. \ (1996) and Sicotte Carignan (1997). The outer half of the rotation curve may be more uncertain than indicated by the error bars, due to uncertainties in the orientation angles.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5204 as an isolated M101-group-periphery galaxy with symmetric optical and radio emission, massive-star-dominated UV emission, and a high thermal radio fraction.

quotes.quote: NGC 5204. -- The galaxy is located on the periphery of M 101 group but has no close companion. The optical emission is symmetric, and the radio emission at 8.35 GHz corresponds to it (Figs. f: d, f:sub b). The UV emission from the galaxy is dominated by very massive stars ( >30 M _ ), which seems to be reflected in our data by a high thermal fraction at 8.35 GHz f_ th,8.35 =0.41 (see Sect. s:magnetic and Table t:radio8 ). A remarkable flattening of the radio spectrum due to significant thermal component at frequencies above 10 GHz was claimed by Fabbiano Panagia ( ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5204 as a nearby Magellanic-type host with an off-nuclear ultraluminous X-ray source and a possible ionized-gas cavity in its ISM.

quotes.quote: This SES is located in the nearby ( d = 4.8 Mpc; Tully 1988) magellanic-type galaxy NGC 5204. The presence of a luminous (log L _ X 39.8 ) X-ray source in NGC 5204 was first shown in data (Fabbiano, Kim Trinchieri 1992). This source has more recently been reported in HRI data, where it is seen to be slightly offset from the nucleus of the galaxy (RW2000; Colbert Mushotzky 1999; Lira, Lawrence Johnson 2000) and is therefore identified as a possible SES as opposed to a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus candidate.
It is at least plausible therefore that this is a cavity in the ISM of NGC 5204, apparently centred on the position of the X-ray source, with a diameter of 100 - 200 parsecs.

sources.summary: The paper reports that MOND falls below UGC 8490's flat rotation curve; a larger distance helps only partly, and a large warp makes the curve shape and amplitude uncertain.

quotes.quote: UGC 8490. -- The MOND prediction for this galaxy falls systematically below the observed flat part of this rotation curve. A larger distance makes this rotation curve more consistent with the MOND curve, although the MOND curve still falls short of the observed rotation curve near the turnover in the rotation curve. Because of the large warp in this galaxy, the shape and amplitude of the rotation curve are uncertain.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 8490 as a nearby compact irregular galaxy with strong warping, inconsistent inner H and HI rotation curves, and a recent star-formation rise.

quotes.quote: UGC 8490 (also known as NGC 5204) is a nearby compact irregular galaxy that is strongly warped, with several changes in inclination Sicotte97 . The innermost points of its H and H i RCs do not agree swaters09 . Its star formation history shows a significant rise at recent times compared with the average star formation rate over the last few Gyr, in consistency with known starburst dwarf galaxies Lelli14a,McQuinn15 .

sources.summary: The paper fits UGC 8490's dark-matter rotation curves with several halo models, finding cored or cuspy profiles fit the data and an oblate spheroidal dark matter configuration is supported by torque estimates.

quotes.quote: In the second part of this manuscript, we employ the tools developed in the first part, to analyse the mass density content and the inner and global structure of the dark matter haloes of UGC 8490 and UGC 9753, through the fits to the dark matter rotation curves of the two galaxies, assuming a triaxial spheroidal dark matter mass configuration. We employ the Navarro Frenk and White, Burkert, DiCintio, Einasto and Stadel dark matter models, and we obtain that both a cored Burkert and cuspy DiCintio and Navarro Frenk and White inward dark matter distributions could represent equally well the observed data, furthermore we determine an oblate spheroidal dark matter mass density configuration for UGC 8490 and UGC 9753. The latter outcome is confirmed by the estimation of the gravitational torques exerted by the dark matter halo of each analysed galaxy, on the corresponding baryonic components.

sources.summary: The paper presents UGC08490 as a flat-rotation-curve galaxy where MOND and RAR perform well and the smooth core also suits the SIDM model.

quotes.quote: UGC08490 is one of the galaxies with a characteristically flat rotation curve and slowly increasing inner curve for which MOND models gives particularly good results. The RAR MOND model (Equation eq:rar ) performs even better here. The smooth core and flat rotation curve make the SIDM model work particularly well, with r_1=8.4 kpc .

gmw_00001114.name: UGC 5721
gmw_00001114.overview: UGC 5721 is a compact, high-surface-brightness barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1005.5456, arXiv:1706.04202] that is classified as an emission-line or H II galaxy [arXiv:1706.04202] and identified as a potential starbursting dwarf [arXiv:2404.16247, arXiv:1706.04202]. It features a compact stellar disk [arXiv:1612.03903], twisting isophotes in its central regions [arXiv:1005.5456], and asymmetric neutral hydrogen kinematics [arXiv:2404.16247].

sources.summary: The paper models UGC 5721 as a cusp-dominated rotation-curve outlier whose compact stellar disk and concentrated halo let SIDM reproduce an almost-NFW profile.

quotes.quote: We turn then our attention to two particular examples highlighted in Oman_2015 , UGC 5721 and IC 2574. These two galaxies have similar circular velocity measured at the outermost point of the rotation curve 80 , which probably indicate that they populate dark matter halos with similar mass, but the shapes of the inner regions are remarkably different: whereas UGC 5721 is consistent with a `cuspy' NFW profile, IC 2574 has a very extended core. This pair is an interesting case of the aforementioned diversity and we can use it as benchmark for our SIDM scenario. In Figs.  fig:UGC5721 and fig:ICgal we show the data together with the best fit CDM (dashed line) and SIDM (solid black) curves.

For UGC 5721 (NGC3724) we use the rotation curve data from Swaters_2003 and stellar and gas densities from Andrew Pace (priv. communication, for comparison one can see Fig. 1 in Swaters_2010 which has a slightly higher mass-to-light ratio). For IC 2574 we use the rotation curve, gas and stellar densities from Oh_2011 .
