We multiply the stellar velocity contribution by 0.5 (mass-to-light ratio lower by a factor 4 ), so the stellar contribution lies between that in Sanders_2002 and Oh_2011 .

Fig.  fig:UGC5721 shows that the SIDM fit for UGC 5721 including all components has an overall ``cuspy'' profile (see black solid line), which is steeper than the circular velocity expected for the corresponding NFW halo without self-interactions (dashed line). For this fit we need a concentrated halo at +1 above the median mass-concentration relation, which together with the observed compact stellar disk (scale radius of 0.5  kpc) combine to create a steeply raising velocity curve. Without the presence of baryons, UGC 5721 is a challenge for SIDM due to the development of an extended low density core at the centre.

sources.summary: The paper presents UGC 5721 as a steeply rising rotation-curve outlier whose possible starburst history and asymmetric H I kinematics may bias rotation-curve interpretation.

quotes.quote: IC 2574 and UGC 5721 are frequently referenced in the literature as examples of the two extremes of the diversity problem: IC 2574 has a slowly-rising rotation curve with a roughly linear slope out to 12 kpc, while UGC 5721 has a very steep rise in circular velocity within the inner few kpc.

We compare the RC reconstructions to the RC measured for UGC 5721, one of the galaxies described in as more cuspy than an NFW profile for a halo of its virial mass. The RCs for m11i that include information about radial and azimuthal velocity in addition to the purely rotational velocity (shown in red and cyan) have a sharp spike that quickly falls off and flattens throughout the rest of the HI disk, which is similar to the RC for UGC 5721, While the star formation history of UGC 5721 is an area of ongoing research, there is evidence that suggests burstiness. One criterion commonly used in the literature to classify galaxies as starbursts is that the ratio of the Infrared Astronomical Satellite (IRAS) 60 and 100 m flux densities (in Jy) exceeds 0.5 IRAS1989MNRAS.238..523R ; for UGC 5721, f_ 60 /f_ 100 = 0.53 using the IRAS Point Source Catalog (PSC) measurements, and f_ 60 /f_ 100 = 0.51 using the IRAS Faint Source Catalog (FSC) measurements 1990IRASF.C......0M, 1994yCat.2125....0J . In addition, Lelli_2014 identify UGC 5721 as a potential starbursting dwarf based on its dynamical and structural properties. Thus, it is feasible that bursty star formation could influence the rotation curve measured for this galaxy.

In Figure fig:m11i_compare , we show the HI column density and LOS velocity for UGC 5721, and compare it to the HI maps for m11i at z = 0.02 at a comparable viewing angle (not exactly edge-on and slightly tilted, and with the hole not visible). In the middle panel, we show the HI maps for m11i at a low resolution and with a gaussian blur applied to make it more comparable to observed HI data. In the bottom panel, we show the same HI maps for m11i, but with the filters and pixelization removed. There are significant visual similarities between the processed image of m11i and UGC 5721: there is a gradient in the LOS velocity, but it is slightly asymmetric. The HI LOS velocity dispersion, as shown in , shows a much more significant asymmetry.

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 5721 as one extreme of inner rotation-curve diversity near 80 km/s, fit by a denser SIDM halo where the disk significantly shapes the profile.

quotes.quote: In Fig.  fig:examples , we show SIDM fits for UGC 5721 , NGC 1560 , and UGC 5750 . All have similar V_f 80  km/s , but the shapes of the rotation curves are very different in the inner regions. UGC 5721 and UGC 5750 are at opposite extremes for the rotation curve diversity in this mass range. Despite the diversity, the SIDM halo model provides an impressive fit to the rotation curves.

We find that NGC 1560 has a median halo, UGC 5721 has a denser halo, and UGC 5750 has an underdense halo, but all within 2 of the median expectation. The observed (2  ) is close to 20  km/s for UGC 5750, while the corresponding CDM halo has (2  ) 30  km/s even with a concentration 2 lower than the median value. The effect of the disk is most significant in UGC 5721, resulting in a SIDM halo similar to the CDM one and a flat V_ cir even at 2  kpc .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3274 as a faint, low-mass spiral with an updated TRGB distance and likely gas-rich dwarf satellite candidates AGC 731457 and AGC 749315.

quotes.quote: NGC 3274 NGC 3274 is a relatively faint SAB galaxy and the second least massive spiral galaxy in our host sample. We adopt the updated TRGB distance of D=10 Mpc sabbi_resolved_2018 instead of the ALFALFA D_ =6.5 Mpc value. AGC 731457 is an R_ 200 satellite candidate with a stellar mass M_ * 9.67 10^ 6 M_ from the ALFALFA-SDSS crossmatch durbala_alfalfa-sdss_2020-1 . The brightest star distance D_ BS =10.52 0.34 Mpc for this dwarf galaxy karachentsev_peculiar_2015 roughly agrees with the ALFALFA value D_ =11.1 Mpc. The combined projected and line-of-sight proximity gives a relatively high confidence that AGC 731457 is a satellite of NGC 3274 (Table table:2R200_satellites ).

sources.summary: The paper finds MOND fails for compact high-surface-brightness UGC 5721, even when inclination or distance is free, and suggests central twisting isophotes or a bar may affect its rotation curve.

quotes.quote: UGC 5721. -- For this compact, high-surface brightness galaxy, the MOND curve fails to describe the observed rotation curve. Even with the inclination or the distance as a free parameter, no good fit can be found. There is evidence of twisting isophotes in the central regions (see Swaters Balcells 2002), perhaps indicating the presence of a bar. If so, the associated noncircular motions could have affected the derived rotation curve.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes UGC 5721 as a barred spiral emission-line/HII galaxy with mismatched H and HI rotation curves, uncertain inner points, and possible starburst properties.

quotes.quote: UGC 5721 (NGC 3274) is a barred spiral, emission-line or H ii galaxy Hunter82 in the constellation of Leo. Its H curve shows a steep rise that is not seen in the H i data. Moreover, the inner points of its RC are uncertain because of insufficient angular resolution swaters09 . It has a circular velocity gradient comparable to those of BCDs Lelli14a , and so could possibly harbor a starburst.

sources.summary: The paper says 5721 has the sample's most extended rotation curve in disk-scale units, though its inner points are uncertain because of angular resolution.

quotes.quote: 5721 has the most extended rotation curve of this sample when the radius is expressed in optical disk scale lengths. The last measured point of the rotation curve is at 16.3h . The inner points of this rotation curve are uncertain, because of insufficient angular resolution.

sources.summary: UGC5721 is highlighted as a problematic low-mass-to-light-ratio galaxy whose MCMC parameters are narrowly bimodal, though the chosen best fit reproduces its rotation curve.

quotes.quote: Among the four galaxies with a low mass to light ratio, one of the most problematic case is UGC5721, showing a very narrow bi-modality in the parameters distribution that cannot be avoided and resolved even changing parameters of the trial distribution in the MCMC. The convergence test inevitably fails in this case, but we have assumed as best fit results the values associated to the highest peak in the likelihood distribution shown in Fig. ( fig:cham_gal4 ), which produce a very good fit to the observational rotation curve. A discrepancy can be found around 2 kpc but as the large error bar shows, in this region there are some observational difficulties related to gas observations which produce the detected faster rise in velocity.

sources.summary: The paper uses UGC 5721 as a high-baryonic-acceleration SPARC comparison galaxy whose observed inner rotation speed is difficult for NIHAO simulations but closer to EAGLE/APOSTLE diversity-plot behavior.

quotes.quote: Here, the gravitational acceleration generated by baryons at 2 kpc is much smaller in F568--3, UGC 5750 and IC 2574 than in NGC 024, UGC 5721 and UGC 7690, and so is the measured circular velocity, and hence the total gravitational acceleration at 2 kpc.

But the three high-baryonic acceleration galaxies of Fig. RC_selected_galaxies , NGC 024, UGC 5721 and UGC 7690 seem to all have too high observed V_ rot (2 kpc ) to be reproduced by the NIHAO simulations.

Interestingly, as originally shown by Oman2015 , galaxies such as NGC 024, UGC 5721 and UGC 7690 can in principle be more closely reproduced in the diversity plot by simulations from the EAGLE and APOSTLE projects, which in turn cannot reproduce the smaller V_ rot (2 kpc ) galaxies.

gmw_00001115.name: UGC 7603
gmw_00001115.overview: UGC 7603, also known as NGC 4455, is a highly inclined late-type spiral dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1804.00469, arXiv:2105.00587] classified as an emission-line Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1706.04202]. It likely forms a dwarf association with UGC 7584 and KKH 80 [arXiv:1804.00469] and exhibits a short observed rotation curve [arXiv:1908.06489].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4455 as a foreground Virgo dwarf Sdm, likely in a small dwarf association, and restricts TRGB measurement to outer-galaxy stars.

quotes.quote: NGC 4455 = EVCC0711. This is a dwarf Sdm galaxy foreground to the Virgo Cluster. Together with UGC 7584 and KKH 80 it probably forms an association of dwarfs having a projected radius of 30 kpc and velocity dispersion of 20 . We use in the distance measurement and show in the CMD only stars in the outer part of the galaxy. We selected the stars situated outside of the ellipse with the parameters: X=2133, Y=2439, A=1705, B=675, PA=73.8, where X,Y are the ellipse center pixel coordinates in the combined distortion-corrected mosaic F606W frame, A and B are major and minor axes in pix, and PA is position angle of the major axis in degrees.

sources.summary: The BEC dark matter fit gives UGC7603 the smallest static halo, largest central density, upper particle-mass limits, and shortest observed rotation curve in the modeled dwarf sample.

quotes.quote: The lower limit is given by galaxy UGC7125 having the largest static BEC halo ( R =14.381 kpc), and the upper limit from UGC7603 having the smallest one ( R =3.793 kpc). It is worth to note, that UGC7125 also has the smallest ( _ =0.105 10^ 24 g/cm ^3 ), while UGC7603 the largest central density ( _ =2.692 10^ 24 g/cm ^3 ) among these galaxies.

Again, the two limits are constrained by the galaxies UGC7125 (from below, R' =15.36 kpc) and UGC7603 (from above, R' =3.794 kpc). We also note that UGC7125 possesses the longest, while UGC7603 the shortest observed rotation curve in the sample, hence the size of the static BEC halo seems to correlate with the length of the rotation curves.

sources.summary: The paper says 7603's H I and H-alpha data generally agree, but its high inclination and steep unresolved inner curve leave residual beam-smearing uncertainty.

quotes.quote: 7603 . The \ and \ data are in good agreement, although the outer points on the \ curve fall below the \ curve. As this galaxy has a fairly high inclination ( 76^ ), and a steeply rising rotation curve that is not well resolved by the \ beam, it is likely that the difference is due to residual affects of beam smearing.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 7603 as an emission-line Seyfert galaxy whose derived HI rotation curve is uncertain because beam resolution and high inclination affect the inner velocity gradient.

quotes.quote: UGC 7603 (NGC 4455) is another emission-line (Seyfert) galaxy Deo07 . The derived H i RC may be uncertain since its velocity gradient is not well resolved by the H i beam. This uncertainty is enhanced in the inner region of the RC due to its high inclination swaters09 .

sources.summary: NGC 4455 is included as a nearby HSB late-type spiral comparison object, with dubious SBd morphology due to high inclination and an estimated mass of log(M/Msun)=11.

quotes.quote: NGC 4455: This HSB spiral is included in this study so that our sample would span a range of surface brightnesses and chosen specifically because of its proximity (only 10 Mpc) and availability during our observing run. It is a late type spiral with an SBd morphology, although the classification is dubious since it is inclined at 78^ . The estimated mass is log( M_ / M_ ) = 11 Kim2007 .

gmw_00001116.name: UGC 5764
gmw_00001116.overview: UGC 5764 is a gas-rich, isolated dwarf galaxy classified morphologically as either an irregular or a blue compact dwarf [arXiv:1706.04202]. It features high neutral hydrogen column density throughout [arXiv:astro-ph/0203133], a regular velocity field [arXiv:astro-ph/0203133], and a warp on its receding side [arXiv:1706.04202].

sources.summary: The paper treats DDO 83 as a faint continuum detection: its resolved source lies inside the HI boundary, lacks an optical counterpart, and is consistent with marginal Effelsberg emission.

quotes.quote: Finally, a faint and somewhat marginal source appears in the map of DDO 83. This source was accepted as a detection because it is resolved and occurs within the HI emission boundary. Optically, nothing is evident at its position. An even more marginal detection S_ 4.8 =3.0 2.2 (Klein et al. 1992) is at least consistent with our result.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 5764 as a gas-rich isolated irregular or blue-compact-dwarf-like galaxy with low luminosity, high central surface brightness, and an uncertain warped HI edge.

quotes.quote: UGC 5764 (DDO 83) is a gas-rich isolated galaxy, classified morphologically as irregular or even BCD. It has a low luminosity but high central surface brightness giving a <1.5 kpc scale length. This galaxy seems to have just started to flatten out at the edge of its detected H i , but it presents a warp on its receding side that makes its last 2 points uncertain VanZee97 .

sources.summary: DDO 83 is described as having high HI column density throughout, a regular spider-like velocity field, and a rotation curve that rises centrally and flattens at the HI edge.

quotes.quote: DDO 83: The HI column density is high throughout the galaxy. The velocity field is regular and spider-like. The rotation curve rises strongly near the center and flattens at the edge of the HI disk.

gmw_00001117.name: DDO 64
gmw_00001117.overview: DDO 64 is an actively star-forming dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:1706.04202, arXiv:2311.03876] that is likely nearly edge-on [arXiv:astro-ph/0203133]. It is linked to its neighbor UGC 5272b by a neutral hydrogen bridge [arXiv:2310.02595] and exhibits non-circular internal motions [arXiv:astro-ph/0604576, arXiv:1706.04202].

sources.summary: DDO 64 shows strong emission across nearly all fibers, structured non-monotonic rotation, possible inner non-circular motions, and halo fits that prefer an isothermal model over NFW.

quotes.quote: DDO 64: There were three pointings of the center and approaching side of this galaxy. The \ fiber positions are shown on a Digitized Sky Survey The Digitized Sky Surveys were produced at the Space Telescope Science Institute under U.S. Government grant NAG W-2166.

Fiber velocities were the average of the \ and \ lines. The amount of emission in this galaxy is very high and nearly all fibers detected emission. The position angle was fixed to the dBB value. The \ points rise and fall in a pattern similar to the long-slit data dBB . The structure in the rotation curve appears to be real.

sources.summary: The paper describes DDO 64 as an actively star-forming dwarf irregular dominated by HII regions, blue young inner populations, emission lines, and possible non-circular motions.

quotes.quote: DDO 64 (UGC 5272) is a dwarf irregular galaxy that is actively forming stars. It is dominated by HII regions around which stars are clumped, giving place to a patchy distribution. The inner regions of the stellar component are dominated by a young population which translates in this galaxy presenting extremely blue colors as compared to other irregular galaxies. Emission lines are measured from spectroscopy of its HII regions Hopp91 . Non-circular motions may be causing a difference between the approaching and receding sides of the velocity field deblok02 .

sources.summary: The paper treats DDO64 as a representative rotation-dominated dwarf irregular, finding fractional-gravity posteriors and a halo-dominated rotation curve.

quotes.quote: Specifically, in the top panel of Figure fig|NGC3741 we illustrate the MCMC posterior distributions for two representative dwIrr in the sample, namely NGC3741 (the one with the best and most extended data) and DDO64. Red lines/contours refer to the outcomes for FG, and green ones for Newtonian gravity; the white crosses mark the best-fit value of the parameters in FG. In the bottom panel the best fit (solid lines) and the 2 credible intervals (shaded areas) sampled from the posteriors are shown.

In particular, for D631-7, DDO64, DDO161, UGC5005, UGC5414, and NGC3741 there is a clear preference for FG both in terms of _r^2 and of the BIC. In such cases, the fractional index takes on typical values s 1.2-1.3 , the stellar mass-to-light ratios M_ /L are slightly larger than for the Newtonian case and more in line with the value around 0.5 expected from stellar population synthesis models, and the DM masses M_ 200 are appreciably larger of factors a few than in the Newtonian fit.

sources.summary: DDO 64 is described as probably nearly edge-on, dominated by a high-column-density HI ridge, with poorly resolved minor-axis velocity structure and nearly solid-body rotation.

quotes.quote: DDO 64: A high-column-density ridge dominates the HI structure of this probably nearly edge-on galaxy. The velocity field is poorly resolved along the minor axis. The XV map shows nearly solid-body rotation with only a hint of flattening. The feature south of DDO 64 is UGC 5272B, the feature north of it is probably noise.

sources.summary: The paper presents UGC 5272 as the central member of an interacting pair with UGC 5272b, linked by an H I bridge and containing identified FUV subclumps.

quotes.quote: The bottom-left panel displays an interacting system, UGC 5272 and UGC 5272b, which has a HI bridge connecting the two galaxies 2002A&A...390..829S . The UGC 5272 is in the centre and UGC 5272b is 2 arcmin towards the south. The bottom-right panel shows the subclumps (in dark blue) identified inside the UGC 5272--UGC 5272b system.

gmw_00001118.name: IC5338
gmw_00001118.overview: IC5338 is a central giant elliptical or cD galaxy that hosts two distinct optical nuclei [arXiv:2009.11210, arXiv:0803.1680]. The southwest nucleus contains an active galactic nucleus detected in radio and hard X-rays [arXiv:0803.1680, arXiv:1705.01787], which exhibits signs of fading [arXiv:1901.03550] and is likely fueled by a merger event [arXiv:2009.11210].

sources.summary: The paper describes IC5338 as the central giant elliptical hosting the Kite radio source, with aligned radio structures, two compact cores, merger evidence, and renewed AGN activity.

quotes.quote: Indeed IC5338 does not show properties of AGN emission from the optical (SDSS) or infrared (WISE) photometry. Furthermore, a recent catalogue identifying quasars from photometric data in SDSS and WISE indicate that galaxies in A2626 have a very low probability of being quasars Clarke_2019 . However, an AGN coincident with IC5338 is clearly detected at 144 MHz (Figure scheme ) .

We note at the centre of the galaxy there are two compact cores shown in the SDSS image. The core to the SW is coincident with the 1.4 GHz radio emission (shown by cyan contours in the right image of Figure comb ) and it exhibits hard X-ray emission Wong_2008 , thus it is likely the host of the AGN. The presence of these two cores in close proximity suggests a merger event had occurred to form IC5338, and there may be significant ongoing tidal forces between the two cores. Evidence for a merger event helps to provide a mechanisms for the supply and accretion of gas onto the AGN in the SW core e.g., for a recent result Ellison_2019 . The spectral index of the AGN ( = 0.8 0.1 , Figure canvas_spectral ) indicates that the supermassive black hole is currently accreting material.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IC 5338 as the central cD galaxy in Abell 2626, with two optical nuclei and a hard X-ray/radio-core AGN on the southwest nucleus.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: NED object IC 5338: NED RA 23:36:30.6, NED Dec +21:08:50

quotes.quote: We extracted the HST image of the center of IC 5338 from the archive. The 300 s exposure was taken on 1999 June 26 using the HST WFPC2 camera with the F555W filter. Figure fig:2nuclei shows clearly that this cD galaxy has two distinct nuclei, with the northeast nucleus being brighter optically. The two optical nuclei are located at the peaks of the soft X-ray image, with the radio core source in IC 5338 centered on the hard X-ray, southwest nucleus. The hard X-ray point source is only separated from the peak of the radio core by less than 1 .

Thus, we believe that this source is the AGN, and that the point-like hard X-ray source arises from this AGN. To study the X-ray properties of the IC 5338 AGN, Chandra data were used to do photometry.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IC5338 as A2626’s cD galaxy with double optical nuclei, radio/X-ray activity, extended emission, and possible jet-precession links to the cluster’s radio arcs.

quotes.quote: Its core-dominant (cD) galaxy IC5338 hosts a pair of optical nuclei with a projected separation of 3.3 kpc, of which only the southern one has a counterpart also in the radio hereafter G13 Gitti_2013b and hard X-rays Wong_2008 bands see Fig. 3 Wong_2008 . The extended radio emission of the cluster has a peculiar morphology resembling a giant kite, with striking arc-like, symmetric features whose origin is puzzling Gitti_2004, Gitti_2013b, Kale_2017 . The arcs are collimated structures having largest extent of 70 kpc, steep radio spectrum < 2.5 In this work the radio spectral index is defined such as S ^ , Kale_2017 , and no evidence of polarized emission (G13).

In this high-sensitivity, low-resolution image we detected extended emission around IC5338. The emission region extends up to 27 '' ( 30 kpc) and is located inside the radio arcs known at 1.4 GHz (G13). We measure a total flux densities of S_ 5.5,NAT = 8.2 0.2, of which 6.2 0.2 mJy are contributed by the core, thus leaving an estimate of 2.0 0.3 mJy related to the extended emission.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IC 5338 as the central cD galaxy in Abell 2626 and links its SW nuclear source to X-ray variability and fading AGN behavior.

quotes.quote: The brightest region near the centre is associated with the cD galaxy IC 5338, while the nearby galaxy IC 5337 is seen on its west at a separation of 85 kpc. Small fluctuations due to the presence of cavities, excess emission, edge, etc. are also apparent in the surface brightness distribution of the X-ray emission in Figure fig_3 . For better visualization this image is smoothed with a 2 Gaussian kernel. Figure fig2 represents 5 5 SDSS g band image of the central region of this cluster, where the cD galaxy IC 5338 and the near by member IC 5337 on its west are clearly visible. The inset shows central 20 20 region of A2626 imaged using HST delineating a pair of bright sources in the core of this cluster. The NE (north-east) source WHL J233630.6+210850 z = 0.0546, RA: 23h36m30.6s, : +21d08m50s; 2013MNRAS.436..275W and the SW (south-west) source IC 5338 z = 0.0546, RA: 23h36m30.4s, :+21d08m46s; 2009A&A...495..707C are seen at a separation of 3.4 kpc.

The observed decrease in the count rates of the SW source could, therefore, be attributed to the fading of the AGN probably due to the variation in the mass accretion rate onto the central black hole e.g., 2005Natur.433..604D,2006ApJS..163....1H . Although simulation study expect the typical timescale of 10 ^4 years for such a change, there are several sources that showed fading or change in states (from type 1 to type 2) within few decades 1999ApJ...519L.123A,2005ApJ...623L..93R,2014ApJ...796..134D,2015ApJ...800..144L,2015AJ....149..155K,2017ApJ...850..168K . Another evidence in support of the fading of the AGN was provided by their associated outflows 2018MNRAS.473.3525Z .

sources.summary: The paper identifies IC 5338 as the double-nucleus cD galaxy in A 2626, associates its southwest nucleus with the compact radio component, and discusses possible jet activity from its nuclei.

quotes.quote: A 2626 is a low-redshift black z=0.0553, Struble-Rood_1999 , regular, relatively poor Abell cluster Mohr_1996 , with a double-nuclei cD elliptical galaxy (IC 5338) showing extended strong emission lines Johnstone_1987 . Early observations with Einstein and ROSAT , then confirmed by Chandra black and XMM--Newton , indicate that it hosts a moderate cooling flow White_1991, Rizza_2000, Wong_2008 . Early, low resolution radio surveys showed that this cluster contains a central radio source exhibiting a compact unresolved core and a diffuse structure with very steep radio spectrum Slee-Siegman_1983, Roland_1985, Burns_1990 . The compact radio component is associated with the southwest nucleus of the cD galaxy IC 5338 Owen_1995 .

This interpretation would imply the existence of radio jets emanating also from the northeast nucleus (separated by only 4 kpc from the active southwest nucleus) of the cD galaxy IC 5338, which however does not show a radio core.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC5338 as the central dominant galaxy in Abell 2626, with two optical nuclei, a detected southern core, and no hard X-ray detection from its northern core.

quotes.quote: The most luminous galaxy is the central dominant (cD) galaxy IC5338, which hosts a pair of optical nuclei with a projected separation of 3.5 arcsec, of which only the southern one has a counterpart also in the radio Owen_1995 and hard X-ray bands see Fig. 3 Wong_2008 . A2626 also hosts the S0 galaxy IC5337, that has been previously classified as a jelly-fish galaxy by Poggianti_2016 for its optical properties.

We detected three point sources associated with A2626, the southern core of IC5338 (23h 36m 30.5s, +21d 08m 47.7s), the core of IC5337 (23h 36m 25.0s, +21d 09m 2.9s) and one in the south tail of IC5337 (23h 36m 24.6s, +21d 08m 48.4s) that does not have a radio counterpart. We did not detect any hard (2.0-10.0 keV) X-ray emission associated to northern core of IC5338, in agreement with the results of Wong_2008 . By considering the contribution of the thermal emission of the ICM and the instrumental background, we could estimate an upper limit for its X-ray luminosity of 1.3 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 (0.5-7.0 keV band). Therefore, we cannot find evidence that the second pair of radio arcs are due to a second AGN, although it could be that the AGN has just turned off.

gmw_00001119.name: IRAS 13120-5453
gmw_00001119.overview: IRAS 13120-5453 is a nearby ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1905.06950, arXiv:2104.06790] classified as a Seyfert 2 [arXiv:2104.08224, arXiv:1510.04453] and late-stage merger [arXiv:1905.06950] with a single nucleus [arXiv:1905.06950, arXiv:1612.08181] and long tidal tails [arXiv:1510.04453]. The galaxy hosts a Compton-thick active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2104.08224, arXiv:1612.08181] and a young central starburst [arXiv:2104.08224] driving a molecular outflow [arXiv:1612.08181].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 13120-5453 as a nearby ULIRG/late-stage merger with high CO isotopologue line ratios, very low 13CO/C18O, and molecular gas concentrated in a single nucleus.

quotes.quote: We present high-resolution ALMA observations of the C ^ 18 O, ^ 13 CO and ^ 12 CO J =1-0 isotopologues in 3 nearby ultra-luminous infrared galaxies (ULIRGS; Arp 220, IRAS 13120-5453, IRAS 17208-0014) and 1 nearby post-merger galaxy (NGC 2623). In all 4 systems, we measure high ^ 12 CO/C ^ 18 O and ^ 12 CO/ ^ 13 CO integrated line ratios while the ^ 13 CO/C ^ 18 O ratio is observed to be extremely low in comparison to typical star-forming disks, supporting previous work. We investigate whether these unusual line ratios are due to dynamical effects, astrochemistry within the gas, or nucleosynthesis in stars. Assuming both lines are optically thin, low ^ 13 CO/C ^ 18 O values suggest that C ^ 18 O is more abundant than ^ 13 CO in the interstellar medium of these systems.

This paper combines new and archival ALMA 12m-array observations of C ^ 18 O, ^ 13 CO and ^ 12 CO J =1-0 lines in 4 nearby ( < 200 Mpc) advanced mergers: Arp 220, IRAS 13120-5453, IRAS 17208-0014, and NGC 2623. With infrared luminosities, L _ IR , exceeding 10 ^ 12 L _ , Arp 220, IRAS 13120-5453, and IRAS 17208-0014 are all ULIRGs, while NGC 2623 is a post-merger galaxy with L _ IR = 10 ^ 11.6 L _ Armus2009 .

The double nuclei of the merger system, Arp 220, are visible in all 3 emission lines while, despite having undergone a major merger at some point in their past, IRAS 13120-5453, IRAS 17208-0014, and NGC 2623 all distribute their molecular gas over a single nucleus. Combined with their different infrared luminosities, these morphological differences suggest our sample can be considered snapshots of different stages of the merger sequence, evolving from the bright ongoing merger (Arp 220), through the late stages of merging (IRAS 13120-5453, IRAS 17208-0014) and, finally, resulting in the fainter post-merger system (NGC 2623).

sources.summary: The paper presents IRAS 13120-5453 as a ULIRG example whose H-band SINFONI data show a rotating-disc velocity map and central velocity-dispersion peak.

quotes.quote: Here we present, as an example, the kinematics results of the ULIRG IRAS 13120-5453 (z=0.031). These results were not taken into account along this work as this object cannot be compared with the LIRG sample in terms of integration time, luminosity, or distance (i.e. spatial resolution). The observations (1800 s on target) were carried out in the H-band and seeing-limited mode (FWHM 0.85 , 0.5 kpc at its redshift), with a plate scale of 0.125 0.250 spaxel ^ 1 . The dithering pattern adopted yielded a FoV 11 11 . The reduction and calibration, and kinematic analysis were performed as in Sect. subsec:ifs and subsec:kin_analysis , respectively.

This ULIRG presents a velocity map compatible with a rotating-disc galaxy with an amplitude of 100 km s ^ 1 at <1 kpc, whereas the velocity dispersion map shows a central peak of 140 km s ^ 1 (Fig. fig:kin_IRAS13120 ). The loss of SN at >1 kpc generates decrements in the measured velocities, as can be noticed in the velocity profile. These results support the advantage of obtaining the stellar kinematics using H-band observations for those ULIRGs in which the K-band does not cover the CO (2-0) and CO (3-1) bands.

sources.summary: The paper finds IRAS 13120-5453 has higher HCN ratios and little resolved HCN-ratio variation, with Seyfert-2 classification, Compton-thick AGN evidence, and a young central starburst.

quotes.quote: We find an offset to higher HCN (3-2)/HCN (1-0) intensity ratios ( 0.8 ) in IRAS 13120-5453 compared to NGC 3256 ( 0.3-0.4 ) and NGC 7469 ( 0.3-0.5 ). The HCN (4-3)/HCN (3-2) intensity ratio in NGC 7469 has a slope of 1.34 0.05 . We attribute the variation within NGC 3256 to excitation associated with the northern and southern nuclei. In NGC 7469, the variations are localized to the region surrounding the active galactic nucleus. At our resolution ( 700 pc), IRAS 13120-5453 shows little variation in the HCN intensity ratios.

IRAS 13120 has been optically classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy Veron2010 . Teng2015 find that observations of IRAS 13120 are consistent with the galaxy hosting an inactive, Compton-thick AGN ( N_ H > 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 ). ^ 12 CO observations by Sliwa2017 show evidence for a young starburst ( <7 Myr) in the central 500 pc region. Privon2017 find the HCN/HCO ^ + ratio is higher in the central starburst and suggest this is from mechanical heating of the gas by supernovae feedback.

sources.summary: The paper studies IRAS 13120-5453 through ALMA and Herschel observations, giving its phase center, unresolved nuclear far-IR emission, and water-line ratios requiring excitation dominated by warm dust.

quotes.quote: An iteration of phase self-calibration was possible since the continuum of IRAS13120-5453 is strong enough to allow us to derive gain solutions on a time interval of 20s. The phase center is 13h15m06.316s --55d09m22.79s (J2000).

The SPIRE observation was performed on 05 January 2011 as part of the OT key program Hercules (PI: P.P. van der Werf; ObsID: 1342212342) with a single pointing centered on IRAS 13120-5453. The observation was conducted in high spectral resolution, sparse image sampling mode with a resolution of 1.2  GHz in both observing bands ( 447-989  GHz and 958-1545  GHz). A total of 29 repetitions (58 FTS scans) were performed, resulting in a total on-source integration time of 3863  s.

We found that the line ratios in IRAS 13120-5453 cannot be reproduced with collisional excitation alone. In fact, the excitation is found to be dominated by absorption of photons emitted by warm dust, with collisions mainly affecting the lowest-lying H _ 2 O levels.

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 13120 5453 as a post-merger single-nucleus ULIRG dominated by starburst light but hosting a Compton-thick AGN and a moderate-column molecular outflow.

quotes.quote: IRAS 13120 5453 : This ULIRG is classified as a post-merger with a single nucleus sti13 . Its luminosity is dominated by the starburst, though it has a Compton-thick AGN iwa11,ten15 . The source is not detected in OH65 (GA15), indicating that the material responsible for the AGN obscuration is too compact to have any significant contribution to the far-IR. Our fit to the OH doublets consists of a central velocity component, best identified in OH84 (Fig. fitsoh84 ), and an outflow component with moderate column density ( 1.5 10^ 22 ) and probably extended.

It gives an estimated momentum flux of 4 10^ 35 dyn, which is significantly lower than the predicted momentum rate supplied by the starburst and the AGN. This suggests a momentum-conserving flow. A weak but apparent blueshifted wing in OH119 up to velocities of 1500 \ (Fig. fitsoh119 ) is not modeled, and could reveal the feedback by the buried AGN.

sources.summary: IRAS 13120--5453 is presented as a Seyfert 2 ULIRG with tidal-tail morphology, extended soft X-ray emission, and a Chandra power-law plus MEKAL spectrum.

quotes.quote: IRAS 13120--5453 has a morphology that is classified as ``single or obscured nucleus with long prominent tails'' by haan11 . Its optical spectral classification is Seyfert 2 vv2010 . This source was detected by Chandra iwasawa11 and has extended soft X-ray emission. The observed 2--7 keV band flux is 1.4 10^ 13 \ and the 2--10 keV X-ray luminosity assuming no extinction other than Galactic is 4.5 10^ 41 . The 3--7 keV spectrum is fit by a power law of = 2.6^ +1.5 _ 0.9 , and the 0.4--2 keV data are fit with a MEKAL component of kT = 0.82^ +0.26 _ 0.14 keV iwasawa11 .

sources.summary: The paper includes IRAS 13120-5453 among ULIRGs with strong AGN contributions, derives its AGN fraction from mid-infrared diagnostics, and groups its mass outflow with starburst-dominated systems.

quotes.quote: For this first study of outflow signatures in our data we use a sub-sample that is mainly constrained by the observing schedule of Herschel, but that covers a broad range of AGN and starburst activity, including a starburst template (NGC 253), a cold, starburst dominated ULIRG (IRAS 17208-0014,) warm ULIRGs (S25/S60 > 0.1) and/or ULIRGs with strong AGN contributions (Mrk 231, IRAS 13120-5453, IRAS 14378-3651), and a heavily obscured ULIRG (IRAS 08572+3915), which hosts a powerful AGN (e.g. Veilleux et al. 2009, V09).

For IRAS 13120-5453 and IRAS 14378-3651 (which are not part of V09) we calculated AGN fractions using method 1 from V09, based on the observed [ O IV ] / [ Ne II ] ratio (and the observed upper limit for IRAS 14378-3651) from Farrah et al. (2007). AGN luminosities are then calculated as L _ AGN = L _ bol .
The starburst-dominated objects of our sample (NGC 253, IRAS 13120-5453, and IRAS 17208-0014) have values of M similar to their star formation rates.

sources.summary: The paper describes this nearby ULIRG as AGN-dominated from X-ray and mid-infrared diagnostics, with optical IFS showing strong Seyfert-like ratios and high-velocity nuclear components.

quotes.quote: IRAS 13120-5453 This nearby ULIRG is dominated by AGN emission at all wavelengths. X-ray data indicate an AGN signature Iwasawa09,Iwasawa11 and mid-IR PAH values and [ Ne V ] are consistent with a galaxy dominated by an AGN Farrah07,Pereira10b,Petric11 . Our optical IFS data are quite consistent with this picture: the strongest emission we detect is dominated by Seyfert-like ratios in the diagnostic diagrams, and the velocity dispersion distribution shows a contribution a high- component in the nuclear regions of 300-400 in addition to the lower 100 component.

sources.summary: IRAS 13120-5453 drives one correlation test; the paper notes its broad [C II] component is ambiguous and affects correlation significance.

quotes.quote: The difference can be very large in some cases: F05189-2524 and Mrk 273 are best fitted either with a second narrow Gaussian or with a broad component, while 07251-0248, 13120-5453, F14378-3651 and F22491-1808 can be fitted either with or without a broad component.
If IRAS 13120-5453 is removed from the sample, the correlation becomes tentative according to the Spearman rank coefficient and significant according to the Pearson correlation coefficient.
For example, 13120-5453 could either have an extremely broad component, or no broad component at all.

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS 13120-5453 as an emission-dominated starburst galaxy whose H2O diagnostic location resembles Galactic high-mass star-forming regions and lacks a prominent obscured nucleus.

quotes.quote: Our extragalactic sample includes a variety of well-known local (U)LIRGs: a QSO (Mrk 231), AGNs such as IRAS 05189-2524, NGC 1068, NGC 7469, NGC 4945, IRAS 08572+3915, and NGC 6240 the latter with strong CO emission associated with shocks, see mei13 , (U)LIRGs with a compact obscured nucleus (NGC 4418, Arp 299a, Zw 049.057, ESO 320-G030, Mrk 273, Arp 220, IRAS 17208-0014), and starburst galaxies (NGC 253 and IRAS 13120-5453). As delineated by the equivalent width of the OH 65 m doublet fis14,gon15 , 6 galaxies (NGC 1068, NGC 7469, NGC 253, NGC 6240, IRAS 13120-5453, and NGC 4945) have overall weak far-IR molecular absorption features but usually strong emission in the atomic/ionic fine-structure lines ( EQW(OH 65 m) <20 km s ^ 1 ), and will be referred to as ``emission-dominated galaxies'' (EDGs), while the rest (with EQW(OH 65 m) >20 km s ^ 1 ) have stronger far-IR molecular absorption features and will be referred to as ``absorption-dominated galaxies'' (ADGs).

The location of the galactic HMSFRs in Fig. rates a resemble the starburst galaxies NGC 253 and IRAS 13120-5453, lacking an obscured nucleus that in its prominent form is a unique feature of extragalactic sources.

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS 13120-5453 as a U/LIRG with the sample’s highest mean CN/HCN ratio and the only statistically significant central CN/HCN decrease.

quotes.quote: Our final sample of 9 galaxies (Table table:physical ) is quite heterogeneous, and includes galaxies with a prominent active galactic nucleus (AGN) or Seyfert nucleus (Circinus, NGC1808, NGC3627, NGC 7469), luminous and ultraluminous infrared galaxies (U/LIRGs: IRAS 13120-5453, NGC3256, VV114), galaxies with a starburst nucleus (M83, NGC 1808), and spiral galaxies (NGC3627, NGC3351).

Comparing the individual mean ratios for each galaxy shows a full range of only a factor of 2.5 (0.40 in log space), with M83 having the lowest average CN/HCN line ratio and IRAS 13120-5453 the highest ratio (Table table:ratios ).
The only galaxy to show a statistically significant decrease in its central CN/HCN ratio (of 20 ) is IRAS 13120-5453.
